question: What if I don't agree with the decision of the CPAS in Brussels?
answer: You can: ask the CPAS to review its decision; appeal the decision to the labour court. The appeal to the labour court must be lodged by application, within 3 months. It is free of charge. You will find information on this possibility of appeal and the procedure to be followed in the CPAS decision. For more information on appeals against a decision of a CPAS, see the section "Appeal the CPAS decision".

answer: Before going any further, you are only entitled to these new amounts if they are more advantageous than your old amount (the one you received in December 2019). question: I don ⁇ t pay for my parking, will I receive a fine?
answer: You will be charged if you do not comply with the signs prohibiting parking (panels 160 E, E3, E5 and E7) or the discontinued yellow lines painted on the sidewalks. For these offences under the Road Traffic Code, an immediate penalty of € 58 is proposed. For these offences under the Road Traffic Code, an immediate penalty of € 85 is proposed. question: I make sure that the landlord has obtained a rental permit (Walloon Region).
answer: Yes. If the property no longer complies, the rental permit can be withdrawn. Control visits can be carried out to verify that the rental permit system is well applied. They can be carried out on the initiative of: the municipality; the Region (Department of Housing); any person by filing a complaint with the latter. Neither the landlord nor the tenant can oppose it.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Safety and health

question: Under what conditions can I apply for Belgian citizenship if I was born in Belgium?
answer: You can apply for Belgian nationality if: you were born in Belgium; you are over 18 years old; you have lived legally in Belgium continuously since your birth. You must make a declaration to the civil registry officer (OEC) of the municipality of your main place of residence. To do this, you must first pay a fee of €150 to the competent legal security office or via MyMinfin. Your file must contain: your birth certificate; a certificate of residence and the history of all cards; your previous addresses since your birth.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (aged 18 and over), persons born in Belgium

question: I am applying for family reunification. I am a student. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: Yes. If you are a student, you can bring some members of your family. To find out who are the members of your family who are entitled to family reunification, you have to ask yourself the following questions: What is the nationality of the person you meet in Belgium: Belgian, European or outside the European Union? What is his residence permit? Why? Because the people you can bring and the conditions depend heavily on your residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I ⁇ m being summoned to court. What is a citation?
answer: The citation is an official document sent to the defendant by a bailiff. It is an official summons to appear in court before such a court, on such a date, at such an hour and for such a reason. Usually, the citation is written by the lawyer who then transmits his project to a bailiff. The bailiff is responsible for forming this draft citation, and sending it to the defendant. Specifically, the bailiff goes to the defendant's home, and hands an original citation to the person he meets there (the defendant himself or someone close to him at his home).
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Summoning to hearing

question: I am applying for family reunification. What are the conditions for family reunification with a Belgian?
answer: In this case, the Belgian adult must meet the following conditions: Have stable, regular and sufficient means of subsistence to provide for his own needs and those of his family card, and to avoid them becoming a burden on the public authorities; Have sufficient means of subsistence to allow him to receive the members of his family (property, lease, title, birth certificate, etc.); Have a sickness certificate that covers the risks for himself and for the members of his family.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: How can I arrange my future protection measure (out-of-court protection) myself?
answer: If you are of legal age and able, you can at any time draw up a mandate to organize your future protection measure, for the day when you will no longer be able to do so. This is the situation of extrajudicial protection. You can draw up this mandate yourself, or have a notary help you. The mandate must contain: the name of the person who will be responsible for managing your property and your person (the notary). This can be a member of your family, your entourage or a close friend.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Extrajudicial Mandate (extrajudicial protection)

question: Am I entitled to unemployment after working?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, except if they were employed before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM), or your payment body (Syndicate or CAPAC) The auxiliary payment card for unemployment benefits. Yes, but you must meet all these conditions. You must have worked a certain number of days for a certain period of time (reference period).
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment after a job

question: Who exercises parental authority if one parent is the only one making decisions?
answer: A parent may be alone in being able to make decisions for his minor child, for different reasons: either because the child has not been recognized by the other parent; or because the other parent has died; or because the other parent is unable to decide, for reasons: psychological: for example because a mental illness makes him unable to make decisions; physical: for example because he has gone abroad without giving any sign of life for a long time, or because he is in a coma; legal: for example because a parent has recognized him unable to make decisions and has placed him under the administration of the person.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: I live somewhere other than my home. I live in a cottage, do I have to live there?
answer: No, as a student, you are not obliged to live at your home address. The municipality knows that you do not live temporarily at your home address. The municipality cannot remove you. You no longer have to declare your main residence - and therefore your home - with your parents. As long as you are a student, the municipality assumes that you retain your main residence with your parents. If you do not return to your home address for more than 3 months, the municipality will record that you are "temporarily absent".
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: Do I need to install fire detectors in Brussels?
answer: No. On the other hand, your landlord has an obligation to install fire detectors in the building he rents. He has an obligation to install fire detectors: in your home; in the common corridors if it is a building with several apartments; in each room of the evacuation path of the rented housing. These are the rooms through which you have to go from the bedroom to the door that leads to the outside of the housing. If you do not comply with these obligations, you are considered partly responsible.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, expenses and insurance, Charges, Distribution of expenses

question: I am in Belgium illegally (without papers). I am in Belgium illegally. Am I at risk of being arrested and deported?
answer: Yes. If you are checked, you are at risk of being arrested and possibly deported. The SEFOR service within the Immigration Office, the municipality and the police create a file together and include your personal data: your fingerprints, your identity photos, a copy of your passport, etc. If you receive an order to leave the country, you must return to the country spontaneously. The Immigration Office indicates the deadline within which you must leave Belgium. In principle, it is a 30-day deadline.
topics: Foreigners, illegal stay

question: If I don't pay my consumer credit, can my income be confiscated?
answer: Yes, it's not a seizure, it's an assignment of wages. Be careful, you can't be forced to give up all your income. For more information, see "Can all my income be seized?" For more information, see "Can all my wages be seized?" For more information, see "Can all my wages be seized?" For more information, see "Can all my wages be seized?"

question: What to do when a new roommate arrives in Brussels?
answer: This lease must be signed by the remaining tenants, the new tenant and the landlord. This lease must at least specify that such a tenant will replace such a tenant. Depending on the content of your original lease, it may be necessary to add certain formalities (which is no longer valid in view of the change of tenants).

question: I am applying for family reunification. I am joining a Belgian. I am in my country of origin. How does the family reunification procedure with a Belgian go?
answer: You must submit your application to the competent Belgian embassy or consulate for your place of residence. If your file is complete, it is sent to the Foreigners' Office (OE). The OOE has 6 months to make a decision. In case of a positive decision, the embassy or consulate gives you a D visa (long stay) and you can go to Belgium. Once in Belgium, you must go to the municipal administration to register in the foreigners' quarter after a residence check done by a registration officer, you receive an F card.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I'm renting an apartment or a house. Do I have to put the price in the ad in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, in principle, any landlord who rents a property used for housing is obliged in all public or official communications (newspapers, website, posters, etc.) to pay: the amount of the rent excluded from charges; the nature of the private and common charges (forfeit or provisions); the amount of any common charges; the amount of the private charges; certain indicators of the performance certificate such as: the code label (unique identification number of the PEB certificate) indicates the total consumption of primary energy; the specific consumption of primary energy in kWh per year; the specific consumption of primary energy in kWh per year; see also this provision.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia)

question: The parents are not married together, so which court should I go to if there's a problem with parentage?
answer: The family court deals with all matters of parentage. You should in principle go to the family court of the child's home. To find the address of the court, go to this link. To contact the family court, there are two possibilities: You should in principle make a citation. You should go to a bailiff, who officially transmits your request and summons the other parties (probably the other parent and the child) to the court.

question: My landlord indexed the rent but he couldn't. Can I ask him to refund me what I paid too much in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. If your landlord made you pay an indexed rent when it could not be indexed, you can ask the landlord to refund you. You must send a registered letter. You can go back a maximum of 5 years from your request. Before that, the rent indexation amounts requested by your landlord are prescribed. You can no longer ask him to refund you. If your landlord does not reindex or does not agree to refund you, you have 1 year to ask the justice of the peace to order him to refund you.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: The lease has expired following the death of the tenant.
answer: The landlord can recover the rental guarantee if the tenant owes him money under the lease. For example: unpaid rents, unpaid actual charges, rental damage in the dwelling. He can only recover the part of the rental guarantee that corresponds to the amounts owed to him. The rest of the guarantee must be returned to the heirs of the tenant. To recover the rental guarantee, the landlord can: agree with the heirs to find a written agreement; file a unilateral request before the peace judge of the place of the rented dwelling.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Death

question: Are my financial difficulties temporary or recurrent?
answer: 1. You have some occasional delays in paying electricity, fuel or telephone bills. 2. You accumulate several debts that repeat month after month, your situation becomes more worrying. Your financial difficulties are recurring. Sometimes some creditors threaten you. But it is dangerous to pay a creditor, forgetting to pay the others.
topics: Money, debts, solutions to fight against debt

question: Who can request the continuation of the observation and how?
answer: Only the chief physician of the ward where the patient is can ask the justice of the peace for the maintenance of hospital care. This request must be made at least 15 days before the end of the period of 40 days of observation. The date taken into account is the date of registration of the patient in the register of the hospital where the patient is. The chief physician sends the justice of the peace a circumstantiated medical report attesting to the need for the maintenance of hospitalization.
topics: Privacy, personal protection measures, protection of the mentally ill, treatment in a hospital environment

question: I'm renting an apartment or a house (main residence lease). Can I ask for proof of solvency from the tenant of a main residence lease in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. As a landlord, you can choose your tenant freely. However, your choice cannot be based on a reason for discrimination. In order to make a selection, you can ask interested parties to provide you with certain information and related documents. Proof of the applicant's solvency is part of it. However, this information is limited to: the name and surname of the applicant; a means of communication with the applicant; the address of the applicant; the date of birth of the applicant or proof of his ability to enter into a contract; the composition of the applicant's household; the amount of financial resources available to the applicant.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia)

question: What's the commute back to work?
answer: Before going any further, be careful not to confuse the return to work journey with: the reintegration journey; the reorientation or rehabilitation journey. For more information, see our section <unk>Return to work during disability<unk>. The return to work journey is a procedure aimed at accompanying and supporting workers and the unemployed who are unable to work to resume a job adapted to their needs and abilities. The unemployed person can make a return to work journey if they have the physical and mental capacity to resume work. The unemployed person is accompanied and supported by the return to work coordinator.
topics: Work, Sickness - incapacity for work, Resumption of suitable work during incapacity, Return to work

question: Under what conditions can I apply for Belgian citizenship if I have lived in Belgium for five years?
answer: You can apply for Belgian citizenship if you can prove: an uninterrupted legal residence of at least 5 years; and a main residence in Belgium; and an unlimited residence permit at the time of application (B card, C card, D card, E card, F card, E+ card or F+ card); and that you are at least 18 years old; and knowledge of one of the 3 national languages (level A2 of the European reference framework); and your social integration; and your economic participation; You can prove your social integration in different ways: minimum: a high school diploma obtained in Belgium or vocational training of at least 400 hours; or an integration course with A2 level.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (from 18 years of age), persons residing in Belgium for at least 5 years

question: I think my dwelling is unhealthy. I am contacting the Walloon Region or the competent municipality to have a health and overcrowding survey carried out. Who should act if my dwelling is deemed unhealthy in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. However, before taking any action, it must hear the parties concerned. For example, the mayor who receives the results of a survey describing a dwelling as uninhabitable-improvable may make an order requiring the owner of the dwelling to repair the dwelling within a certain period of time. Otherwise, if the survey report concludes that the dwelling is uninhabitable, the mayor may order the closure of the dwelling or its demolition.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: Can my landlord evict me if I don't pay my rent?
answer: Only a justice of the peace can decide your eviction. The landlord who wants to evict you must have a judgment that pronounces the termination of the lease and your eviction. So you cannot be evicted by your landlord if he does not have the authorization of the justice of the peace in a judgment. The eviction judgment must be served to you by a justice of the peace. From that moment on, you have, in principle, 1 month to leave the housing. But the justice of the peace can provide for a shorter time: if you and the landlord have agreed; if the housing is abandoned; if there are circumstances of particular gravity.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a lease agreement

question: I didn't choose a student lease, what kind of lease is it in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further, since 1 January 2018, there is a student lease in Brussels, with specific rules for terminating the lease. The rules of the student lease apply to your lease only if: the accommodation is located in the Brussels area; you have signed or renewed your contract after 1 January 2018; you have explicitly chosen to use the student lease. Your lease is not a student lease. The rules of the student lease may be a main residence lease.

question: Does the official tenancy automatically end when it expires in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. If your apartment lease is also a main residence lease: It does not end automatically. You must send your notice to the tenants or the landlord in order for the lease to end: the tenant (s) must send their notice at least 3 months before the deadline. The landlord must send their notice: 3 months before the deadline for a short-term lease; 6 months before the deadline for a lease of 9 years or more. You must see your more details.

question: I ⁇ m a parent. What are the extraordinary expenses?
answer: Extraordinary expenses are all expenses that arise from "extraordinary" circumstances, i.e. that do not occur regularly. These expenses are not included in the food contribution. The food contribution covers only ordinary expenses, i.e. the expected expenses for the daily maintenance of the child. For example, food, its share of consumption of water and electricity; Exercise equipment, clothing, etc. Parents must bear the extraordinary expenses. If the parents are separated or divorced, it is necessary to determine: the extraordinary expenses, in view of the situation of the child; the distribution of these extraordinary expenses between the parents; how to claim them.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: How does the naturalization application process go?
answer: Once your publication is complete, the Registrar or the House of Representatives will send you an acknowledgement of receipt of your application. If you have addressed your application to the Registrar, he or she will send it to the House of Representatives within 15 days of receipt. Once your application has been published, the House of Representatives will send you a copy of the acknowledgement of receipt. Within 5 working days of receipt of your application by the House of Representatives, a notice will be sent to the Belgian Public Prosecutor, the Foreigners' Office and the State Security.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (over 18 years of age), persons claiming exceptional merits

question: Can I file an urgent appeal with the court against a decision of the CPAS?
answer: Depending on the judicial district, the procedure usually takes between 2 and 6 months, if you do what is necessary to ensure that the procedure is not delayed. But if you are in a particular emergency, you can file a request for referral before the president of the labor court. The president of the court makes a provisional decision, given the emergency. For example, if there is a serious risk of serious, imminent and difficult to repair damage to your health, you can get a decision in a few days, or even in a few hours in case of extreme urgency.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, challenging the decision of the CPAS, appeal to the labour court

question: What income is taken into account for legal aid if I live with my children?
answer: The legal aid office (BAJ) takes into account your monthly income and your means of subsistence, as well as those of your adult children. The BAJ calculates your monthly income in euros: net salary; replacement income: unemployment benefits, sick pay, pension, etc.; real estate or furniture income; etc. 26 BAJ has to take into account the average income. For example: BAJ has to take into account the total amount of vacation allowance and the multi-year allowance: EUR 13.92 (or EUR 12.92 if there is no monthly allowance), it is deducted from the daily allowance.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Income-related situations

question: I'm living as a couple, can I be dependent on my partner at the mutual?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only some general information. For any details or additional questions, please contact your mutual. Be aware of two things: The rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. The notion of a dependent person is not the same in the different social security schemes. For example, a dependent person for health insurance and disability is not necessarily a dependent person for disability benefits, nor for unemployment benefits. Yes, under certain conditions you must live together.

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. As a legal cohabitant, do I have to contribute to household expenses if I have no or few resources?
answer: Yes. Each of the legal cohabitants must contribute to all the expenses of living together. Each one contributes according to his personal resources, in proportion to his faculties. You can contribute to the expenses of the household financially, or otherwise: through housework, child rearing, etc. You cannot escape this obligation. You cannot provide, for example by a cohabitation agreement, that one of you does not contribute to the expenses of the household. You cannot, in the event of separation, demand that the other repay you all that you have invested for the expenses of the household (provided that each has contributed in proportion to his faculties).
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: Who can receive family allowances in Brussels?
answer: In principle, family allowances are paid to the child's mother. It is said that the mother is the beneficiary: it is she who receives the family allowances. But there are special cases. If the parents are of the same sex (comaternity or adoption by a homosexual couple), the oldest parent receives the family allowances. If the mother does not sign the Internet, the person who raises the child receives the family allowances. If the parents are separated, nothing changes in principle, except in special cases.
topics: Social protection, family and birth allowances, family allowances in Brussels

question: I live somewhere other than my home. I'm in prison, what happens to my home?

question: Can I ask the APA service of my mutual not to recover the allowance for the care of the elderly in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, you have two possibilities to try not to reimburse the APA. Contest the withdrawal decision of the APA service of your mutual before the labour court. You have 3 months from the receipt of the letter of recommendation sent by the APA service of your mutual to contest the withdrawal decision. For more information, see our section "Contest a social security decision". Ask the withdrawal committee to withdraw the APA service of your mutual. question: Can the creditors object to the judge's appointment of the person as the RCD's debt mediator?
answer: If there are legitimate reasons to doubt the independence or impartiality of the mediator, he may be disqualified at the request of one of the parties concerned (you, your creditors or your income debtors). The disqualification request must be submitted, by request, within 8 days from the date on which the applicant became aware of the reasons for the disqualification. It cannot be submitted after the deadline of 1 month from the dispatch of the admissibility decision, unless the reason for the disqualification was revealed to the applicant after that deadline.

question: I am married. Do I need to take out a family RC insurance?
answer: You are not obliged, but it is recommended. Family liability insurance, also known as "private life" liability insurance, is not legally mandatory. It is still highly recommended to take out one. Overall, family liability insurance covers damages caused by the insured's fault in their entire private life. For example: repair of the squash partner's broken pair of glasses, coverage of the neighbor's medical expenses following the bite of your dog, repair of the neighbor's window destroyed by your son's football, etc. This insurance covers the following:

question: What is a seizure of income?
answer: If your creditor has obtained your conviction to pay alimony in court, he may seize part of your income: your professional income, your unemployment benefits, your disability benefits, your pensions, etc. If you work, the employer is notified of the seizure and must pay to the creditor all the sums that are the subject of the seizure. If he does not do so, he may be declared a debtor and becomes a debtor.

question: Can the child decide for himself which parent he wants to live with?

question: What decisions can the Council for the Dispute of Aliens (CCE) take in an international protection case?
answer: The ECJ can make several types of decisions in response to your appeal against a negative decision of the CRA. The ECJ can also ask the ECJ to complete its investigation into your application for asylum. Your case is referred to the ECJ, which must make a new decision. The new decision must take into account the additional investigative duties requested by the ECJ. The ECJ can appeal against a negative decision to grant you refugee or subsidiary protection status only in the absence of a decision of the ECJ.

question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. We are legal cohabitants, what are the consequences on the relationship with our children?
answer: There is no difference between a child born to: married parents; legally cohabiting parents; cohabiting parents. Parental authority arises from the bond of filiation (who are the legal parents?) and not from your status as a couple (married, legally cohabiting or cohabiting). If the father recognized the child at birth, you are all 2 holders of parental authority. Your rights and obligations towards your children are therefore identical to those of married parents.

question: My neighbor is making a lot of noise, what to do?
answer: First of all, we have to prioritize dialogue! Noisy neighbors are often unaware of the disturbance they are causing. They can quickly change their attitude if you discuss it, especially when the solution is simple: use headphones for music, more regular walks for a noisy dog, etc. The advantages of the amicable solution are many: speed, free of charge and the possibility of a solution that suits everyone. There are other possibilities: Intervention of a mediator Anyone can play the role of "mediator"; for example, the police officer can play this role or the syndic in a co-ownership.

question: I am applying for family reunification. What are the conditions for family reunification with a recognised refugee?
answer: The family member who wants to join the recognised refugee (or beneficiary of subsidiary protection) in Belgium must attach to his file: a medical certificate certifying that he does not suffer from a disease that could endanger public health; an extract from the criminal record if he is over 18 years old; proof of family status; proof of refugee status or beneficiary of subsidiary protection from abroad.

question: I'm becoming a legal cohabitant. We're registered at the same address. Are we legal cohabitators?
answer: Legal cohabitation is a legal status offered to two people who wish to create a common life, and who are not already married or legal cohabitants. Legal cohabitation is addressed to two people who wish to live together and give a legal framework to this marriage. These two people can be of the same sex or not, live as a couple or not, be bound by bonds of friendship or by family ties. Legal cohabitation offers you the same rights and obligations.

question: What is the difference between the fixed rate and the charge provision in Brussels?
answer: The landlord cannot ask you to pay a surcharge because you have consumed a lot this year. However, you or your landlord can always ask the justice of the peace for the future only: the transformation of the lease or the change to real charges. The judge then rectifies the situation when it no longer corresponds to the original situation. If it no longer corresponds to the original situation, such as a change of family, or more or less frequent absences, you have to pay.

question: Under what conditions can an observation measure be requested?
answer: To be placed under observation, the person must meet three conditions: have a mental illness. This illness will be established by the doctor in his circumstantial medical report. This report states the mental illness of the person and describes in a precise and complete way how this illness represents a danger to the person himself or to others. Being on the margins of society does not constitute a mental illness.

question: What are the municipal criteria for unhealthiness in Wallonia?
answer: If you encounter a problem of this kind, you can contact the mayor of your municipality and/or the municipal service that ensures the right to decent housing. The mayor has a "general" police competence in matters of sanitation (he is therefore competent for housing but also for other buildings and for the public road). If the mayor finds a sanitation problem, he can take all the measures he wishes (they are not limited by law), for example: impose renovation work; demolish a building;

question: I am a student, am I entitled to social assistance?
answer: Yes. If you apply for financial social assistance, the CPAS may ask you to provide proof that you are willing to work. But if you are in full-time education, you are in principle not available to work. The CPAS may consider that your studies are a reason for equity that prevents you (partially) from working. To do so, the CPAS may accept that you do not prove your situation at work, or only during school holidays. But you must prove that the studies you are taking are likely to increase your chances of integration into working life. You must also prove your aptitude for studies and your willingness to improve your living conditions.

question: The child's father and mother live separately. Who can make decisions about the child if the parents are separated?
answer: Even if the child's parents are separated, they must make all the decisions about their minor child together. This is the principle of joint parental authority: the parents decide together. Each parent must therefore ask the other parent's consent before making a decision about his child (enrollment in school, authorization for an emergency operation, placing a sum of money in a bank account, choosing an extracurricular activity, authorization for a school trip, etc.). If the parents are separated, the one who hosts the child is not the only one called to make important decisions about this child at the time when it must be housed.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: We are separated or divorced, can we each receive half of the family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: Before we go any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since January 1, 2019, and since January 1, 2020.For more information, see the sheet What changes with the reform of family allowances? Yes. You can agree with each other, and you can share the amount of family allowances. You can, for example, provide that the mother receives family allowances, and then she returns half to the father. You can also provide that one of you keeps a little more than half, for example because he is entitled to a family allowance.

question: Can I go abroad and keep my income replacement allowance (ARR)?
answer: Yes. You can go abroad for a maximum of 90 days a year and continue to receive your income replacement allowance (ARR). You can take these 90 days at a time or in several times. You are not obliged to notify the Directorate-General for Disabled Persons (DGPH) of your stay abroad. However, it is advisable to do so. You continue to receive your ARR even if you stay abroad for more than 90 days a year if you are staying: in a hospital or a care facility; with a relative or ally who must temporarily stay abroad to carry out a mission or perform duties in the service of Belgium (e.g. military, diplomatic member in an embassy, etc.); with a relative or spouse who must temporarily stay abroad to carry out a mission or perform duties in the service of Belgium.

answer: No, the rental guarantee is not mandatory. But if it is provided in the lease, the rental guarantee is mandatory. question: I am a salaried worker. Can I gradually return to work after my maternity leave?
answer: Yes You can convert 2 weeks of optional maternity leave into days off. You must take these days off during the 8 weeks following your return to work. The aim is to allow you to return to work gradually and to take part of your postnatal leave on a part-time basis. To be able to do this, you must meet 2 conditions. The duration of your postnatal leave must be at least 11 weeks because you: carry forward optional weeks not taken in pre-natal protection. In other words, if you have not taken all 6 weeks of pre-natal leave; you must extend your postnatal leave because your employer has been hospitalized; or if you have given birth to twins or triplets.

question: I'm getting a divorce. Do I have to blame my spouse for getting a divorce?
answer: You don't have to prove that your spouse was at fault if you want to get a divorce. Fault divorce hasn't existed since 2007. Today, you can get a divorce on your own, without your spouse's consent and even if he or she didn't commit any fault. On the other hand, fault can affect the right to alimony. If you claim alimony, your spouse can invoke your grave fault for not having to pay, even if his or her standard of living is higher than yours.

question: My apartment has been declared uninhabitable due to unsanitary conditions.
answer: The loss has different consequences depending on whether it comes from a fortuitous case or the fault of one of the parties. question: As legal cohabitators, what judge can settle our problems before separation?

The measures that concern the children apply during the proceedings, and even after the end of the legal cohabitation, until a new decision intervenes. question: Can I stay in my social housing if I inherit a house in Wallonia?
answer: If you inherit a house (in usufruct or full ownership), the SLSP may terminate your social housing lease. Exceptions: you are not at risk of losing your social housing if: you are not the sole owner (or usufructuary) of your house; your house is uninhabitable, non-improvable or unsuitable; you give the management of your house to a social housing operator.

question: Under what conditions can I apply for Belgian nationality if I am disabled, invalid or retired?
answer: You can apply for Belgian citizenship if: you can prove that: you cannot, because of your disability or disability, have a job or engage in an economic activity, or that you have reached the age of retirement; and you are over 18 years old; and you have lived in Belgium legally for at least 5 years. You must make a declaration to the civil registry officer of your main place of residence. You must also submit a file containing: your birth certificate; a certificate of residence with a record of all your addresses; a photocopy of your residence card, certified that you meet the above conditions. All documents issued by foreign authorities must be legalized and translated.

question: Can I go abroad and keep my Grapa?
answer: Yes, you can go abroad for a maximum of 29 days a year and keep your Grapa. You can take these days in one or more installments. The day of your departure abroad and the day of your return to Belgium are not taken into account for the calculation of your 29 days. If you have to go abroad for more than 5 consecutive days, your Grapa is suspended. You no longer receive your Grapa during the months when you are abroad. You receive it again during your stay in Belgium.

question: Which CPAS is competent to help me create my rental guarantee in Brussels?

If you submit your application before moving into your new home (which is normally the case), you must contact the CPAS of your current place of residence (the one you are leaving). question: I am a professional and I am aware of a situation of domestic violence. Am I bound by professional secrecy if I am aware of domestic violence?

The penal code provides for the possibility of lifting professional secrecy if you are aware of domestic violence. This is not an obligation. In this case, you can report to the King's Prosecutor (PR).
