answer: No, the authorization of the other parent is not necessary in principle. It is recommended to provide for written authorization in case of travel abroad, especially if you have to take the plane. You can get a form type "authorization to leave the territory" from your municipal administration. If a judgment organizes the accommodation of your child, you can freely organize your holidays during your period of accommodation. You do not need the agreement of the other parent on the destination or the type of vacation. If your child is afraid of the danger of traveling abroad, etc.
topics: Family, Parent-child relationship, Child abduction abroad, Before the abduction
Family, Parent-child bond, Children's accommodation, After an agreement or a judgment

question: When can the landlord ask me to index the rent in Wallonia?
answer: If your landlord forgot to ask you to index the rent on the anniversary of the lease, he can ask you to index it later, when he thinks about it. But he can ask you to index only: for the last 3 months; and for the future. The rest is lost. Your landlord must use the health index that he would have used on the anniversary of the lease (not the one from the time he asks for indexation in our lease). For example: The lease was signed on November 1st.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: Is the guardian obliged to accept his appointment?
answer: No. The guardian must accept his mission. He can refuse. The justice of the peace must be sure that the person has accepted in order to be able to appoint him as guardian. The person appointed by the parents can refuse his mission: at the opening of the guardianship (for example at the death of the parents): the person can refuse to become a guardian; during the guardianship: the guardian can ask the judge to be relieved of his role as guardian. If the person chosen by the parents or by the justice of the peace does not accept the guardianship and there is no one else, it is the CPAS who takes care of it.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, guardianship, appointment of the guardian

question: Am I entitled to unemployment benefits if I agree to the termination of my employment contract by mutual agreement?
answer: In principle, no. If you sign a notice of termination by mutual agreement, the ONEM considers that you are voluntarily unemployed: you are responsible for your unemployment since you have agreed to the termination of your employment contract. You risk being excluded from unemployment benefits for 4 to 52 weeks. In practice, for the time being, the maximum exclusion for unemployment benefits is 13 weeks. This may be accompanied by a deferment. The exact duration of your exclusion (between 4 and 52 weeks) is decided by the director of the ONEM office.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, termination by mutual agreement

question: I receive confidences as a professional, do I have to tell the police everything if I'm bound by professional secrecy?
answer: No, in front of the police, you can't lift professional secrecy (unless, of course, you're in one of the exceptions provided by law - see the other sheets). Sometimes the police insist and try to convince you to give them information; saying that it's necessary for their investigation, or for the arrest of a dangerous person. This is probably part of a good intention, and you may be tempted to cooperate with the police in exchange for good services.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: What is an extract from a criminal record (certificate of good life and morals)?
answer: For example, if you are sentenced to a term of imprisonment, it is recorded in your criminal record. The criminal record gives information: to the judicial authorities: for example, the judge has access to the criminal record to check if you are a repeat offender; to public administrations: for example, to the police; or if you are looking for a job in education, to verify that you have not committed certain offences such as abuse of a minor. The criminal record extract is the document you get from your municipality.
topics: Justice, Judicial Record, Contents of the Judicial Record and Excerpts from the Judicial Record

question: What are the charges in Brussels?
answer: Charges represent the amounts that you have to pay in addition to your rent. They can relate to your home (private charges) or the co-ownership (common charges). These are for example your gas consumption, electricity consumption, the owner of the co-ownership, the cleaning of the hall, etc. You sometimes pay some charges directly to the supplier. This is especially the case when you yourself have concluded an electricity contract with a supplier; In this case, you pay the bills directly to the supplier of electricity.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, charges and insurance, Charges

question: Will the rent of my student lease be indexed in Brussels?
answer: Before we go any further, since January 1, 2018, there is a student lease in Brussels, with specific rules for terminating the lease. The rules of the student lease apply to your lease only if: the apartment is located in the Brussels area; you have signed or renewed your lease after January 1, 2018; you have chosen to use the student lease. Yes, under certain conditions. Please check our documents: How can my rent be indexed (Brussels) It calculates the index of my rent?
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, Kot (Brussels), student lease

question: As legal cohabitants, can the family judge order one of us to leave the house?
answer: Yes, each of you can ask the judge to decide, depending on the circumstances, to whom to attribute the family housing. Attention, this decision of the judge is in principle limited in time (urgent and temporary measures). In situations of marital cohabitation, the family housing must be attributed preferably to the victim of marital violence. The other cohabitant must expressly ask the judge to be able to stay in the housing, whether at the time he asks for separation; the judge must settle the violence and the victim has been convicted of domestic violence. Attention, this decision of the judge is in principle limited in time.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation, separation before the judge

question: Can you apply for and receive the birth allowance before the birth?
answer: Yes, you can apply for the birth allowance in advance, after 24 weeks of pregnancy. You can also apply for it at the time of the child's birth, or later, up to 5 years after the child's birth, even if the child is stillborn. For more information, see the sheet What changes with the birth allowance reform? Yes, you can apply for the birth allowance in advance, after 24 weeks of pregnancy.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowances, Birth allowance for children born from 1 January 2020

question: I make sure the landlord has a rental permit.
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. Yes. The rental permit is valid for 5 years, but the municipality and the region can make control visits. Neither the landlord nor the tenant can oppose it. These visits are made either on their own initiative or as a result of complaints.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: What documents does my employer have to give me when my employment contract is terminated?
answer: At the end of your employment contract, your future employer must give you several documents: a vacation certificate (only for employees). This document tells you how many days of vacation you have already taken in the year. Thanks to this document, your future employer knows: how many days of vacation you have left to take until the end of the year; your last paycheck; an unemployment certificate (C). This document allows you to submit an application for unemployment benefits to an employer; a payment certificate (the CAPAC or your employer) tax return 2810.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract

question: I am a minor, am I entitled to the RIS?
answer: You are not entitled to social integration income (RIS), but you can apply for social assistance. 1. In principle, minors are not entitled to RIS. Exception: minors who are assimilated to adults are entitled to RIS, if they meet the other conditions. Minors who are assimilated to adults are: the minor who proves that she is pregnant; the minor who has one or more children under her care (not necessarily her own children); the minor emancipated by marriage.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Being of age or assimilated to an adult

question: Can I go abroad and keep my elderly care allowance in Brussels?
answer: Yes. You can go abroad for a maximum of 90 days a year and continue to receive your elderly care allowance (APA). You can take these 90 days at a time or in several times. You are not obliged to notify Iriscare APA Service of your stay abroad. However, it is advisable to do so. You continue to receive your APA even if you are staying abroad for more than 90 days a year if you are staying: in a hospital or care facility with a relative or ally who needs to stay; temporarily staying abroad to carry out a mission or perform duties in the service of India (e.g. military, diplomatic agent in an embassy, etc.); with a relative or ally whose spouse needs to stay abroad to carry out a mission or perform duties in the service of Belgium.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Brussels

question: What income is taken into account for legal aid if I live with a person with whom I have a conflict?
answer: If you have a conflict with your spouse or cohabitant (for example, if you want to get a divorce), the BAJ does not take into account the income of that person when calculating your income and livelihood. The BAJ only takes into account your income and that of other people who live with you but with whom you do not have a conflict of interest. To find out how this income is taken into account, see the questions below, depending on the composition of your household without taking into account the person with whom you have a conflict:
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Income-related situations

question: Are there any costs during the procedure?
answer: Yes. Different procedural incidents can occur, once the case is brought. The cost of these incidents is variable. It is part of the court fees. For example: a view of the premises by the judge: relatively low cost; a view of the premises by the judge but in the presence of an expert: the cost is higher (about EUR 300), but it can save you a much more expensive and longer classical expertise; a witness hearing (about EUR 5 per witness); an expertise: - in real estate matters, it happens that the cost of the expertise exceeds (largely) EUR 2,500; - in family matters, a pediatric psychiatric expertise (to assess the educational abilities of parents, parents and the child, and make proposals) can also be heard.
topics: Justice, In court, Expenses related to the functioning of justice

question: My child's name is misspelled on his birth certificate. Can I have this mistake corrected?
answer: Yes, you can have this error corrected easily and free of charge. You must contact the population service of your municipality. You must report the error and prove it by showing other official documents on which the name is correctly spelled. If it is your child's name, you can for example present your identity card, or that of the other parent, on which the name is correctly spelled. If it is the first name or the date of birth, the birth certificate issued by the hospital can serve as evidence.
topics: Family, Relationship between parents and children, Transmission of the name to the child, Mistake or change of name

question: When are my resources considered insufficient for the RIS?
answer: If your resources are less than the amount of the RIS of your category (single, cohabitant, or person living with a dependent family), they are considered insufficient. You are therefore entitled to the amount of the RIS of your category (single, cohabitant, or person living with a dependent family) if you meet all other conditions. To be entitled to the RIS, you cannot have sufficient resources, nor be able to obtain them yourself. Are you entitled to the RIS? Your resources exceed the amount of the RIS of your category. You are not entitled to the RIS.
topics: Social protection, Aid from the CPAS, Social integration income (RIS), Not having sufficient resources

question: I have a PEB certificate A, B or C. How do I calculate the indexation of my rent in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further From 1 November 2022 to 31 October 2023, there are temporary rules on rent indexation in Wallonia depending on the PEB certificate. Your rent indexation is calculated according to the legal formula. See the sheet "How to calculate my rent indexation (Wallonia) ?". The amount of indexation does not vary according to your PEB certificate, if you have a PEB certificate A, B or C. For more information, see the SPW Housing website.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Are the property I owned before our legal cohabitation common?
answer: No. If you are legal cohabitants, your property is separated. Otherwise, in the absence of proof, the property is presumed to belong to you. All property you owned before the beginning of the legal cohabitation remains yours, i.e. you are the sole owner. You must also pay the debts related to your property (unless these debts are or become a burden of the household).
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: I am unemployed and I have children. Am I exempt from looking for a job if I take care of a sick relative?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. You can apply for an exemption for caring for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the first pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age. You must: be completely unemployed; and provide in an effective, permanent and regular manner: either palliative care; either care for a member of your household or family (parent or ally) up to the 2nd degree, who is seriously ill; or care for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the 1st pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age; You must apply for this exemption; You must complete a new application for the exemption from unemployment benefit.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: Hosting a friend: can the owner forbid me to do it in Wallonia?
answer: If the lease agreement limits the accommodation of friends: The lease agreement may provide for restrictions on the accommodation of friends. For example, when the landlord lives in the same building as the free tenant, these restrictions may not be excessive. For example, a total eviction ban would be excessive, because it would prevent you from having a private, emotional or family life. In case of eviction, it is up to the justice of the peace to assess whether the eviction is excessive or not.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), (un) legal clauses

question: How and how often are home visits organised in Brussels?
answer: Your landlord must contact you to make an appointment. You cannot object to your landlord exercising his right of visitation unless you have a reasonable reason (e.g. the landlord has already visited your home recently). Without this prior authorization, a "wild" visit may be considered a violation of your home. Your landlord may not enter your home in your absence, except in exceptional circumstances (e.g. when a water leak occurs and your home is uninhabitable).
topics: Accommodation, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Visit of the accommodation

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. How much do I have to pay to file a lawsuit?
answer: For example, in the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, EUR 200 for the registration fee, etc. In some cases, for example, in the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, etc. In the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, etc. In the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, etc. In the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, etc. In the case of a lawsuit, you have to pay EUR 400 for the registration fee, etc.
topics: Justice, In court, Introduction of the application

question: I'm unemployed and I have children. I'm unemployed, am I entitled to the dependent family rate if one of my children has an income?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were previously self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules. For more information, please contact the National Employment Office (ONEM), or your payment agency (union or CAPAC <unk> Auxiliary Fund for the Payment of Unemployment Benefits). If you live only with one or more children, you are entitled to family allowances for at least one child.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: I'm a landlord. What does my fire insurance cover in Wallonia?
answer: The following risks are covered unless your insurance contract excludes them: fire; damage caused by lightning, explosion, implosion, fall or impact of aircraft or objects. The objects or animals that fall or are projected to fall (e.g. car, pigeons, etc.) are normally also covered by fire insurance: damage caused by bombings, damage caused by labour conflicts, damage caused by storms, damage caused by natural disasters. Other risks are regularly covered: electricity; smoke; water; civil liability.
topics: Money, Insurance, Fire insurance, Renting a house, In Wallonia

question: Can the CPAS ask me to find a job to qualify for the RIS?
answer: Yes. To be entitled to social integration income (RIS), you must "be willing to work". So the CPAS can ask you to prove that you are actively looking for a job. If you do not, the CPAS can withdraw your RIS. To appreciate your efforts to find a job, the CPAS must take into account your concrete personal possibilities, depending on your personal situation, your age, your education, your health, your education, etc. Specifically, the CPAS must help and support you in your efforts to find a job.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Willingness to work

question: I'm a European, I'm a European and I'm coming to Belgium for a few weeks.
answer: Before going any further, the right to social integration income (RIS and social assistance) of European citizens depends on several elements: The right to residence: E-card, F-card, annex 19, annex 19to register, annex 20, annex 21 or annex 35.The date on which the residence permit was issued.The reason for residence: European worker (and his family members), European jobseeker (and his family member), other categories of European citizens (student, holder of a CP card).It is always necessary to distinguish between the right to RIS and the right to social assistance.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and CPAS aids

question: What happens to the lease in the event of the death of the owner in Wallonia?
answer: If the landlord was married or a legal cohabitant, one of the legal heirs is the surviving spouse who is entitled to use the housing by personally occupying it or by collecting the rent.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Death

question: The parents are not married together. The child's mother is married to someone other than the father. Before which court do you go in case of a problem related to parentage?
answer: The family court deals with all matters of parentage. You should in principle go to the family court of the child's home. To find the address of the court, go to this link. To contact the family court, there are two possibilities: You should in principle make a citation. You should go to a bailiff, who officially transmits your request and summons the other parties (probably the other parent and the child) to the court.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. The perpetrator of domestic violence is a non-European foreigner, will I lose my residence permit?
answer: In order to obtain family reunification, it is necessary to have an effective family or marital life between the two spouses. For more information, see Belgium: Romanian are the conditions for a psychological reunification; with a foreigner (outside the EU) in a couple he or she has a foreign nationality, they have obtained their residence permit through family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) in a limited residence permit, so their residence permit is linked to that of their partner.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: When and how to create a rental guarantee in Brussels?
answer: When do I have to create the rental guarantee? There is no time when the rental guarantee has to be created. It all depends on what has been agreed in writing with your landlord. So in general, it is at the time indicated in your lease agreement. Generally, either the contract does not provide for anything, or the contract provides that you have to create the rental guarantee before entering the housing. How do I create the rental guarantee? In principle, you can choose the type of guarantee you prefer.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Creating a rental guarantee

question: What happens if changes are made to my rental apartment in Wallonia?
answer: If major changes have been made to the dwelling after the state of the premises, you can demand an amendment to the state of the premises. Simple repairs do not entail an amendment. The landlord cannot impose an increase in rent as a result of the work carried out. You are never obliged to accept an increase in rent, unless the judge imposes it on you.
topics: Housing, Rental in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Repairs, maintenance and works, Works and improvements: consequences

question: I'm being asked for a vacation certificate from my old job, what's the point?
answer: Your new employer knows how many days of leave you have left to take by the end of the year. It also knows how many days of leave are covered by the leave allowance you received from your old employer when you left. If your new job offers you more days of leave than those covered by your leave allowance (e.g. extra-legal leave days), your new employer only pays you those days.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, holiday allowance

question: We're not married, what will our child's name be if his father recognizes him before or at birth?
answer: If you have a child born before June 1, 2014, you can choose the name of your child from the following options: father's name; mother's name; mother's name + father's name (one name per parent); father's name + mother's name (one name per parent). Be careful, all children of the same siblings (same father and same mother) must also have the same surname. Once you have a child, you can keep the same name for the next child (s). If you do not agree with the other parent's name, your name has the same surname.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Transmission of the name to the child, Unmarried parents

question: Can the landlord vacate the dwelling of his deceased tenant in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, if the dwelling is unoccupied and the deceased tenant's furniture is still in it. Since September 1, 2018, the landlord can evacuate the dwelling of the deceased tenant by following a strict step-by-step procedure. The landlord must comply with: a period of 3 months after the death of the tenant; a period of 3 months after the death of the tenant; a period of 3 months after the death of the tenant; a period of 3 months after the death of the tenant; a period of 3 months after the death of the tenant.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Death

question: Will I lose my survivor's pension or my transition allowance if I have other income?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for wage earners, self-employed workers and civil servants. If you meet the two conditions of marriage and age, you are only entitled to general information. For any details or additional questions, please contact: Federal Pension Service (SFP) for employees and civil servants, National Pension Insurance for Self-Employed Workers (INTIAS). Not necessarily the Internet.
topics: Social Protection, Pensions and Grapa, Survival Pension

question: Do I have to apply for a planning permit if I build a garden shed in Wallonia?
answer: You can install or remove a garden shelter without a planning permit if all of the following conditions are met: you have no other garden shelter; the shelter is not visible from the roadway or the shelter is located at the back of your building relative to the roadway; the shelter is located at least 1m from other properties; the shelter has a maximum area of 20m2, its height does not exceed 2.50m at the gutter and 3.50m at the fault (calculated from the natural ground level).
topics: Housing, urban planning, Wallonia and the surrounding area

question: I am joining a foreigner authorised to stay in Belgium (non-European).
answer: Yes, but only in certain cases. In principle, you must submit your application for family reunification from your country of origin to the Belgian embassy or consulate competent for your place of residence. In some situations, you can submit your application directly from Belgium to the municipal administration of your place of residence. These are the following cases: Either you already have a residence permit of more than 3 months in Belgium. Either you are admitted to stay for less than 3 months in Belgium and you are in one of the following situations: you are exempt from the visa requirement; you have a visa to return to another country of the European Union; you have a visa for a minor child or a consular cohabitation.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: Do I always have to undergo a medical examination before returning to work?
answer: 1. If you feel you are able to return to work, the employer cannot refuse to take you back, or require a certificate of recovery from your attending physician. It does not matter if you return to work before the end of your medical certificate or at the end of your medical certificate. 2. But if you are in a "risky position" for your health, you may have to pass a return to work examination. For example, if you are employed in a security position, or exposed to chemicals and toxic products.
topics: Work, Sickness - incapacity for work, Resumption of work after incapacity

question: Can all the roommates together end the unofficial roommate lease before it expires in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. It depends on the type of lease. If the lease is (also) a main residence lease: The tenants can terminate the lease all together if they want to leave the apartment at the same time. They must comply with two conditions: send a three-month notice to the landlord. The three-month period begins only on the first day of the following month. All tenants must sign the lease if they are in solidarity.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Duration and end of co-location, Duration and end of the unofficial co-location lease (Co-location Brussels)

question: What to do if a roommate no longer pays in Wallonia?
answer: Look at what's in your tenancy agreement. If you've signed an official tenancy agreement, you've officially written a tenancy agreement. That is, the landlord can ask you to pay the share of another tenant. If you don't, you're wrong and can sue him. If you've paid the share of your tenant, you can claim the distribution of that share.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Loyers, charges and insurance

question: I'm starting a new life. I'm living alone. I'm working. How can I dispute the amount I have to pay?
answer: If you do not agree with the amount of contributions mentioned on the role-extract warning you have just received, you can: 1. Apply for correction to your tax office. 2. Apply to the Advocate General. 4. You can file a complaint with the Advocate General in charge of the establishment of income taxes.
topics: Money, taxes

question: Can I work as a student?
answer: To work as a student you must meet two conditions. 1. You must be a student. There is no definition of the word "student". Your studies must be your main activity. Your work must be your secondary activity. So it must be checked on a case-by-case basis whether you are considered a student. For example, you are no longer a student if you attend evening classes, reduced-hour education, etc. In case of doubt, you can contact the Control of Social Laws of the Federal Public Service Employment, Labour and Social Concertation 2. You must be of a certain age to work as a student.
topics: Work, salaried work, student work

question: What are the types of rental guarantee in Brussels?
answer: However, in practice, the contract generally provides that "the tenant has opted for this type of guarantee". In this case, you must comply with what is stipulated in the contract. This does not mean that the landlord can provide for anything. You must comply with these 3 types: 1. The guarantee on a blocked and individualized account (max 2 months of rent) requires you to open the account yourself. This is even the most logical solution, since it is an account in your name. Most banks have collateral contracts that you only have to sign with your landlord.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Creating a rental guarantee

question: I'm unemployed and I live with a couple. Am I exempt from looking for a job if I'm taking care of a sick relative?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. You can apply for an exemption for caring for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the first pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age. You must: be completely unemployed; and provide in an effective, permanent and regular manner: either palliative care; either care for a member of your household or family (parent or ally) up to the 2nd degree, who is seriously ill; or care for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the 1st pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age; You must apply for this exemption; You must complete a new application for the exemption from unemployment benefit.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: Where can I find a lease application form in Wallonia?
answer: You can usually find application templates at the registry of the justice of the peace concerned. You can also find application templates on the website rechtbanken-tribunaux. Each judicial district offers different application templates. To find the application templates for your district, you must: go to the bottom of the page and choose your district on the card; 2. then click on <unk> Forms <unk> in the black box on the right side of the screen. If your district does not have a suitable model for you, you can propose to change it.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Resolving a rental dispute (Wallonia)

question: My child is sick. Can I take a leave of absence?
answer: If you work in the private sector, you can apply for leave for compelling reasons. This is a leave you can take, for example, when you have to stay with your sick child. You are entitled to a minimum of 10 days of leave for compelling reasons per year. A collective agreement (CCT) may provide for a longer period of time for the sector in which you work. You must notify your employer of your absence as soon as possible and give him a medical certificate. This leave is in principle unpaid, unless it is paid.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, leave for young parents

question: I've been summoned to court. Who can I contact for information on the court proceedings?
answer: 1. You can find information online on the website of the courts-rechtbanken. You will also find information about the courts, including: a search function to find the type of court that is competent depending on the type of problem (here); a glossary; explanatory brochures. 2. You can also refer to the registry of the jurisdiction concerned (the registry of the justice of the peace, the registry of the family, the registry of the court, etc.).
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Summoning to hearing

question: My landlord is not making the necessary repairs, can I make the repairs for him in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. The first thing to do is to check who has to make the repairs: you or your landlord. To do this, you need to specify that it is you who makes the repairs but that it is he (the landlord) who pays for them. If your landlord has not authorized you to do the repairs in his place, you must warn him. If the repairs are not urgent, you cannot do them in his place.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work, Inaction of the owner

question: How can I know the takeover value of the co-ownership?
answer: If you can't agree on the price, you can ask: a mediator; a justice of the peace. Where to find a mediator? Some cities and municipalities offer a neighborhood mediation service. Please contact your municipality. The website of the Federal Postal Mediation Commission offers a free list of approved mediators.
topics: Accommodation, neighbourhood, neighbourhood

question: Do I have to pay court fees if I contest a decision of a social security agency?
answer: No, it's always the social security organization that pays the costs of the proceedings, called expenses, even if you don't win your case. This is the case when your proceedings are brought against all the rules of common sense and it was almost certainly doomed to failure. In practice, this is very rare.
topics: Social protection, challenging a social security decision

question: What is a declaration of preference?
answer: It is a declaration that indicates the person you would like the judge to appoint as administrator of property and/or the person, if one day you are unable to manage your property and/or your person. You can make your declaration preferably with a notary, or with the justice of the peace of your place of residence. The declaration is then registered at the central declaration center. It costs EUR 18,50 for VAT. This declaration of preference is not a designated designation that the administrator you have chosen believes is capable, it will require:
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), Anticipating a protection measure

question: I'm self-employed. I'm giving birth earlier or later than expected. What are the consequences on my maternity leave if I'm self-employed?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The self-employed regime applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For ease of use, here we use the term "self-employed woman" in the broad sense, encompassing these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the self-employed regime.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: Real estate agency management fees: do I have to pay them in Brussels?
answer: No, the fees of a real estate agency cannot be claimed from you if the agency intervenes for the landlord. On the other hand, they can be claimed from you if you yourself asked the real estate agency to intervene (for example, you mandated it because the landlord accepts your early departure provided that you find a new tenant). If a clause in your lease stipulates that you have to pay the agency fees (rent, management fees during the lease, etc.), you can refuse to apply it (and therefore to pay) and challenge it before the justice of the peace.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, expenses and insurance, Charges, Distribution of expenses

question: What is the risk of the person with whom I am registered as a reference address?
answer: Your household composition You do not appear on the household composition of the person with whom you are registered as a reference address, so you are not considered cohabitators. Municipalities generally refuse to register as a reference address with a person with whom you have a direct relationship to the debtor to avoid fraud regarding the status of "cohabitant". However, this restriction is not provided for by law.
topics: Accommodation, residence, reference address, reference address of an individual

question: Can I not pay my last two months' rent to make sure I get my rent guarantee back in Brussels?
answer: No. It's two different things. You can't: decide for yourself not to pay the last two months of rent because you've paid a mortgage; break the lease two months before the term on that basis alone. On the other hand, if your landlord agrees, there's no problem. In that case, put your agreement in writing.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Receiving the rental guarantee
Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Functioning of the rental guarantee

question: If my lease is not registered, can I terminate it without notice or compensation in Brussels?
answer: The rules presented in this sheet apply to leases signed in the Brussels region: after 1 January 2018; before 1 January 2018 and which are still in progress. Be careful before terminating the lease! Check that your lease is not registered. Your landlord may have done so without you. To find out, you can ask the landlord at the registration office of the place where your apartment is located (coordinated here).
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Registration

question: I don't have a PEB certificate. Can my rent be indexed in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further From 14 October 2022 to 13 October 2023, there are temporary rules on rent indexation in Brussels depending on the PEB certificate. No. Your landlord must inform you of the PEB certificate (new since 14 October 2022). If your landlord indexed your rent before 14 October 2022, he can do so even if you did not have a PEB certificate. For more information, see our sheets: "What is rent indexation (Brussels) ?" "How to calculate rent indexation (Brussels) ?".
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I'm a victim of domestic violence. I'm a friend of the victim of domestic violence, can I file a complaint?
answer: If the situation is serious and urgent, it is important to act and contact the police. The police will also try to convince your friend to go to a victim support service (SAV - within the prosecutor's office) or a police victim assistance service (SAPV - within the police zones). These services can provide initial legal information but also offer psychological and social follow-up.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: Does my CDD end automatically on the scheduled date?
answer: Yes. The CDD ends automatically on the date indicated in the contract. No need to send notice or pay compensation. But to avoid any misunderstanding, it is better to send a written confirmation to remind, a little before the scheduled date, that the contract ends on such a date. Moreover, if the worker continues to work after the scheduled date, and if the employer agrees and continues to pay it, the CDD becomes a CDI.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, termination of the fixed-term contract (CDD)

question: Am I entitled to the birth allowance if I have a miscarriage or if my baby is stillborn?
answer: Before we go any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since January 1, 2019, and since January 1, 2020.For more information, see the sheet <unk> What changes with the reform of family allowances? <unk> Yes, if your pregnancy lasted at least 180 days, you are entitled to the birth allowance. You must send <unk> the certificate of <unk> child without life <unk> drawn up by the municipality to your family allowance fund against, if the miscarriage occurs before the 180th day of conception, you are not entitled to the birth allowance.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowances, Birth allowance for children born from 1 January 2020

question: I'm an employee. Am I entitled to paternity leave if I'm an employee?
answer: Before we go any further, only employees, the self-employed and some civil servants are entitled to paternity leave. The unemployed are not entitled to paternity leave. Be careful, the rules are not the same for public sector workers (employees) or self-employed workers. Paternity leave is called paternity leave because gay partners are also entitled to it, under certain conditions. You are entitled to paternity leave if you are a private sector employee or a mutual agent.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid paternity leave (birth leave)

question: Is my lease a short-term lease in Brussels?
answer: Before we go any further, it's not because you've signed a short-term lease that you're still in a short-term lease! If these conditions are not met, your nine-year lease becomes a nine-year lease if: you've renewed the lease and the total duration of the renewed leases is three years; or you've stayed in the lease after the lease expires and you haven't renewed the lease; you haven't signed a lease and you haven't agreed to a lease; and in the same case, you're supposed to leave the lease.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, End of short-term lease (3 years or less)

question: I'm filing a complaint with a mediator. The company is located in Belgium. How soon will the consumer mediator respond to me?
answer: The Consumer Ombudsman (SMC) has 90 days to deal with your complaint. However, the SMC can extend this deadline by an additional 90 days. It can only do so once. The SMC warns you of the extension of the deadline.
topics: Money, Consumer protection, Resolving a dispute with a company, Consumer Ombudsman (SMC)

question: How to obtain the status of long-term resident in Belgium?
answer: To obtain the status of a long-term resident in Belgium, you must: be a citizen of a third country (non-European); be legally resident: you must therefore have an A, B (certificate of registration with the administration of foreigners), C (foreign identity card), F or F+ (family member of a citizen of the European Union). Some foreigners are excluded, such as students or persons who have applied for international protection; prove a legal and uninterrupted stay in Belgium during the 5 months immediately preceding the application for the acquisition of long-term resident status (the period is calculated according to the rules above); you must provide proof of sufficient resources to support your family.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, long-term resident

question: When should the cohabitation agreement be signed in Wallonia?
answer: You must sign the tenancy agreement at the latest on the day of signing the tenancy lease. Attention, all tenants must sign the tenancy lease on that day. The date of signing the tenancy lease must be indicated in the tenancy lease. For more information, see our sheets: "What is a tenancy lease?" "What should my tenancy lease contain?"
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Location (Wallonia)

question: My dwelling has been declared uninhabitable because of unsanitary conditions.
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. When a dwelling is declared uninhabitable or denied access (on the basis of the Walloon Code of Sustainable Housing), the mayor has the decision on the dwelling displayed. The decision remains displayed on the dwelling for as long as it applies. In addition, the municipality keeps up to date a list of dwellings declared uninhabitable or denied access.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: I've been summoned to court, what's the schedule for the exchange of conclusions?
answer: At the first hearing, if the defendant wishes to draw up conclusions to challenge the plaintiff's arguments, the parties set a schedule for the exchange of documents and conclusions. The parties must agree on the deadlines for this schedule for the exchange of documents. They can themselves agree on the number of times they will conclude. In principle, it is the defendant who begins to conclude, and it is also the defendant who concludes last.
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Conduct of hearings

question: How does care in the family environment take place in the context of observation?
answer: If protective measures are necessary, but the patient's condition and circumstances allow him to be treated outside the hospital environment (for example in the case of mild disorders not requiring heavy therapy), the patient can be treated in a "family environment". By "family", we mean: the patient's own family; or any other family; or any other place in which the patient feels like in his family (home, community, retirement home or rest).
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: Can I combine the income replacement allowance (ARR), the integration allowance (AI) and the elderly care allowance (APA)?
answer: You can combine: the income replacement allowance (ARR) and the integration allowance (AI). You must meet the conditions of the ARR and the AI to be able to combine them. The allowance for assistance to the elderly (APA) cannot be combined with the ARR or with the AI. If you were already receiving an ARR and not receiving an AI, please inform yourself before applying for an API. Look at what is most advantageous to keep your ARP and receive your AI or receive the APA. If you decide to receive the APA, the Directorate General for Disabled Persons (DGPH) stops paying the ARP.
topics: Social protection, disability, various allowances for disabled people

question: Can all the roommates together terminate the official roommate lease before it expires in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet deals only with the case where all the tenants wish to terminate the lease. For the other tenants' leases, see our section "Rent and termination of the lease". If not all tenants wish to terminate the lease or not all tenants wish to terminate the lease at the same time, see our sheet. If the lease is an official main residence lease, all tenants can terminate the lease only if they leave the apartment at the same time.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Duration and end of co-location, Duration and end of the official co-location lease (Co-location Brussels)

question: I'm breaking up. We're breaking up, what happens to the house we bought together?
answer: If you have bought a house together, you are divided on this property. If you separate, you do not have to pay notary fees and registration fees (1% of the total value of the property). If a mortgage loan is still pending, the bank must authorize the one who serves to repay the loan. In this case, you must necessarily go to a notary to sign an deed of transfer. You must set the value of the transferred share. The notary can help you with this.
topics: Family, situation of couples, free union
Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation
Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I am a salaried worker. Is maternity leave converted into paternity leave if the mother is hospitalized?
answer: Before we go any further, please note that this sheet only applies to paternity leave for salaried workers. The rules are not the same for public sector workers (employees) or self-employed workers. Officially, paternity leave is called maternity leave because homosexual partners are also entitled to it, under certain conditions. Yes, under certain conditions: the mother must be hospitalized for more than 7 days after birth; and the child can go home with you. You must also meet all the following conditions: The father or co-parent and the mother must be employed.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid paternity leave (birth leave)

question: How does the CPAS decide to take into account the resources of my parents or adult children?
answer: In order to find out at what rate you are entitled to social integration income (RIS), the CPAS must carry out a social survey. It must examine your personal and family situation. It must in principle meet you and meet your parents or adult children. In practice, the CPAS sometimes simply sends you a document to fill in. The objective of the social survey is to verify: whether your parents or adult children are present; whether your parents or adult children can help you financially sufficiently to enable you to live.
topics: Social protection, Aid from the CPAS, Social integration income (RIS), Not having sufficient resources

question: My children's father just died and they're minors.
answer: Since your children are minors, they don't have any proceedings to make. You're the one who represents them for all the deeds related to their father's estate. First of all, you need to make an inventory of the assets and liabilities. The assets correspond to the assets of the deceased: bank accounts, house, car, etc. The liabilities correspond to the debts of the deceased: credits, invoices, funeral expenses, hospital expenses, etc. If a notary is in charge of the estate, he can help you establish this inventory.
topics: Money, death, inheritance and will, proceedings after death, formalities after death

question: My partner died. What are my rights in his estate?
answer: Before going any further, you must check your marital status. Married? Legal cohabitant? Single? If you have never been married or if you have not legally cohabited with the deceased, you are considered single. The law considers you to be "foreign" or "foreign" to the deceased. 1. You are married. You inherit the usufruct of the entire estate (buildings, furniture, vehicles, bank accounts, etc.) if the deceased partner leaves children or grandchildren.
topics: Money, Death, Succession and will, Succession and situations of couples

question: Can I be asked to refund the integration allowance (AI) I received?
answer: Yes, if you received an integration allowance (AI) to which you were not entitled. This is called the reimbursement of the lindu (you did not declare your income, or you did not say that you lived with another person, etc.). The General Directorate of Disabled Persons (DGPH) can recover what it paid you unjustly. This means that the DGPH can no longer recover the lindu.
topics: Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: My doctor made a medical mistake, is he responsible?
answer: Your doctor is liable if: he has committed a fault; you have suffered damage; there is a causal link between that fault and your damage. To be able to be compensated, you must prove the existence and extent of your damage, for this it is advisable to call an expert. You must prove the fault and the causal link that exists between the damage and the fault. And if your doctor has an obligation result, you must prove that he had an obligation result; and if the result is not reached, your doctor must operate on your left knee and he operates on your right knee.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, medical failure

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am in Belgium illegally. Can I recognize my child in Belgium?
answer: Yes, as a father, you can recognize your child born in Belgium if you meet one of the following criteria: you are domiciled in Belgium; you are habitually resident in Belgium at the time of recognition; your child was born in Belgium; your child's mother has her habitual residence in Belgium at the time of recognition.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Am I entitled to unemployment if I resign?
answer: For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEMON); your payment agency: union or LAC (Association for the Payment of Unemployment Allowances); In principle, you are not entitled to unemployment, unless you have been dismissed for reasons beyond your control. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEMON); your payment agency: union or LAC (Association for the Payment of Unemployment Allowances); In principle, you are not entitled to unemployment, unless you are dismissed for reasons beyond your control.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of employment contract, resignation

question: Does the income replacement allowance (ARR) decrease if I live with other people?
answer: Living with other people does not make you lose your right to an income replacement allowance (ARR). On the other hand, it can reduce or increase the amount of your allowance, depending on the relationships you have with these people. The amount of your ARR depends on: 1. Your family situation The amount of ARR depends on your family situation Depending on the people you live with, you belong to one of these 3 categories: Category A = cohabiting; Category B = isolated; Category C = cohabiting with a dependent family (head of household).
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits
Social protection, disability, income replacement allowance (ARR)

question: End of the observation ordered urgently by the King's Prosecutor?
answer: If observation has been urgently decided by the King's Prosecutor, this measure shall cease if the King's Prosecutor has not requested confirmation from the justice of the peace within 24 hours. Similarly, the King's Prosecutor may always terminate the measure until the justice of the peace has confirmed the observation decided on an emergency basis. Finally, the chief medical officer may also terminate the measure if he considers that the sick person no longer fulfils the necessary conditions for such a protection measure.
topics: Privacy, personal protection measures, protection of the mentally ill, treatment in a hospital environment

question: I have a procedure in another judicial district, what should I do?
answer: For each judicial district, there is a bar association. And for each bar association, there is a legal aid office (BAJ). Each BAJ can only appoint lawyers from its bar association. But the appointed lawyer can obviously move around, both inside and outside the district, depending on the needs of the case (searches, appointments, hearings, etc.). He can plead before any court (tribunal or court) in Belgium. If you want a lawyer from Namur or Brussels, you must contact the BAJ of your district.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro deo), Steps to obtain legal aid (ex pro deo)

question: I'm an employee. Can I get my maternity leave back if the mother is dead?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this sheet only covers paternity leave for salaried workers. The rules are not the same for public sector workers (public servants) or self-employed workers. You must prove that you are a salaried employee. Otherwise, you must be a salaried employee and be paid as a salaried employee.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid paternity leave (birth leave)

question: How to recover the rental guarantee in Brussels?
answer: If your landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered If the landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered If the landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered If the landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered If the landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered If the landlord and you agree on the amount of money to be recovered

Housing, renting in Brussels, cohabitation (Brussels), rental guarantee

question: I'm renting a caravan in a campsite for the year. What rules apply to my lease in Wallonia?
answer: If this is not the case, you must indicate in the lease that you occupy the dwelling (you are the occupier), and that you establish your main residence there. 1. The caravan serves as the main residence of the tenant. This is the case if: you actually live in the dwelling; it is your main dwelling; you are the one who signed the lease. If this is not the case, you must indicate in the lease that you are the owner of the dwelling.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Scope

question: The company asks me to leave, how can I contest in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. If you wish to contest: 1. Check that the legal conditions for the termination of the lease are met. Did the SLSP send its leave on time and is the reason for the leave indicated? Did the SLSP send the leave by registered mail? For more information, see 'How can the company terminate the social housing lease?' 2. Talk to your SLSP. Contact the SLSP and express your point of view. The SLSP may not have known all the useful elements to make its decision.
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Social housing (Wallonia), Duration and end of lease

question: Can I go abroad during my internship?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM); or your paying body (union or CAPAC <unk> Auxiliary Fund for the Payment of Unemployment Allowances). Yes, but it is possible that your internship will be extended. During your internship, you must be available for the labour market in Belgium, respond to job offers and calls from the offer (or Act); and from the ONEM; you must be able to go abroad without your internship being extended, you must apply for authorization to the director of the unemployment office. Before leaving, you must submit the form 36.5.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment and first professional experience

question: I'm unemployed and I live in a roommate. Am I entitled to unemployment if I live in a roommate?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were previously self-employed, subject to certain conditions. Civil servants have a separate scheme.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: Is a foreign child entitled to family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: A child is entitled to family allowances in Wallonia if: he is Belgian, or a foreigner legally resident in Belgium, or born to two stateless parents; and is domiciled in Wallonia (i.e. not in Flanders, nor in Brussels, nor in the German-speaking Community), or actually resides there. If at least one of the parents is European, the child does not have to meet these conditions.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born from 1 January 2020 (Wallonia), Applying for and receiving family allowances (Wallonia)

question: I'm becoming a legal cohabitant. What steps do we need to take to become a legal cohabitant?
answer: You must go to the municipality of your common residence, to the population service. You fill in a form in which you indicate: the date of the declaration; your names, surnames, place and date of birth and signatures; the address of your common residence; the mention of your willingness to cohabitate legally; the mention of what you have become aware of the legal rules relating to legal cohabitation; the mention of any agreement you have concluded to specify the consequences of your common legal cohabitation. The notary will give you an acknowledgement of receipt of this request. The price of the declaration varies according to the municipalities, but it usually revolves around marriage.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: I am in Belgium. How can I apply for family reunification with a foreigner (non-EU) authorised to stay in Belgium?
answer: Before going any further, please note that you can only apply for family reunification from Belgium in a limited number of situations. If your card is valid, you will receive a certificate of registration from the Office. In principle, you have 9 months to make a decision. This period can then be extended 2 times for a period of 3 months. In most situations, the procedure and requirements for applying for residence in Belgium are different: if you are a tourist, you will be granted a residence permit and if you are a spouse of a foreigner, you will receive a temporary residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: Does the unofficial tenancy automatically end when it expires in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. If your rental lease is also a main residence lease: It does not end automatically. The tenants or the landlord must send their notice so that the lease ends: the tenant (s) must send their notice at least 3 months before the due date. The landlord must send their notice: 3 months before the due date for a short-term lease; 6 months before the due date for a nine-year or more lease.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Duration and end of co-location, Duration and end of the unofficial co-location lease (Co-location Brussels)

question: How many days of leave am I entitled to if I work part-time?
answer: Before going any further, this file contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the Social Inspectorate of the ONSS (National Social Security Office) for the social security of part-time workers; your union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. You are entitled to a number of holidays proportional to your working time. This must correspond to 4 weeks of legal holidays in your part-time work schedule. For example, if you have worked with your employer for more than 20 weeks, you are entitled to 20 days of unpaid leave per year.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights to social security as a part-time worker

question: Can the worker apply for adapted work or other work after the assessment of incapacity?
answer: Before going any further Since November 28, 2022, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure has been amended. Yes. The worker can ask the occupational physician to examine whether an adapted work or other work is accounted for by his state of health. He can ask: during the evaluation of incapacity; or after the evaluation of incapacity. If the worker has received the agreement of the occupational physician after the evaluation of incapacity, he has 7 calendar days after receiving the decision of the worker to ask him. He must justify his position and send it by letter to the occupational physician and his employer.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: My doctor committed a medical error, how can I claim compensation in court?
answer: If you are not satisfied with the judge's decision, you can appeal to the Court of First Instance. If your decision comes from the Court of First Instance, you must appeal to the Court of Appeal. Your case will be seen by another judge. If you find that this second judge has misapplied the law, you can go to the Court of Appeal. If the Court of Appeal finds that the judge has misapplied the law, you can go to the Court of Appeal.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, medical failure

question: What fees do I have to pay if I rent a boat in Wallonia?
answer: For example, your lease may stipulate that you pay for the maintenance of the garden, the salary of the concierge, the costs of cleaning the common areas, etc. Attention! If your lease is a main residence lease and a student lease, 2 clauses are prohibited: The clause that sets out the rules; The lease must be paid by the tenant; The lease must be paid by the tenant; The lease must be paid by the tenant; The lease must be paid by the tenant; The lease must be paid by the tenant; The lease must be paid by the tenant.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Charges

question: Can I ask for a reduction in the rent during the lease in Wallonia?
answer: Examples: If the end of a current three-year lease is December 31st, the rent revision can be requested between April 1st and June 30th. If the end of a current three-year lease is January 31st, the rent revision can be requested between May 1st and July 31st and so on. If your landlord agrees to the amount of the rent revision, the new rent revision will be effective on the 1st day of the next three-year lease. If your landlord does not agree, you can apply to the justice of the peace as soon as the lease expires on the 3rd day of the next three-year lease.
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent revision

question: In what cases can you ask the CPAS to waive social assistance?
answer: The CPAS may decide to waive recovery (or to apply lower amounts than those provided for by the scale) for "reasons of equity", i.e. taking into account the particular situation of the family. This is why it must carry out a social inquiry before receiving the aid. The CPAS may freely decide whether there are reasons of equity. For example, the CPAS could waive recovery if: a family is heavily indebted or has to bear significant burdens; family relationships are at risk of being seriously disturbed;
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid

Family, maintenance obligations, maintenance obligations (family solidarity), with a request for intervention from the CPAS, recovery from maintenance debtors of the aid granted (retrospective appeal)

question: I've been summoned to court. What if my opponent doesn't respect the court's decision?
answer: You can start by forcing your opponent to comply with the court decision. You can start by forcing your opponent to comply with the court decision (or the conciliation minutes), by registered mail. You inform him that if he does not comply within 8 or 15 days (or more), you will demand the enforcement of the decision. If, after this period, your opponent still does not comply with the decision, you can proceed with the enforcement of the decision. You must be able to apply for the enforcement of the decision.
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, The court's decision

question: Does the amount of family allowances increase with the age of the children in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since 1 January 2019, and since 1 January 2020.For more information, see the sheet <unk>What is changing with the reform of family allowances?<unk> Yes, for 3 reasons. There are age supplements, which increase the amount of family allowances. These supplements apply for the age groups of 6 to 11 years, 12 to 17 years and 18 to 24 years. For more information, see the FAMIWAL website. The amount of family allowances is indexed.
topics: Social protection, Family allowances and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born before 1 January 2020 (Wallonia)

question: I am an employee. When should I inform my employer that I am pregnant?
answer: Before going any further, note that the rules are not exactly the same for salaried workers, self-employed workers and civil servants. In principle, you must inform your employer of your pregnancy as soon as you know that you are pregnant. In order to be entitled to maternity leave, you must submit to your employer, no later than 7 weeks before the expected date of delivery (9 weeks if you are expecting twins or triplets) a medical certificate indicating the exact date of delivery.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, During pregnancy

question: How much does my property or personal administrator cost me?
answer: If your administrator is a professional, for example a lawyer, his remuneration consists of 3 positions: His ordinary remuneration: a maximum of 3% of your annual net income (pensions, rent received, unemployment benefits, social integration income (RIS), interest on your bank accounts, tax refund, etc.) His expenses are: couriers, mail, arrests, photocopies, etc. Generally, the justice of the peace distributes one-third of these expenses correctly.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), The task of the administrator of property and or of the person

question: Can I be asked to reimburse the allowance for helping elderly people in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, if you have received an elderly care allowance (APA) to which you were not entitled. This is called the repayment of the Hindu (what you received in excess). After these deadlines, the APA service of your mutual can recover what it paid you undue. The APA service of your mutual can go back: 2 years (we count the 2 years from the payment of the Hindu) ; 5 years if you have made fraud or false statements (for example; you did not declare income, or you did not say that you live with another person, etc.).

question: End of maintaining the observation before the end of the fixed period?

question: I'm filing a lawsuit, what's the risk if I don't comply with the decision?
answer: Your opponent can force you to enforce the decision. If the decision sentences you to pay a sum of money, you may be visited by a bailiff for seizure. Indeed, your opponent can ask for the enforcement of the decision by means of seizure (movable seizure, real estate seizure, seizure of judgment). If the decision sentences you to do something, there are other modes of enforcement depending on the procedures referred to (especially the penalty). You must pay the enforcement fees, which increases your debt.

answer: Before going any further, this sheet is about disability benefits and disability benefits. For more information, see our sheet "I'm a salaried worker: how much do I get paid if I get sick?" During pre-trial detention, you keep your mutual benefits, regardless of your family situation. The amount of benefits does not change. During incarceration, the mutual does not pay you pre-trial benefits if you: actually live with your parents in prison; or are able to: leave leave or leave prison; you keep your mutual benefits if you work outside prison with another person. question: I'm renting social housing. Who has to repair the acts of vandalism committed in the corridors of the building in Wallonia?
answer: However, if you are the cause of the damage (or one of your guests), you must pay for the repair. For example, if during a fight a tenant breaks the window of the front door, the tenant or tenants who are the cause of the fight must pay for the repair. On the other hand, if the front door of the building was broken as a result of a robbery, the repair is the responsibility of the SLSP. If unknown people come to clean the walls, the SLSP must pay for the repair itself.

question: Can the landlord increase my charges in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. The answer is different depending on whether you pay your expenses in the form of a lump sum or a provision. The change is only valid for the landlord.

question: Who is a "long-term resident third-country national"?
answer: Long-term resident third-country nationals are non-European foreigners who have resided for a certain period of time in a Member State of the European Union. Specifically, if you have resided legally and continuously for at least 5 years in a country of the European Union, you can apply for recognition of long-term resident status. Obtaining long-term resident status also allows you to access the employment market, activate a family contract, etc.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, long-term resident

question: How to file an appeal against the decision of a social security body?
answer: To challenge the decision of a social security body (ONEM, CPAS, mutual, etc.), you must file an appeal with the labour court within 3 months of the decision. The 3 months are calculated from the date of notification of the decision, i.e. from the date on which you received the decision by mail (in principle, the day after the day of dispatch); from the date of the notice left by the invoice, if you were absent; from the date on which the accused came to drop off the registered mail at your home; from the date on which you signed the receipt of the decision, if you received the decision from the social security body.
topics: Social protection, challenging a social security decision

question: My dwelling has been declared uninhabitable because of the unsanitary conditions. My dwelling has been declared uninhabitable because of the unsanitary conditions. Should I be relocated to Wallonia?
answer: When a mayor issues an uninhabitable order on the basis of a health investigation, he must verify whether there is a solution to resettlement in public housing on the territory of the municipality. Attention, the mayor must seek resettlement only if the following three conditions are met: The mayor (or the municipality) has issued a ban on resettlement on the basis of an uninhabitable order; The ban on resettlement is not caused by overcrowding of housing; The deadline between the provision of housing by the Resettlement Society and the date of resettlement does not allow the occupants to find resettlement.
topics: Housing, unsanitary conditions in Wallonia, my house has been declared uninhabitable.

question: What is human trafficking?
answer: This file was updated more than a year ago. Trafficking in human beings covers a multitude of situations of exploitation, including of foreigners, in various sectors. It is punishable under Belgian law. Persons who: recruit, transport, transfer, host, host, or transfer the control they exercise over a person, with the aim of committing against them offences relating to the corruption of youth and prostitution, the exploitation of begging, the exploitation of human dignity, the abduction of human beings, or the commission of a crime or misdemeanour.

question: My landlord demands the down payment. Do I have to pay in Wallonia?
answer: No. The mortgage advance can never be claimed from you, even if the contract provides for it. This is an imperative rule. This does not prevent you from benefiting from the reduction in the mortgage advance to which you are entitled when you meet certain conditions relating to your family situation. This is the case, for example, if you have a disabled person or at least 2 children, 1 of whom is dependent.

question: I have a PEB F or G certificate. Can my rent be indexed in Brussels?
answer: No. Your landlord cannot index your rent if: your your PEB certificate is F or G; and the anniversary date of your lease is between October 14, 2022 and October 13, 2023. Attention, the anniversary date of the lease is the annual date of the beginning of your lease, that is, the date indicated in the lease agreement on the beginning of the lease. In general, the date of the beginning of the lease indicated in the lease agreement corresponds to the date you entered the apartment. Attention, your landlord could index your rent before October 14, 2022 even if you had a PEB or F G certificate. But this indexation is no longer valid.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I'm self-employed. I'm giving birth earlier or later than expected. What are the consequences on my maternity leave if I'm self-employed?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The self-employed regime applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For ease of use, here we use the term "self-employed woman" in the broad sense, encompassing these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the self-employed regime.

question: I'm moving in. How's the home inspection going?
answer: Each municipality checks the presence or absence of people in its territory. The police are responsible for making these checks. A neighbourhood agent comes to the declared address and checks whether the name of the inhabitants corresponds to the declarations. He fills out an inquiry sheet. You will find an example of an inquiry report in the standard documents. Several criteria have been put in place by the municipalities to assess whether a person actually lives in a place: the person has been registered on the spot; the person lives at that address for most of the year; the person joins the municipality after work.
