question: Can my employer fire me whenever he wants?
answer: Yes, but he must comply with certain rules, including the notice and compensation rules. 1. Your employer must give you notice: he must warn you in advance of his willingness to terminate the employment contract. During the notice period, you continue to work and be paid normally. 2. If your employer does not comply with the notice, or if he wants to give you a shorter notice than you are entitled to, he must pay you a compensation allowance. 3. Your employer must comply with our special rules; if you are protected against dismissal; 4. Some workers are protected against dismissal, for example: foreign delegates; pregnant workers, on maternity leave or on union leave. For more information, see our website.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, dismissal

question: I'm a parent. How can I determine the amount of the alimony?
answer: The law does not impose any method of calculation. It provides that each of the parents must contribute according to their financial capabilities. To assess the financial capabilities of the parents, the whole is taken into account: net income (e.g. salary, rent, share income, etc.); benefits in kind (e.g. company car); holiday allowance; allowances; end-of-year bonuses, etc.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: How can society end the lease of social housing in Wallonia?
answer: For contracts signed from 1 January 2008 onwards, the SLSP can terminate the lease in several situations: the lease expires on 1 January following the expiry of a three-year period or on the expiry of the lease, with a notice period of 6 months.Only if: The tenant is the owner or usufructuary of a dwelling, except if the dwelling is recognized as unsuitable or unsuitable or if a social housing operator provides the means to rent the dwelling.For travel where a member of the SLSP is over 70 years of age or disabled or where the tenant has rented a dwelling designed specifically for students in these categories, the SLSP must always comply with the conditions of the lease.
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Social housing (Wallonia), Duration and end of lease

question: Who exercises my patient rights if I am no longer able?
answer: If you are unable to exercise your patient rights, different people intervene to exercise them for you, in the following order of priority: Your patient rights representative (or attorney), if you had appointed one when you were still able to express your will. The person's administrator if you have one, and if the order provides that the administrator exercises the patient's rights. Consult the protection order issued by the justice of the peace. The person's administrator can never consent to or request certain acts for you, including sterilization, euthanasia or pregnancy interruption.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, the rights of the incapacitated adult patient

question: Who can dispute paternity or maternity and within what time?
answer: The child can act between the ages of 12 and 22, or for one year after discovering that the person with whom a filiation bond is established is not his mother or his biological father. Attention, the Constitutional Court considers that this one-year deadline does not respect the right to private and family life of the child. The child, even if he has reached the age of majority, can therefore contest the filiation bond, even if he has known for more than one year that it does not correspond to the biological bond.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Disputing a parent-child bond

question: I live alone with the children, am I entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Wallonia?
answer: Only if your income is less than a certain amount. This supplement is added to your social supplement if you are a single parent. Monthly amounts of the social supplement and the "single parent" supplement (Wallonia) indexed as of 1 December 2022 For a single parent household with gross annual income of less than 31.603,68 EUR 64,44 EUR per child + 23,43 EUR = 87,87 EUR per child between 31.603,68 EUR and 29.9 EUR per child + 11,72 EUR = 51,01 EUR per child. If you are a single parent, your income is taken into account.
topics: Social protection, Family allowances and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born from 1 January 2020 (Wallonia), Amount and supplements of family allowances (Wallonia)

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am a foreigner. Is the recognition of a child the same for a Belgian or for a foreigner?
answer: In theory, yes. The fact that the child, the father or the mother is Belgian or foreign makes no difference. The declaration of recognition and the recognition usually take place at the same time if: you and your partner are Belgian or you and your partner were born in Belgium or you and your partner are foreigners with a permanent residence permit. You receive an acknowledgement of receipt. The civil status officer (OEC) draws up a declaration of recognition.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I have a PEB certificate A, B, C or D. How do I calculate the indexation of my rent in Brussels?
answer: From 14 October 2022 to 13 October 2023, there are temporary rules on rent indexation in Brussels depending on the PEB certificate. Your rent indexation is calculated according to the legal formula. See the sheet "How to calculate my rent indexation (Brussels) " The amount of indexation does not vary according to your PEB certificate. The amount of indexation varies only if you have a PEB E certificate. For more information, see our sheet "I have a PEB E certificate How to calculate my rent indexation (Brussels) "

question: I'm a self-employed worker. What steps should I take with the mutual before the birth of my child?
answer: Before going any further, your mutual is here to inform you. Contact it. This sheet only gives some general information. For more details about sickness and disability insurance, see the section "Sickness and disability for work". First of all, make sure that you are: affiliated with a mutual; and in order of contributions. Indeed, pregnancy and childbirth involve quite a lot of medical expenses (especially for gynecological examinations, childbirth, prenatal and postnatal kinesiology sessions, consultations with a midwife, hospitalization, pediatric care, etc.).

question: Am I still entitled to family allowance if I work as a student?
answer: 1. Before the age of 18 You keep your family allowances unconditionally until 31 August of the year you turn 18. No matter how many hours you work or how much you earn. 2. Between the ages of 18 and 25 It depends on the region where you are domiciled. If you are domiciled in the Walloon Region: you keep your family allowances if you do not work more than 600 hours per calendar year (from January to December) If you work more than 600 hours per calendar year, you are entitled to your family allowances.
topics: Work, salaried work, student work

question: I am married. My spouse receives an inheritance. Do I also benefit from it?
answer: No. Regardless of your marital status, if one of you inherits, his inheritance belongs to him alone. He has no obligation to share it with you. Similarly, if one is in debt, one cannot seize the property inherited by the other to pay these debts. If the property inherited provides income (for example, if it is an apartment that is rented), the fate of this income depends on your marital status: If you are married under the community of property system, the rent received in the common heritage belongs to both of you.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: If I'm in debt, does that count for legal aid?
answer: Yes. If you are no longer able to cope with your financial deadlines, it is possible to ask the Legal Aid Bureau (LAB) to take this into account. If you wish to introduce a collective debt settlement procedure, the LAB does not examine your income or your means of livelihood or your debt. For more information, see the question "Am I entitled to legal aid if I am over-indebted?" If you want the assistance of a lawyer for your other procedures, you must prove exceptional debt: your debt must be extraordinary; and you must actually repay your debts to your creditors every month.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Income-related situations

question: I ⁇ m going back to work after a period of disability, how is it going?
answer: As soon as your disability ends, you must return to work. 1. If the employer does not offer you the same working conditions: You must return to work adapted to your disability as a result of the reintegration procedure. 2. For more information, please refer to our disability certificate under the heading "Reintegration certificate or Your employer's behaviour is considered to be a breach of disability".
topics: Work, Sickness - incapacity for work, Resumption of work after incapacity

question: If I am a Belgian at the time of his birth, does my child automatically receive Belgian nationality?
answer: Yes, but only in certain cases. If you are a Belgian at the time of your child's birth, your child automatically receives Belgian nationality in three cases: your child was born in Belgium; your child was born abroad but you were born in Belgium; your child was born abroad and has no other nationality; in this case, there is no special administrative procedure. Your child receives Belgian nationality and the municipality registers it in the population register.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, minors (under 18 years of age)

question: I receive confidences as a professional, can I be accused of not assisting anyone in danger if I remain silent?
answer: It's rare, because the conditions are very strict. Many professionals are afraid of being accused of not assisting a person in danger if they don't report certain information. But your obligation is to help. And you can help without lifting the confidentiality, other than by reporting. First consider the alternatives to lifting the confidentiality: anything you can do to help the person. Even if this help is inadequate or ineffective, it doesn't matter. If you try to help the person, you can't be prosecuted for not assisting a person in danger.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: Who can apply for an observation measure except in emergencies?
answer: The request must be made by submitting a request to the registry of the justice of the peace of the place where the person is. The submission of this request is signed. You will find a request model in the free "documents-types" section. This request must be accompanied by a circumcised medical report dated less than 15 days.
topics: Privacy, personal protection measures, protection of the mentally ill, treatment in a hospital environment

question: What if we agree on some things but not on everything?
answer: If you do not agree on all aspects, the mediator may draw up a partial agreement. For example, you agree on all aspects concerning the children but not on the fate of the house. You draw up an agreement which takes up all the points relating to the children and leaves the rest pending. If you want the other points to be settled, you will have to initiate proceedings before the family court. During this procedure, you present your agreement to the family judge and ask him to settle only the outstanding points.
topics: Family, Resolving a family conflict, Finding an amicable solution, Family mediation

question: Where should I submit my application for elderly care allowance in Brussels?
answer: You must submit your application for the elderly care allowance (APA) on the Iriscare website - Service APA; or via a paper form. If you need help with your application, you can contact: your mutual; your municipality; your CPAS; your nursing home; a relative. For more information, see the Iriscare website.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Brussels

question: Does the rental guarantee paid in cash generate interest for the tenant in Wallonia?
answer: Yes The rental guarantee which is most often a sum of money generates interest, even if it has been paid in cash, hand-to-hand. The Court of Cassation considers that in the case of a lease for the main residence, a rental guarantee which has been paid hand-to-hand to the landlord produces interest for the tenant. The interest is capitalized, i.e. it is added to the capital. The capital and interest are generated as a result of the additional financial interest. The landlord must place the sum of money in an individualized account in the name of the tenant with a bank if this sum of money has been paid to him: cash, i.e. hand-to-hand; or paid to his account.
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rental guarantee, Definition and operation of the rental guarantee

question: Can I resume part-time work with the permission of the mutual?
answer: Yes. If you resume work with the authorization of the medical advisor of the mutual, you are said to be partially disabled. This is what many call the medical half-time. But this is not necessarily a half-time resumption. You can resume work unless you are part-time or more. The mutual continues to pay you the allowance, but it deducts part of the amount of your part-time remuneration (part of the remuneration is immunized; therefore not deducted from the allowance). This is called the rules of authorization between remuneration and mutual allowances. For more information, see our website.
topics: Work, Illness - incapacity for work, Resumption of adapted work during incapacity, Resumption of partial work with the authorization of the mutual (<unk> half-time medical <unk>)

question: What are the charges in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. The charges correspond to actual expenses, unless they are fixed in the lease agreement or an endorsement. The amount of the charges claimed from you must correspond to reality, i.e. what you actually consumed; the costs actually paid by the landlord.
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Charges, Distribution of charges

question: Is my lease a lease for a main residence in Wallonia?
answer: Your lease is a principal residence lease if: 1. The rented dwelling serves as your principal residence. This is the case if: you actually live in the dwelling; it is your principal residence; you are the one who signed the lease. If this is not the case, you must indicate in the lease that you occupy the dwelling (you are the occupier), and that you establish your principal residence there. For example, your parents signed the lease at your place.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Scope

question: The parents are not married together. What steps should I take to be recognized as the father of my child if neither the mother nor I are married?
answer: If the declaration is made during pregnancy, you must provide a certificate issued by a doctor or midwife confirming the pregnancy. Some people must give their consent for this recognition. If your child is a minor: if he is between 0 and 12 years old, you need the consent of his mother; if he is between 12 and 18 years old, you need the consent of his mother and the consent of the child.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish a father-child bond, Neither parent is married

question: What is a summary order to pay procedure?
answer: The summary order for payment procedure is a simplified procedure aimed at the payment of a debt. This procedure takes place before the justice of the peace. It allows the creditor to be paid more quickly than by an ordinary procedure. Several conditions must be fulfilled in order to be able to follow this procedure: the request must be within the jurisdiction of the justice of the peace, and the debtor must be exempt from paying a sum of money.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts

question: I am applying for family reunification. What are the conditions for family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) on a limited stay?
answer: 1) The grouping must have: sufficient housing to receive the family member or members. This is proven by: a registered lease agreement or a title deed. Health insurance covering the risks in Belgium for himself and his family members. This can be proven by: a nominative certificate from the mutual confirming the possibility of affiliating the family members as soon as they arrive on Belgian territory; or a private insurance contract signed by a stable, regular and sufficient means of subsistence; to provide for his own and his family's needs, and to avoid becoming a burden for the public.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I'm getting married. If I get divorced, will I lose my residence card?
answer: This is the case if: you are divorced; or you are divorced; or your marriage is annulled (e.g. in the case of an arranged marriage); or your spouse or cohabitant (e.g. a Belgian or European) has died; or there is no longer a common residence (e.g. if your spouse or cohabitant is a Belgian or European); The withdrawal of your residence permit is not automatic.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and family, After getting married in Belgium
Foreigners, Foreigners and family, Marriage

question: Can I ask for a change in a decision on my children's accommodation?
answer: Yes. If your situation or that of your children changes, you can always: find an amicable agreement to change the terms of accommodation of the children (the agreement can be made between you or through the help of a family mediator), or; go to the family court to ask the judge to decide. It does not matter the reasons: the old organization no longer suits you, or it is no longer possible (for example because you are moving, the children change schools, etc.), as long as your children are minors, you can re-interview the parent of the family by a simple written request or by filing conclusions at the registry.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Children's accommodation, After an agreement or a judgment

question: Do I need a rental permit for a flat in Wallonia?
answer: In principle, yes. The rental permit is mandatory for "collective housing" that: is rented as a main residence, or is occupied by students; and there is only one rental contract for all tenants. For example: if Mrs. X rents the two rooms on the ground floor to Mrs. A and Mrs. B, she does not need a rental permit. If Mrs. A rents a house to a family of three and Mrs. B rents a house to a family of two, Mrs. X must also apply for a rental permit.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Rental permit

question: How do I file my appeal with the Council for Foreigners' Disputes (CCE)?
answer: To file an appeal before the CCE, you need the assistance of a lawyer specializing in foreign law. You will find the list of lawyers specializing in foreign law on the website advocats.be. See also if you are entitled to free legal aid. The procedure is written. You must present your arguments in writing. You must file your appeal by request. This request must be very complete; explain all the facts and the arguments you invoke.
topics: Foreigners, International protection (formerly called asylum), Challenging a decision on international protection (formerly called asylum)

question: Who can be appointed as administrator of property and or of the person?
answer: As the administrator of the protected person, the justice of the peace may appoint: the parent (s) of the protected person (they can all be appointed together); the spouse, the legal cohabitant or the partner of the protected person; a close family member of the protected person; a person who accompanies the protected person; a close family member of the protected person; if the protected person has no trusted person, the justice of the peace must appoint two administrators: one for the person and one for the person.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)

question: After how long does my debt to my friend expire?
answer: For the loan agreement between individuals, there are two statute of limitations: 10 years for the payment of the capital. 5 years for the payment of interest and fees. 5 years for the payment of interest and fees. 5 years for the payment of interest and fees. 5 years for the payment of interest and fees. 5 years for the payment of interest and fees.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a loan between individuals

question: I'm a parent. Do I still have to pay alimony if I'm no longer in contact with my children?
answer: Yes. There is no link between the payment of alimony and the accommodation of the children. You must pay alimony until your child becomes independent. Autonomy is generally achieved when the young person has a diploma or has completed training giving him access to the labour market. The child's disrespectful behaviour towards his parents does not prevent the payment of alimony. In case of conflict, you can contact the family judge.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: Am I entitled to more days off because I have a child?
answer: No. The days of leave to which you are entitled are calculated on the basis of the days of work you have worked during the previous year. It does not matter whether you have children or not. However, your employer must give you your leave as a priority during the school holidays, if your children are of school age. In addition, you are entitled to certain days of leave due to circumstances, especially at the time of your child's marriage, or when one of your children dies.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, leave for young parents

question: Can the landlord claim rental damage after I leave for Wallonia?
answer: The landlord can in principle claim from you the payment of the rental damage that occurred during your occupation (or that is due to your occupation). So you have to establish a contradictory exit status when you leave the apartment. If there was no exit status, you will not be able to prove that the damage claimed by the landlord occurred after your departure. In practice, your landlord can therefore claim from you the rental damage that occurred after your departure, if there is no exit status.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Departure and arrival of a roommate

question: What is the role of the CPAS in the event of expulsion?
answer: The CPAS is notified only if: the eviction concerns a building rented through a main residence lease (or a commercial lease) and this building is the tenant's home or residence. The objective is to enable the CPAS to help the tenant in the procedures he will undertake (justice; amicable settlement, relocation, financial assistance, etc.).
topics: Housing, eviction

question: I'm renting a cottage. What are the criteria for granting a rental permit in Wallonia?
answer: To obtain the rental permit, small individual dwellings (less than 28 m2) and collective dwellings must: comply with the health criteria provided for in the Walloon Housing Code (regional rules); comply with the minimum area criteria; be equipped with fire detectors; comply with the regulations of your municipality on health and fire safety; For more information, contact your municipal administration. To comply with the criteria for the inviolability of the dwelling and the respect for privacy (closing doors, bells and fire extinguishers); comply with the energy efficiency standards set by the Walloon Government.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting an apartment (Wallonia), Do I need a rental permit in Wallonia

question: What is the immediate termination of the employment contract for serious misconduct?
answer: In practice, it is more often invoked by the employer, because it is risky and difficult to prove for the worker. It can be invoked in all types of employment contracts: CDI, CDD, replacement contract, internship contract, etc. It is necessary to be very careful before invoking serious fault, because there are very specific rules to be observed: serious fault, delay of proof, etc.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, immediate termination for serious misconduct

question: Can my landlord charge me all the expenses he wants in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further Since 1 January 2018, there is a student lease in Brussels, with specific rules for terminating the lease. The rules of the student lease apply to your lease only if: the accommodation is located in the Brussels area; you have signed or renewed your contract after 1 January 2018; you have explicitly chosen to use the student lease. No. As part of a student lease, the landlord cannot charge the tenant: the real estate advance and the real estate agency fees if it is provided for in the lease.
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, Kot (Brussels), student lease

question: I've been summoned to court. Do I have to hire a lawyer?
answer: No, you are not obliged to hire a lawyer, except: when the law requires you to do so - it is quite rare - (for example, minors must be represented by a lawyer); when the judge requires you to do so: he can do so if he believes that the context makes the discussion impossible. However, in some proceedings, it is advisable to get insurance from a lawyer: either because the case is complicated at the legal level; or because the procedure is special; or because you do not have to go in person to the hearings; or because the issue of the dispute (the amount of the conflict) is important.
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Summoning to hearing

question: Can I not pay my last two months' rent to make sure I get my rent guarantee back in Wallonia?
answer: No. These are two different things. You can't even decide not to pay the last two months of rent because you've paid a lease guarantee. You also can't terminate the lease two months before maturity on that basis alone. On the other hand, if your landlord agrees, there's no problem. In that case, put your agreement in writing.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Guarantee of the rental
Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rental guarantee, Return of the rental guarantee

question: Can the break-up procedure for medical force majeure be introduced several times?
answer: Before going any further Since November 28, 2022, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure has been amended. Yes. If the occupational doctor decides that the worker is temporarily unable to work during the 1st procedure for medical force majeure, the worker or the employer may reintroduce a new procedure. They must wait before reintroducing the procedure. The worker must have been unable to work for at least 9 months without interruption.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am a foreigner. Can the municipality refuse me to recognize my child?
answer: Yes The civil registry officer (OEC) can refuse to recognize paternity if he suspects fraud. He can refuse to recognize paternity if there are serious indications that: you do not have a parent-child relationship with the child; or your intentions are solely to obtain a file for your right of residence, that of the child or the mother. For example, you have already recognized a child with another woman in "illegal residence" and you do not live with the child you want to recognize. In this case, the OEC can find out that your intentions are not to introduce this child but to the mother of the child.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I'm getting married. Under what conditions can I get married in Belgium?
answer: If you are a Belgian, to be able to marry: You must be of legal age, i.e. 18 years or older. Exception: the family court may allow a minor to marry if: the parents agree; and serious circumstances justify the request (e.g. having one or more children together and a real plan of living together). You must be able to give your consent to the marriage. Certain people, e.g. people with mental illness, or people placed under the administration of the person, are not considered to be able to give their consent; You cannot already be married or marry again, the previous marriage must no longer be due to an annulment, divorce or death.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: What is the administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)?
answer: For example, it can be useful for a person with a physical or mental disability, temporary or permanent, for a person in a coma or following a stroke. This measure is ordered by the justice of the peace. It is then said of the person who is protected or ordered. Due to his state of health, this measure protects the person against himself and others. For example, it can be useful for a person with a physical or mental disability, temporary or permanent disability, for a person in a coma or following a stroke.

question: I'm married. Do I have to pay my spouse's work debts?
answer: It depends on your marital status. You have to refer to your marriage contract. If you are married under the community of property regime (this is the regime that applies if you have not signed a marriage contract), your spouse's professional debts can be recovered on his own property AND on your common property. Only your own property is protected. So, if your spouse has taken out a loan for his electric truck, he has to pay it back. If he does not pay, the bank can recover on his own property, but also on your common property (if each of you buys me or during the marriage, etc.).
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I'm unemployed and I'm living in a couple. I'm separating or divorcing, what does that change for my unemployment benefits?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were employed before becoming self-employed, subject to certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules. For more information, please contact the National Employment Office (ONEM) or your payment agency (Union or CAPAC; Auxiliary Payment Fund; Unemployment Benefits). You must also believe in your new situation after separation. If you are an unemployed couple, you must receive unemployment benefits at the reduced rate.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: I'm unemployed and I have children. I'm unemployed, am I entitled to the dependent family rate, if I have children in alternating accommodation?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, except if they were wage-earners before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules.

question: I live in a roommate, how can I keep my rate isolated for the RIS?
answer: If you live under the same roof as one or more other people, you are considered to be living under the same roof. If you live under the same roof, you are considered to be living under the same roof. If you live under the same roof, you are considered to be living under the same roof. If you live under the same roof, you are considered to be living under the same roof. If you live under the same roof, you are considered to be living under the same roof.

question: Can my landlord terminate my lease if I no longer pay my rent?
answer: Yes, but he has to ask the landlord to terminate the contract. The owner of your apartment asks the judge to terminate the contract. This is the termination of the lease. If you do not respond to this termination, the landlord can ask the judge to order you to pay the rent and to terminate the lease. Sometimes, there is a conciliation attempt, the justice of the peace tries to find an agreement between the landlord and the tenant.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a lease agreement

question: I'm renting out an apartment or a house. What does my fire insurance cover in Wallonia?
answer: The following risks are covered unless your insurance contract excludes them: fire; damage caused by lightning, explosion, implosion, fall or impact of aircraft or objects. The objects or animals that fall or are projected to fall (e.g. car, pigeons, etc.) are normally also covered by fire insurance: damage caused by bombings, damage caused by labour conflicts, damage caused by storms, damage caused by natural disasters. Other risks are regularly covered: electricity; smoke; water; civil liability.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia), Insurance

question: I'm a salaried worker. I have to be hospitalized after giving birth. What are the consequences on my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see our sheet "What are the conditions under which I am entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. For self-employed women, see our sheet "What are the conditions under which I am entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For self-employed women, see our sheet "What are the conditions under which you are entitled to maternity leave?"

question: What's the difference between the package and the charge provision in Wallonia?
answer: If the amount of the lump sum paid does not correspond to the actual expenses, that's too bad! Each party bears the risk. The landlord cannot ask you to pay a surplus based on the actual expenses consumed. However, you or your landlord can always ask the justice of the peace to modify the lump sum or transform it into multiple expenses.
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Charges, Calculation of charges

question: I am a citizen of a country of the European Union, am I entitled to the RIS?
answer: It depends on your right of residence in Belgium. If you are in Belgium for a stay of less than 3 months, for example as a tourist, you are not entitled to social integration income (RIS). If you come to Belgium for a long stay, it depends on the reason for your stay: you are a job seeker, a beneficiary of sufficient resources or a student: you are entitled to RIS if you meet two conditions: you have an E-card; and you have been in Belgium for more than 3 months. If you are a self-employed person or a CP and you have an E-card, you are entitled to RIS.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Being Belgian or belonging to certain categories of foreigners

question: I have just returned to Belgium, am I entitled to reimbursement of health care?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only some general information. For any details or additional questions, please contact your mutual. Be aware of two things: The rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. The concept of a dependent is not the same in the different social security schemes. For example, a dependent for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent for disability benefits, nor for unemployment. You must register with a mutual as soon as possible.
topics: Social protection, Health and illness, Reimbursement of health care

question: I'm an employee. Do I get paid during my breastfeeding breaks?
answer: Before we go any further, please note that this sheet applies only to salaried workers. The rules are completely different for public sector workers (civil servants). During your breastfeeding breaks you are not paid by your employer. On the other hand, you are entitled to mutual allowances of 82% of your gross hourly earnings. There is no ceiling. To receive these allowances, you must send each month to your mutual, a document completed by your employer, and including the hours or half-hours of breaks you have taken.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, Resumption of work and breastfeeding, Breastfeeding breaks and breastfeeding leave

question: I'm filing a complaint with a mediator. The company is located in Belgium. What mediator for health care (outside the hospital)?
answer: Before you complain to the federal mediation service "Patient's Rights" you must address your complaint to the practitioner and not receive a satisfactory answer. As a patient, you have a number of rights. More information on our sheet "What are my rights as a patient?" If you feel that your rights as a patient are not being respected, you can file a complaint. If it concerns care received within a hospital, you can contact the federal mediation service. If it concerns a practitioner working outside a hospital, you can contact the federal mediation service.
topics: Money, Consumer protection, Resolving a conflict with a company

question: I am married. Does my spouse ⁇ s salary also belong to me?
answer: It depends on your marital status. If you are married under the community of property regime, the salary is a common property. It belongs to both of you. It is in your common property, even if only one of you works. Each of you can make expenses with the common income, without needing the agreement of the other. However, if these expenses are excessive or not related to the expenses of living together, your spouse can ask a judge to order you to repay.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Am I entitled to my survivor's pension if I'm in prison?
answer: You keep your survivor's pension for the first 12 months in prison. After 12 consecutive months in prison, you no longer receive your survivor's pension. You also no longer redeem your survivor's pension after 12 consecutive months in: pre-trial detention; exit permit; prison leave; limited detention. It does not matter if you are an employee or self-employed. You can recover pensions that you did not receive during pre-trial detention, if you prove: you have been acquitted; or there has been a decision of non-prosecution (end of prosecution) or dismissal.
topics: Social protection, detainees and former detainees, pensions and Grapa

question: I'm unemployed and I have children. Will my unemployment benefits increase if I have children?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were previously self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: Am I entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo) if I receive aid from the CPAS?
answer: Yes, in principle. If you are helped by a public social action centre (CPAS), you are in principle entitled to legal aid. What conditions must you meet? You must be entitled to: a social integration income (RIS) based on a decision of the CPAS at the time of applying for legal aid; or recurring social aid (financial or material) to feed you, clothe you, provide you with housing, care for you, help you in case of hospitalization, etc. In these cases, you are presumed not to have sufficient financial resources to pay for a lawyer.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Situations related to a social category

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Can I rent or sell our house without the consent of my legal cohabitant?
answer: No. You can never sell or rent the building that serves as the family residence, without the agreement of the other legal cohabitant. This is a consequence of legal cohabitation: the family housing is protected. No decision can be made by one of the cohabitators, without the agreement of the other, about: the building that houses the family; and the furniture that is in it.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: Real estate agency management fees: do I have to pay them in Wallonia?
answer: No. The fees of a real estate agency cannot be claimed from you. Unless you have asked the real estate agency to intervene yourself. If a clause in your lease stipulates that you must pay the agency fees, it is not taken into account. The clause is said to be "reputedly unwritten".
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Charges, Distribution of charges
Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: Can I be asked to reimburse the family allowances I received in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, in some cases, if you have received family allowances to which you were not entitled. This is called the refund of the lindu; pay what you received in excess. The family allowance fund can recover what it paid you unfairly, that is, what it paid you when you were not entitled to it. Unless the lindu is due to an error of the fund. If it has delayed you because it was mistaken, it cannot ask you to repay the fraud. It can make a new decision to rectify the amount, and then pay the new (although high) amount.

question: What is the Council for Foreigners' Disputes (CCE)?
answer: But before going any further, since 2018 the asylum procedure is called the international protection procedure. The asylum seeker must therefore submit an application for international protection. This procedure can allow him to obtain: refugee status; or subsidiary protection status. But the Council for Foreigners' Disputes (CCE) is an administrative court. It is responsible for examining all appeals against the decisions of the Office for Foreigners (OFE) or the General Commission for Refugees and Refugees (CGRA).
topics: Foreigners, international protection (formerly called asylum)

question: Under what conditions am I entitled to the old-age benefit in Wallonia?
answer: You must be a Belgian or belong to certain categories of foreigners: foreigners legally residing from a country of the European Union; foreigners registered in the population register: regardless of your nationality; stateless persons; refugees; etc. You must be domiciled in Wallonia and you are actually in Belgium. This means that you must live in Belgium. You must meet this condition: at the time of application for the entire period during which you receive the APA. There are exceptions to this condition.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Wallonia

question: We're not married, what will our child's name be if his father recognizes him after he's been declared in the commune?
answer: If the child is recognized after June 1, 2014, he or she must choose the name of the father; if the child is recognized after June 1, 2014, the name of the father; if the name of the mother is followed by the name of the father; if the name of the mother is followed by the name of the mother; if the name of the mother is followed by the name of the father.

question: Does the unofficial tenancy automatically end when it expires in Wallonia?
answer: If your tenancy is also a main residence lease: It does not end automatically. The tenant (s) or the landlord must send their notice for the lease to end: the tenant (s) or the landlord must send their notice at least 3 months before the lease expires. For a short-term lease: 3 months before the lease expires; for a lease of 9 years or more: 6 months before the lease expires. For a long-term lease: 1 month before the lease expires. For a short-term lease: 1 month before the lease expires. For a long-term lease: 1 month before the lease expires.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Duration and end of the colocation, Duration and end of the unofficial colocation lease (Colocation Wallonia)

question: I am a tenant, am I paying an excessive rent in Brussels?
answer: Since 2 December 2021, a rent is presumed to be unfair if: it exceeds 20% of the reference rent; or it does not exceed 20% of the reference rent, and the dwelling has "substantial quality defects". For the time being, there is no list of these "substantial quality defects". But there are examples in the preparatory works of the law: a dwelling without an individual meter for water or for electricity; a dwelling without a private room for the bathroom or toilet; a dwelling without a kitchen equipment; a dwelling without an elevator; etc.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, charges and insurance, Rental, Revision of rent
Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Fixation

question: I'm renting a garage. What are the rules for my lease in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. A garage cannot be used as a dwelling (except in exceptional circumstances). So if you rent a garage alone, the common law rules apply. These protect the tenant very little. On the other hand, if the garage is part of your dwelling, the rules of the housing lease apply (main residence lease, colocation lease, cot lease, etc.).
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Scope

question: I'm getting married. Can I challenge the refusal of my marriage?
answer: Yes. You can appeal to the family court against the decision of the civil registry officer to refuse to celebrate the marriage. This possibility of appeal must appear on the letter informing you of the decision. The terms of the appeal are explained there. You must appeal within one month of the notification of the decision. So do not delay in reacting. If you appeal, the King's Prosecutor already has the decision of the civil registry officer.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: My neighbor's tree bothers me, what are the planting distances?
answer: Each municipality can determine the planting distances for the trees on the properties of its inhabitants. Contact the Espace-verts service of your municipal administration to find out the planting distances. If your municipality does not have specific rules, these rules apply to situations before September 1, 2021: 2 m for trees with a high trunk. 0 m for trees with a low trunk. 0 m if there is a fruit tree.
topics: Accommodation, neighbourhood, plantations

question: What happens if there is no state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: If there is no state of the premises, you are presumed to have received the dwelling in the state in which it is when you return it. This presumption applies even if your lease contains the following clause: "the building is in a good state of maintenance". Such clauses are not valid. This presumption is in your favor as a tenant. If your landlord believes that there is damage, he must prove it. He must demonstrate that the state of the dwelling was different from your entry into the premises.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Location

question: I am expelled. Can the municipality expel me?
answer: Yes, the municipality can delete you from the registry when your address no longer corresponds to reality (main residence). If the municipality knows your new address, it does not delete you. It transfers the information to your new municipality so that you are domiciled there. On the other hand, if your new address is unknown, the municipality can delete you from the registry. In practice, the district registrar conducts his investigation and sends the results to the civil registry officer.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: I'm unemployed and I live in a couple. My partner has a lot of income, am I entitled to unemployment benefits?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM) or your payment agency (union or CAPAC <unk> Auxiliary Fund for the Payment of Unemployment Benefits). Yes, if you meet the conditions to be entitled to unemployment benefits, you are entitled to them if your partner has a lot of income. But don't see unemployment benefits because you live in a couple.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: When is the employer obliged to analyze whether a reintegration plan is possible?
answer: Before going any further, since October 1st, the reintegration pathway procedure has been amended. The new rules apply to: ongoing reintegration pathway procedures; new reintegration pathway procedures. As of November 28th, the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure is no longer done via the reintegration pathway information form. Since October 1st, the employer must analyze whether a reintegration plan is possible.

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. Can I ask my (ex) spouse for financial assistance if we separate?
answer: Yes, if you are married or have been married, you can ask him to pay you alimony. Be careful, we are talking about alimony between ex-spouses and not alimony for children. When you get alimony: either you arrange directly with your ex-spouse (you sign an agreement together); or you ask the family judge (as part of a request for urgent and provisional measures or as part of a divorce proceeding). The judge can grant it provided that the spouse is in a state of debt that the income is not enough for him to meet his basic needs.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: What are the risks if I don't pay for my parking?
answer: If you don't have a parking meter or a timer, or if you've forgotten to put your disk in, you'll be charged a tax or a lump sum fee. This fee is based on the findings of the officers assigned to check the parking meter. You'll be charged a lump sum fee.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to various taxes or fees, parking tax or fee

question: I live in a roommate with a friend, how much will I receive for my income replacement allowance (ARR)?
answer: Living with a friend does not make you lose your right to the income replacement allowance (ARR). On the other hand, the amount of your ARR can change depending on: 1. your family situation The amount of the ARR depends on your family situation. If you live with a friend, you belong to category C: cohabiting with a dependent family (head of household).

question: What is an electronic judicial public sale of movable property?
answer: Before going any further, the sales of seized furniture will soon be able to be sold at auction on the Internet. How to participate in the public auction? Anyone who wants to participate in a judicial auction will have to register on the auction platform. How will the platform be able to be informed? Some sales will also be announced on the platform. The auction platform will soon be able to be operational.

question: When to take stock in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. The state of the premises must be made before entering the dwelling or at the beginning of the rental, but at the latest during the first month of occupancy (within the first 15 days if it is a lease of less than 1 year). The state of the premises must be drawn up at the end of the occupancy: after you have left the dwelling but before you have returned the keys to the landlord. If one of you two refuses to redeem or sign the state of the premises, the other must go to the justice of the peace before handing over the keys!
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Location

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I'm getting married. Can I appeal if my marriage is refused?
answer: Yes. You can file an appeal with the family court. You must file your appeal within the month of notification of the decision. If you file this appeal, the King's Prosecutor already has the decision of the Civil Registry Officer (OEC) and the useful elements of your file, because the Civil Registry Officer sends him these documents in copy. During the procedure, you can add new elements that are beneficial to you, for example if a child is born, etc. During the hearing, the Family Affairs Judge may ask you about your relationship.

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). How many weeks of maternity leave can I take before childbirth (pre-natal leave)?

question: I live in a civil union. What are my partner's rights over our children born in a civil union?
answer: 1. The status of the couple does not change the relationship between the parents and the children. 2. The status of the couple has free consequences on the filiation of the children. If you are in a union, the father must recognize the child, to become his legal father. Specifically, you must make a declaration of paternity: to the municipality or a notary. The mother of the child must agree to this. This recognition can be done before the birth. The law does not impose a deadline for this, it can be done at any time before the birth.

question: How can I check the charges charged to me in Brussels?
answer: If you want to verify the reality of each of the charges, you can ask your landlord to send you proof of each of the charges (such as bills for rent, electricity bills, retail bills, etc.) He must also send you a receipt with the actual charges; the amounts requested must correspond to the actual charges, i.e. the charges you have actually consumed. question: Can I ask for a reduction in the rent during the lease in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. At the end of the three-year term, you can ask your landlord for a rent reduction. Your landlord must accept the rent reduction between the 9th and the 6th month preceding the end of a current three-year term: so be sure to make your request on time. If your landlord does not agree, you can contact the justice of the peace between the 6th and the 3rd month before the expiry of the three-year term. The competent justice of the peace is the one of the place of residence.

question: I'm a self-employed woman. How long is my maternity leave if I'm a self-employed woman?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. As a self-employed woman, you are entitled to a total of 12 weeks of maternity leave, of which: 3 weeks You must pay the following benefits: 1 week just before childbirth, and 2 weeks just after childbirth, 9 weeks optional: 2 weeks before childbirth, 3 weeks before the expected date of childbirth, 36 weeks after the expected date of childbirth, 38 weeks after the expected date of childbirth, etc.

question: Can I make a new consumer credit to repay the ones I already have?
answer: Yes. You can group your loans and make a new loan to repay the ones you already have. Be careful, the grouping of loans is often sold as an advantage because it reduces the amount of your monthly payments. You have to take into account the new duration of your loan and the total amount (principal, interest, fees) that you will have to repay. Smaller monthly payments mean a longer duration and therefore more interest to pay in total.

question: In what cases do I have to sign a PIIS if I receive a RIS?
answer: Yes. 1. The Individualized Social Integration Project (PIIS) is mandatory for all new beneficiaries of a Social Integration Income (RIS). Anyone who has not received a RIS in the last 3 months must sign a PIIS to be entitled to a RIS. 2. Particularity for beneficiaries under 25 years of age. 3. The PIIS is mandatory for them in 3 cases: when the beneficiary has not received a RIS in the last 3 months; when the CPAS accepts that the beneficiary starts, continues or studies full-time; when the beneficiary considers realizing the right to social integration through employment.

question: What resources are taken into consideration for the income replacement allowance (ARR)?
answer: The General Directorate for Disabled Persons (DGPH) takes into account several resources to calculate your income replacement allowance (ARR). The DGPH takes into account: your total taxable net income; the total taxable net income of the person with whom you form a household. That is, your spouse, legal cohabitant, de facto cohabitant and friend who lives with you. For example, it takes into account income from 2 years ago. For example, if you enter your application for ARRGPH, it takes into account income from 2020; If your income from your household has decreased or increased by at least 20% compared to 2 years ago, the DGPH takes into account income from the cadastral index. For example, if you do not receive any income from your household, it takes into account the income from the index.

question: We're not married, what name will my child have if the father doesn't recognize him?

question: I am getting married. What happens if we delay the celebration of my wedding?
answer: You are informed of the decision to postpone the wedding, by registered mail or by direct delivery of the decision. The decision must be substantiated. The civil registry officer who decides to postpone the celebration of your wedding must make a further inquiry within 2 months, from the date you had chosen to celebrate your wedding. This further inquiry allows to verify whether it is a simulated marriage. If the civil registry officer considers it necessary, he can ask the King's Prosecutor of the judicial district in which you want to get married.

question: Can all my income be confiscated if I don't pay my food contribution?
answer: Yes. If you don't pay your alimony, your ex can have your entire income seized. For the payment of alimony obligations, we don't take into account the unaffordability thresholds for other types of debts (i.e. the minimum amount of income that we have to leave you, that we can't seize). This total seizure can apply regardless of your income: - mutual benefits - unemployment allowance - salary - social integration income (RIS), etc. In addition, the alimony creditor (your ex to whom you have to pay the alimony contribution) is paid before your other creditors.

question: Can we separate by mutual agreement without declaring the end of legal cohabitation?
answer: Yes. You don't have to have the permission of the municipality or the judge to live separately. But be careful, as long as you don't make a declaration of termination of legal cohabitation to the municipality, you still have the status of legal cohabitants. The only cases where legal cohabitation ends automatically are marriage or the death of one of you. As long as the legal cohabitation has not ended, you are bound by all the consequences of legal cohabitation (obligation to contribute to the costs of the household, prohibition to sell the housing that serves as family housing, you are heirs of the other, etc.).

question: Can the CPAS force me to ask for help from my maintenance debtors for the RIS?
answer: Yes. The CPAS can force you to ask for help from some of your alimony debtors. The idea is that family solidarity (your relatives) should take precedence over collective solidarity. The CPAS is not obliged: it can force you to ask for help from your alimony debtors, but it should not. Referral to alimony debtors is always optional. The CPAS has discretion. To assess whether a referral to alimony debtors is possible or not, the social worker in charge of the case conducts a social inquiry ("the alimony inquiry") to determine: whether alimony debtors (e.g. parents, children, a spouse, etc.) have the financial capacity to contribute.

question: Which CPAS is competent to help me create my rental guarantee in Wallonia?
answer: The competent CPAS to grant this aid is the CPAS of your place of habitual residence, according to the general principle of competence. So everything depends on when you submit your application. If you submit your application before you move into your new home: you must contact the CPAS of your current place of residence (the one you are leaving). If you submit the application after you move into your new home: you must then contact the CPAS of the municipality where this new home is located. Some CPAS simply declare themselves incompetent and refer the person to their former CPAS, because they are used to considering that it is the CPAS of the place where they leave a guarantee.

question: Can the judge give me a deadline if I don't pay back the money I borrowed?
answer: Yes. If you have not respected your commitment to repay, your friend the lender can sue you (after giving you notice to execute you). In this case, you have received a summons to appear in court. The best reaction is to appear before the judge on the scheduled date and time. You can ask him for payment facilities by explaining your situation. In principle, you cannot force your friend to pay the amount due to you. The judge can grant you moderate payment deadlines, or even suspend payment, even if the debt has been found in an authentic act.

question: How do I read and understand my water bill in Wallonia?
answer: You pay periodically, usually every quarter, a provision for your water consumption. This advance invoice is drawn up on the basis of your past consumption, based on the statements of your meter from the previous year. Once a year, your consumption is the subject of a summary balance. This is the regulation invoice that follows the regulation of your meter. A closing invoice must also be drawn up if you move Your regulation invoice must detail at least: your name and address; the place of supply; a histogram of the consumption with a summary of them (3 years); the minimum meter index; the period of consumption; the old and the new; the amount of the invoice calculated separately by the price of the distribution; the date of the payment; the structure of the TV wall; the cost of the service; the coordinate;

question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. In legal cohabitation, who has to pay for the car loan?

question: What is a single permit for foreigners?

All you have to do is submit a single application for authorization: an application for a work permit; and an application for a residence permit.

question: The child's father and mother live separately. Do the school, the doctor, etc. always have to ask for the consent of both parents if they no longer live together?
answer: No, not necessarily. Each of the parents can go alone to a third party (director of school, teacher, doctor, banker, municipality, etc.) and make a decision that is within the parental authority (enrolling the child in school, having his child treated, etc.), without having to prove that the other has given his consent to this decision. The third party can execute the act requested by only one of the parents, provided that he is not aware of the consent of the other parent. It is said that the third party must be "in good faith". If the third party knows that the other parent does not agree with this act, it is "in bad faith".

question: Do I have to install fire detectors in Wallonia?
