question: I'm separating. What are the consequences of our separation for our children?
answer: Your separation has no impact on your relationship with your children. Of course, you will have to arrange for the accommodation of the children and cover their expenses, but your status as a parent does not decrease. You must always make all the decisions related to your children, and inform each other of these decisions (school, health, leisure, etc.). We are talking about joint parental authority. Only a judge could change this principle. The presence of a stepfather does not change anything: the stepfather has no legal right to your children.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Can I stay in our house if my legal cohabitant dies?
answer: If the deceased legal cohabitant was the sole owner, or if you were both co-owners of your housing: You have the right of use of the legal cohabitant's housing and the furniture that is your heirloom. You can continue to live in this housing, or you can rent it and receive the rent. But you must be careful, if you are a descendant of this deceased person, this protection does not apply to the heirs of the deceased cohabitant (for example, his children) who do not have the right of use of this housing.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: For what problems should a family mediator be called?
answer: All problems affecting the family are handled by the family mediator. He intervenes for example for: questions that arise during a separation or divorce; problems of inheritance; conflicting relationships between parents and children; housing of children after a separation; issues of food contribution; departure from the family; etc. Mediation is often voluntary. You decide yourself to call a mediator and enter into oral mediation. Mediation can begin even if a legal proceeding is underway. If you feel that an agreement is possible, you can ask the judge to suspend the proceeding.
topics: Family, Resolving a family conflict, Finding an amicable solution, Family mediation

question: Am I entitled to a portion of my spouse's pension if we separate?
answer: Before we go any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants.You are therefore entitled to a pension even if you yourself have not reached the retirement age (65 years).But you must meet certain conditions.You must be married (same-sex couples, cousins and cousins are not entitled to it);Your spouse is entitled to a pension (career, age, independent pension, etc.)
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, retirement pension

question: Am I entitled to social integration if I live in a roommate?
answer: Before going any further, these conditions relate to your nationality, your age, your place of residence, your resources, etc. For a more precise answer, see the section "CPAS assistance". No conditions relate to the fact that you live under the same roof, the fact that your income is under the same roof as your CPAS. Yes, if you meet the conditions for granting the right to social integration (not to be confused with the right to social assistance). These conditions relate to your nationality, your age, your place of residence, your resources, etc.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits
Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social integration income (RIS)

question: Who determines if I am able to exercise my patient rights?
answer: Only a professional can determine whether you are capable of exercising your patient rights or not. Several criteria can be taken into account to assess your (in) capacity, such as your ability to make a choice; to understand relevant information; to be aware of the information and appreciate it in your own situation; to consult logically. This assessment is very important.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, the rights of the incapacitated adult patient

question: What is a rental guarantee and what does it cover in Wallonia?
answer: The rental guarantee is often a sum of money that your landlord asks you to pay if you leave: no rent, no charges, no end-of-lease compensation; no repair of rental damage. Your rental guarantee normally covers these costs. These are the obligations of the tenant. Please note: The rental guarantee cannot be used to pay your rent.
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rental guarantee, Definition and operation of the rental guarantee

question: Under what conditions can I apply for Belgian nationality if I claim exceptional merits?
answer: You can apply for Belgian nationality if: you have exceptional merits in the scientific, sporting or socio-cultural fields; and you are over 18 years old; and you live in Belgium legally; and you cannot acquire Belgian nationality through the declaration of nationality. To claim exceptional merits, you must prove that you make a special contribution to the international radiance of Belgium. In case of exceptional merits in the scientific field, you must prove that you have obtained a doctorate. In case of your exceptional merits in the sports field, you must also prove that you have met the international selection criteria or the criteria imposed by the IOC for a European championship, a world championship or the Olympics.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (over 18 years of age), persons claiming exceptional merits

question: The child's father and mother live together. Do the school, the doctor, etc. always have to ask for the consent of both parents for decisions concerning the child?
answer: No, not necessarily. Each parent can present himself alone to a third party (director, teacher, doctor, banker, municipality, etc.) and make a decision under parental authority (enrolling the child in school, having his child treated, etc.) without having to prove that the other has given his consent to this decision. The third party can perform the act requested by only one of the parents, provided that he is not aware of the other's disagreement.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: Is the notice extended if I get sick?
answer: It depends on whether you resign or get fired. If you resign, the notice starts (or continues) during your illness. It's not suspended, it's not extended by your illness. If you're fired, your notice is suspended during your illness. It's somehow extended by the duration of your illness. If the notice had already started before your illness, it's suspended for the duration of your absence due to illness. For example, you have to give 3 months' notice.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, during the notice

question: How can I appeal against a decision of the municipality regarding a residence permit or a residence permit?
answer: If the municipality refuses to register you, the municipality will automatically register you at an address or remove you from its population registers. Generally, the municipality will send you a certificate of non-registration or non-registration of the municipality. You will find the model in the documents-types of the municipality. The complaint is made by the Minister of the Interior. You must write: all the information (and documents) necessary to be able to make a correct opinion of the situation. Complete information: name, date of birth, last address, identification number of the municipality.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: What, to whom and when is the extension for exceptional circumstances in Wallonia used for?
answer: You can ask for an extension no matter who terminated the lease and for what reason. You must ask your landlord for an extension for exceptional circumstances. The extension request must: be sent by registered letter, be sent at least 1 month before the end of the lease, specify that it is an extension request for exceptional circumstances. If you do not meet these 3 conditions, your application is void. You can decide the duration of the extension together. If you do not agree, the justice of the peace decides.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Prolongation of the lease in exceptional circumstances

question: Where should I submit my application for integration allowance (AI)?
answer: You must enter your application for integration allowance (AI) on the website of the Directorate-General for the Disabled (DGPH). You must log in to "My Disability for Citizens" with your identity card and your identity card reader, Itsme, etc. The application for AI is made at the same time as the application for income replacement allowance (ARR). You must enter only one application for these 2 allowances. If you need help to enter your card, you can: apply to your nearest municipality; your CPAS mutual; an assistant of the DGPH by making an appointment.
topics: Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: What is a transfer of remuneration?
answer: An assignment of remuneration an assignment of income is also an agreement by which you transfer to your creditor the seizable part of your salary or income, in case you do not comply with your commitments. The clause by which you transfer, for example, your remuneration to the credit institution cannot be found in the main contract. You must sign 2 separate documents. The purpose of the assignment of remuneration is to attract your attention. The assignment of remuneration cannot be written in any other way, except by a separate letter.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of income

question: Can the administrator choose the residence of his protected person?
answer: This depends on the decision of the justice of the peace. The justice of the peace must follow a list of acts, and decide for each one whether the protected person is capable or incapable. The judge can add other acts to his decision, but he must at least decide for the acts on this list. The choice of residence is part of this list.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), The task of the administrator of property and or of the person

question: How much for the monthly deposit (interim bill) for gas and electricity?
answer: For gas and electricity, you receive a monthly interim bill (monthly down payment) for your monthly consumption. On this bill, the amount claimed is a monthly down payment. This amount is an estimate of your consumption. Interim bills are down payments to be deducted from your annual regularization bill. The amount of the monthly down payment is determined by your energy supplier. If you disagree with the proposed amount (you think it's too expensive or not expensive enough), you ask for an amount appropriate to your situation?
topics: Money, debts, debts related to the supply of energy

question: What is a cohabitation pact in Wallonia?
answer: The roommate pact is a document written between roommates. Each roommate must have a copy of the roommate pact. It organizes the rules of cohabitation between roommates. If you have a roommate lease, you must write a roommate pact. For more information, see the sheets: "What should my roommate pact contain?" "When should the roommate pact be signed?"
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Location (Wallonia)

question: Damage to housing: who pays what in Brussels?
answer: This file has been updated more than a year ago. Repairs and maintenance are distributed as described in the table below. During a shared trip, a tenant broke the window and damaged the chassis. Examples: A burglar broke the window to enter. Failure to enter, the tenant did not leave the heater on the roof. Repairs caused by a rental property were delayed unless the tenant was able to prove that he was not responsible for the damage.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work

question: I want to study in Belgium. For what kind of studies can I come to Belgium with a student stay?
answer: Before going any further, this question only concerns non-European students. You can get a residence permit to come to study in Belgium only for: higher education; or a preparatory year for higher education. You must continue your studies in an institution organized, recognized or subsidized by the Belgian public authorities. This can be a university or a high school. If you want to pursue higher education, this must be a preparatory year for higher education.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, student

question: After how long does my alimony debt expire?
answer: The food contribution debt is prescribed after a period of 5 years. This period of 5 years also applies to: extraordinary expenses; the indexation of the food contribution. This period begins on the day on which each monthly food contribution has to be paid by the debtor. For example: In March, the food contribution for February 2017 is prescribed. It is no longer possible to claim the amount of the food contribution if it is not paid, nor its indexation. But be careful, the prescription can be suspended or interrupted.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to food contributions

question: Can a relationship established between a mother or a father and his child be annulled?
answer: Yes, a judge can annul a bond of filiation, under certain conditions. In order to annul a bond of filiation between a mother or a father and their child, you must challenge the filiation before the family court. The time limit for challenging the filiation depends on: the person challenging, and on; the manner in which the filiation was established. For more information, see the question "Who can challenge paternity or maternity and within what time?"
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Disputing a parent-child bond

question: I'm a salaried worker, how much does the mutual pay me if I'm suspended from my job during my pregnancy?
answer: Before going any further, your mutual is here to inform you. Contact it. This sheet gives only a few general indications. For more details about sickness and disability insurance, see the section "Sickness and disability at work" If you work at a risk to your health or that of your baby, you can be excluded from your work during your pregnancy, under certain conditions. For more information, see the sheet "I am pregnant, am I excluded from my work?" During this period of pregnancy, you receive benefits from your mutual, from the first day of work.
topics: Social protection, Pregnancy and birth, During pregnancy

question: Can I be deported to Brussels in the winter?
answer: It depends, if you are a tenant of a private dwelling or a social dwelling. Yes, if you are a tenant of a private dwelling, you can be evicted during the winter. However, the justice of the peace has a discretion. He can "delay" the eviction.
topics: Housing, eviction
Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, Expulsion

question: Which CPAS should I contact for an installation premium?
answer: You must contact the CPAS of the municipality in which your dwelling is located. To be entitled to the installation premium, you must in particular no longer be homeless. You must be in a dwelling that serves as your main residence.
topics: Social protection, help from the CPAS, competence of the CPAS for the homeless

question: How is the use of food debtors for the RIS practically done?
answer: In principle, the CPAS must: grant you the social integration income (RIS) if you meet the conditions; check if you have alimony debtors who have sufficient resources, and if the family situation is serene (no conflicts); accompany you in the procedures to obtain alimony. Often, in practice, the CPAS decides to refuse the RIS and asks the person to impose himself on his alimony debtors. But legally, nothing obliges you to contact your alimony debtors before applying for the RIS. If you meet the conditions for obtaining the RIS, the CPAS cannot simply refuse it and the CPAS must analyze whether it is materially possible, and if it is appropriate, to help you in the procedure to obtain alimony.
topics: Social protection, Aid from the CPAS, Social integration income (RIS), Enforce your rights to a maintenance pension

Family, maintenance obligations, maintenance obligations (family solidarity), with a request for intervention from the CPAS, recourse to maintenance debtors at the time of granting the aid (a priori recourse)

question: What is the procedure for my adopted child to receive Belgian citizenship if I am a Belgian?
answer: In the event that your adopted child does not automatically receive Belgian nationality, you can make a declaration of granting Belgian nationality. This procedure is free of charge. Please note that you must make the declaration of granting Belgian nationality: within five years of the adoption; if your child is born before the age of 18 or is adopted in Belgium, you must make the declaration of granting Belgian nationality to the common residence.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, minors (under 18 years of age)

question: How to avoid deportation in Wallonia?
answer: To avoid eviction: Before your landlord starts proceedings If your lease expires: If your landlord has sent you notice but you haven't found a solution to relocate, you can ask for an extension of the lease for exceptional circumstances. For more information if: your home is in Wallonia, see the sheet "What, to whom and when does the extension for exceptional circumstances serve?" If your home is in Brussels, see the sheet "What, to whom and when does the extension for exceptional circumstances serve you?" If you are in arrears with your landlord: if your landlord threatens eviction, try to find a solution with him.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: How can I refuse an inheritance?
answer: If you do not wish to inherit, because there are debts or for personal reasons, you must also make a declaration of waiver. The declaration of waiver is made at a notary's office. You can go to the notary of your choice at the notary's office to find out the cost of this act. If you do not take any action, you are deemed to have accepted the inheritance.
topics: Money, death, inheritance and will, debts related to an inheritance
Money, death, inheritance and will, proceedings after death, formalities after death
Money, debts, debts related to an inheritance

question: I'm getting married. Can I marry a person of the same sex?
answer: If you are both Belgian, yes. If one of you is a foreigner or if you are both foreigners, same-sex marriage must be permitted under the law of your (or your) country. There is an exception: if one of you has the nationality of a state or has his or her habitual residence in the territory of a state that allows same-sex marriage, then you can marry in Belgium. Example: You are both foreigners, of the same sex, and you wish to marry in Belgium when the law of your nationality prohibits it.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. Can I withdraw my complaint of domestic violence?
answer: Yes, but this will have no influence on any prosecutions that the public prosecutor's office may bring. That is to say, the perpetrators will be prosecuted even if the victim does not file a complaint. This is the case for domestic violence. However, complaints of domestic violence are always forwarded to the public prosecutor's office. Once the complaint is received, the public prosecutor's office decides whether or not to prosecute.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: What happens if the bailiff seizes a piece of furniture that doesn't belong to me?
answer: If the bailiff has indicated in his inventory an object that does not belong to you, the owner of the property in question must contact the bailiff as soon as possible and provide him with proof that the property is his property. The bailiff can then delete the property from the inventory of the seizure. If the bailiff remains inflexible, the owner must then act before the judge of the owners (this is called the action in distraction or claim). The purchase is introduced by citation. The costs can therefore be high.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of movable property

question: Who can submit the application for the administration of property and or the person?
answer: Any interested person can ask the justice of the peace to put in place a measure of administration of the person's property. The person to be protected can also ask for it for himself. This interested person can be a relative, a neighbor, a head of a family assistance service, an adult child, a social worker, etc. It is necessary to be able to justify an interest and not to act in order to harm the person. The best thing is to encourage the person to protect to do it himself and possibly accompany him in this procedure.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)

question: At what age can I take out my pension?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants.This sheet contains only general information.For any details or additional questions, please contact: The Federal Pension Service (SFP) for employees and civil servants, the National Institute of Social Insurance for Self-Employed Workers (INASTI).In principle, you can retire at 65 years of age from the date of application Currently and until 1 January 2025 65 From 1 January 2025 to 1 February 2030 You can retire at the age of 67 on the 1st day of the following month.
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, retirement pension

question: Under what conditions do I have to pay alimony to my father or mother?
answer: The law provides for family solidarity between parents and children. If one of them is in need, the others must help him financially. This solidarity is reciprocal: parents must help their children, just as children must help their parents. However, strict conditions frame this request for help from parents and their children.
topics: Family, maintenance obligations, maintenance obligations (family solidarity), without a request for intervention from the CPAS

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce because of an irretrievable break-up. We agree to get a divorce. In what cases should I get a divorce because of an irretrievable break-up?
answer: The judge pronounces the divorce: either if you prove that you will be separated for more than 6 months; or if you prove that the continuation of the common life is no longer possible. This form of divorce has been submitted to: accelerate the procedures; eliminate the notion of fault in divorce; create a genuine "right to divorce" for the same reason when the other party does not agree to the divorce.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: How much does an appeal against a court decision cost?
answer: If you are not satisfied with the first judgment, you may file an appeal against the judgment of the Court of First Instance. • If you are not satisfied with the first judgment, you may file an appeal against the judgment of the Court of First Instance. • If you are not satisfied with the judgment of the Court of First Instance, you may file an appeal against the judgment of the Court of First Instance. • If you are not satisfied with the judgment of the Court of First Instance, you may file an appeal against the judgment of the Court of First Instance. • If you are not satisfied with the judgment of the Court of First Instance, you may file an appeal against the judgment of the Court of First Instance.
topics: Justice, In court, Expenses related to the functioning of justice

question: Can I get a free first legal advice (first line legal aid)?
answer: Yes legal. You are entitled to first legal aid, regardless of your financial situation. This is first-line legal aid. Specifically, this aid can be: practical information; legal information; a first legal opinion; a referral to a specialised body or organisation. So this is a first consultation open to all, but limited to first legal aid without concrete assistance. This should in principle allow you to assess whether to go further in a procedure or not. First-line legal aid is organised by: lawyers, who hold: physical offices in courthouses, Justice Houses, CPAS, certain associations, etc.
topics: Justice, the lawyer, legal aid (ex pro Deo)

question: I am a self-employed worker. What steps do I have to take to be entitled to "independent" maternity leave?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The independent scheme applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For ease of use, the term "self-employed woman" is used here in the broad sense, covering these three categories of women on maternity leave in the independent scheme. You must apply for maternity leave from your mutual. You must complete your mutual application form.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: How does the observation procedure take place?
answer: As soon as he receives the request for observation, the justice of the peace requests the appointment of a lawyer, to assist the person whose observation is requested. The assistance of the lawyer is mandatory due to the seriousness of the measures that can be taken. The lawyer is the spokesperson of the person, he ensures that his rights are respected. Within 24 hours of the filing of the request: Either the justice of the peace is not competent or the request is incomplete, he rejects the request. Then the justice of the peace considers that the request is admissible.
topics: Privacy, personal protection measures, protection of the mentally ill, treatment in a hospital environment

question: How much unemployment benefit will I receive if I haven't worked yet?
answer: Before proceeding further, this sheet contains only general information. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM); or your paying body (union or CAPAC <unk> Auxiliary Fund for the Payment of Unemployment Benefits). After your apprenticeship, you can receive unemployment benefits based on your studies, under certain conditions. The amount of unemployment benefits depends on your age and your family situation (single, cohabiting or cohabiting with a dependent).
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment after studies

question: I am a recognised refugee. I am a recognised refugee, or I have been granted subsidiary protection status. Am I entitled to help from the CPAS?
answer: Yes. As soon as your refugee status is recognised or if you have been granted subsidiary protection, you are entitled to social integration. However, you must meet the other conditions of the right to social integration and you must have left the collective reception centre. You can also apply for social assistance, for example: a rental guarantee, help to buy furniture, etc. You are registered in the register of foreigners and you have a temporary residence card for 1, 2 or 5 years (A card) which is renewable.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and CPAS aids

question: Under what conditions can I be regulated for medical reasons (9ter)?
answer: You can apply for a residence permit for more than 3 months in Belgium if you already live in Belgium and suffer from a serious illness. You can apply for a residence permit for more than 3 months if you are already residing in Belgium. You can apply for a residence permit for more than 3 months. You can apply for a residence permit for more than 9 months.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and healthcare, Medical stay
Foreigners, non-European stay, medical regularization (9th)

question: Real estate advance: do I have to pay it as a tenant in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. No. The mortgage can never be claimed from you, even if your lease provides for it. This is an imperative rule. The lease agreement cannot derogate from this. This does not prevent you from benefiting from the reduction of the mortgage. You are entitled to this reduction if you meet certain conditions related to your family situation. This is the case, for example, if you have 2 dependent children or 1 disabled person.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, expenses and insurance, Charges, Distribution of expenses

question: How do I submit my application for a declaration of nationality if I am the spouse of a Belgian?
answer: You must submit a file containing: your birth certificate; proof of your current nationality; proof of your spouse's Belgian nationality; an extract from your marriage certificate; a certificate of residence and the history of all your old addresses for the last 5 years; proof that you speak sufficiently the language of the municipality where you are applying (A2 level); proof of your social integration and therefore: You must also pay a certificate of completion of at least 400 hours of vocational training and proof of work (or payment of 3 days of social contributions if independent) for the last 5 years; a copy of your application must be submitted to the Civil Registry Office.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (from 18 years old), person married to a Belgian

question: I am a tenant. Do I have to install fire detectors in Brussels?
answer: No. On the other hand, your landlord has an obligation to install fire detectors in the building he rents. He has an obligation to install fire detectors: in your home; in the common corridors if it is a building with several apartments; in each room of the evacuation path of the rented housing. These are the rooms through which you have to go from the bedroom to the door that leads to the outside of the housing. If you do not comply with these obligations, you are considered partly responsible.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance

question: Am I entitled to more vacation days if I get married?
answer: The rules are not the same for public sector workers (civil servants) or for self-employed workers. There are also differences between employees and workers. In addition, this sheet explains the general rules provided by law, but there are special rules for certain sectors of activity or for certain companies. Please check with your personnel department, or with the National Office of Annual Leave (ONVA). You no longer have them; days of unpaid leave for employees, i.e. legal leave and extra-legal leave. These days are determined by your status (married, self-employed or self-employed) and the number of days of unpaid leave (including holidays) you can take during the year.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, annual leave

question: What happens if at the end of the lease the rental guarantee is used in Brussels?
answer: At the end of the lease, the rental guarantee can be used to pay the landlord if: there is rental damage (rent loss) or unpaid rents or charges. Let's review the different assumptions: If you have fully repaid this guarantee, the amount in the blocked account can be used to pay the landlord. If there is a conflict regarding the rental loans or rents, it can be resolved by the justice of the peace.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Aid to create a rental guarantee, Aid from the CPAS

question: I'm settling in. Where should I settle?
answer: As soon as you move, you have to make the declaration to the municipality. For more information, see our file How do I change my address? To verify if you actually live in a place, the municipality sends a neighborhood agent to your home. To verify that you and your belongings are in place. To mention the other verification criteria, see our file How does the urban verification inquiry take place? If the municipality finds that you actually live in the mentioned place, it is obliged to check it. Then the municipality can only refuse you if you do not live in my place. If you do not have a temporary residence in Belgium.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: I had an accident driving my child to day care: am I covered by the employer's insurance?
answer: Before going any further, the rules for work-related accidents are very complex. This sheet does not explain them all in detail. This sheet applies only to workers in the private sector (not the public sector). For more information about my work-related accidents, you can contact the following services: The prevention advisor of your company's internal and external prevention and protection service.
topics: Work, salaried work, illness and accident, accident at work

question: If my student lease is not registered, can I terminate it without notice or compensation in Brussels?
answer: First check that your lease is not registered. Indeed, your landlord may have done so without warning you. To find out, you can: ask the legal security office (formerly the registration office) of the place where your accommodation is located (coordinated here); or consult your portal 'My Minfin'. You must have an ID card reader and your landlord's electronic ID card to connect you. You can leave your accommodation without notice or compensation if: Your lease is not registered; Your lease is a student lease and you have signed the lease more than 2 months ago and you have to comply with the rules of the lease.
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, Kot (Brussels), student lease

question: Am I entitled to family allowances for my children if I work part-time?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the social inspection of the ONSS (National Office of Social Security) for the social security of part-time workers; your union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. Yes, on 1 January 2019, the child opens his right to family allowances.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights to social security as a part-time worker

question: How does the medical regularization procedure take place?
answer: If you do not have a valid residence permit, you can apply for a new residence permit. • If you have a valid residence permit, you can apply for a new residence permit. • If you do not have a valid residence permit, you can apply for a new residence permit. • If you do not have a valid residence permit, you can apply for a new residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, medical regularization (9th)

question: I'm filing a lawsuit, I've appealed the verdict, what happens while we wait for the new verdict?
answer: In some cases, your opponent can already force you to enforce the judgment (forced enforcement) even if you have appealed. This is called provisional enforcement (or enforcement by provision). The judgment specifies whether it is enforceable provisionally or not. But if the judgment does not specify anything: it is enforceable provisionally if both parties were present at the proceedings (adversarial proceedings); it is not enforceable provisionally if you were not present at the proceedings (default proceedings).
topics: Justice, In court, The court decision

question: I am an employee. Am I entitled to paternity leave if I have not recognised my child?
answer: The rules are not the same for public sector workers (civil servants) or for self-employed workers. Officially, paternity leave is called birth leave because same-sex partners are also entitled to it, under certain conditions. Only if you can prove your permanent relationship with the child's mother: by a declaration of legal cohabitation; or by a minimum and affective cohabitation of 3 years before the child's birth. You must prove this cohabitation by an extract from the population register: you must be married to the same mother as the child's mother for at least 3 years.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid paternity leave (birth leave)

question: In what cases can the CPAS recover social assistance from my children?
answer: It depends on the help you have received. But the CPAS must respect the general limits (resources of maintenance debtors exceeding a certain ceiling, reasons of equity and the existence of a right to a maintenance pension). answer: The employer and or the worker can invoke the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure only if: the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure has been carried out to the end; and the worker is definitively unable to perform the agreed work; and the worker's permanent incapacity for work has been ascertained by: the occupational doctor of the SP; or the social inspector doctor of the General Directorate of Control of Well-being at Work; and the 21-day deadline to file an appeal against the decision of the occupational doctor is over.

answer: If your income has decreased or increased by at least 20% during the information that precedes your application compared to 2 years ago, the DGPH takes into account your income from the year that precedes your application. question: Who appoints the guardian?
answer: It is always the justice of the peace who appoints the guardian. Parents can choose a guardian in advance for their minor children. They can do this in a declaration: before the justice of the peace of their place of residence; at the notary. The registrar or the notary registers the declaration within 15 days in the central register of declarations. A parent who exercises exclusive parental authority can choose a guardian in a will. After the death of the parents or if they can no longer exercise parental authority, the justice of the peace will approve the choice of the guardian of the parents. The guardian must accept his mission.

question: Which parent is responsible for the actions of our minor child if we are separated?
answer: You are both responsible for the acts committed by your minor child. Even if you are separated or divorced, your duties of supervision and upbringing remain common. If your child causes damage while he is with the other parent, you can more easily prove that you have not committed any fault in supervision. But this does not prevent you from committing a fault in upbringing, which could also make you responsible for the damage.

question: I'm renting a cot. Can I ask the tenant in Wallonia for proof of solvency?
answer: Yes. As a landlord, you can choose your tenant freely. However, your choice cannot be based on a discriminatory reason. In order to make a selection, you can ask interested parties to provide you with certain information and related documents. Proof of the applicant's solvency is part of it. However, this information is limited to: the name and surname of the applicant; a means of communication with the applicant; the address of the applicant; the date of birth of the applicant or proof of his ability to enter into a contract; the composition of the applicant's household; the amount of financial resources available to the applicant.

question: What are my obligations as a worker?
answer: You have several obligations. You must perform the agreed work, at the agreed place, according to the agreed schedule, etc. You must perform your work carefully and conscientiously. If your job requires the use of certain tools, instruments or materials, you must use them carefully and return them in good condition. You must respect the orders and directives of your supervisor, because you are under his authority. You cannot compete unfairly with your employer. For example, you cannot reveal manufacturing secrets. You must not do anything that would endanger the safety of your employees.

question: Who has to pay the funeral expenses?
answer: When the heirs accept the estate, they have to pay the funeral expenses, even if they have not signed the contract. But the problem arises mainly when there is not enough money in the estate, and all the heirs have renounced the estate. So you can sign a contract with a funeral home even if you then want to renounce the estate. This is not considered a tacit acceptance of the estate.

question: Who has to pay the down payment on the property?

A real right implies a right that is exercised on the property: possession, the possibility of occupying it, living in it and renting it. The situation on January 1 of the tax year determines who has to pay the real estate advance of the year. If the house is sold during the year, the owner still has to pay the real estate advance for the year started.

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. What forms does domestic violence take?
answer: Marital violence is defined as any form of physical, sexual, psychological or economic violence between: spouses or cohabiting (or cohabiting) persons; and maintaining (or having maintained) a lasting emotional and sexual relationship. This definition is broad and encompasses many situations. It is the partner who is referred to in this definition. It does not matter whether the persons are married, legal cohabiting or simple concubines. It is the unbalanced nature of the relationship and the domination of one partner over the other that determines violence.

question: Am I entitled to unemployment benefits when I get out of prison?
answer: Yes. When you leave prison, you can reopen your right to unemployment, subject to certain conditions. You may be entitled to unemployment benefits based on your work before your detention. If you received unemployment benefits before your detention, you are entitled to them again. If you were detained for less than 3 years, you do not have to disclose your detention. If you were detained for more than 3 years, you must disclose your detention. If you did not receive unemployment benefits before your detention, you may be entitled to unemployment benefits based on the work you did before your detention.

question: I am married. Should I remain faithful to my spouse?

question: Under what conditions can I apply for Belgian nationality if I am stateless?
answer: You can apply for Belgian nationality if: you are stateless, i.e. you have no nationality; and you are 18 years old or an emancipated minor; and you have resided in Belgium legally for at least 2 years. To be of legal age: up to the age of 18, your parents are the ones who manage your property and or the person. 2. To be unable to manage your property and or the person, regardless of whether this is total or partial, temporary or permanent. 3. Due to a state of health: the incentive to manage your property and or the person must come from a state of physical or mental health.

question: What's the vacation money?
answer: The vacation allowance is the amount of money you receive during your vacation. There are two allowances. The simple vacation allowance is the amount of money you receive during your paid vacation days. It is your remuneration for those paid vacation days. So it is not a bonus, it is simply because you are entitled to paid vacation days. The double vacation allowance is a sum of money you receive on your vacation, so that you can take advantage of your vacation.

question: How is the hearing before the CPAS going?
answer: Following your request, the CPAS will send you a written summons. You will be invited to appear before the Social Action Council or the Special Committee on Social Assistance. This council or committee is usually composed of a small dozen people. According to the CPAS, your social assistant or the head of the social service, and possibly the lawyer of the CPAS, will also be present. Generally, the hearings take place 15 days to 1 month at the most. The CPAS usually makes a new decision immediately after this hearing.

question: Can my employer fire me from my student job?
answer: Yes, your employer can fire you from your student job. Your first three days of work are considered a probationary period. If your employer fires you during these three days: it must not give you notice; it must not pay you compensatory notice. If it fires you after your probationary period, it must give you notice: you must continue to work for a certain number of days before your contract actually expires. In other words, there is a reduced period of time between your termination and the end of your employment. For students, the notice period varies depending on your seniority.

question: Does my employer have to pay me compensation if he fires me?
answer: Only if: he dismisses you without notice; or if he does not respect the duration of the notice; or if he does not respect a specific procedure (especially when there is a clause of stability of employment in the sector of activity); or if the worker is compensated against dismissal. Your employer must essentially comply with 2 rules: give you notice, i.e. warn you in advance of his willingness to terminate the contract of employment; respect the duration of the amount of work, which varies according to your employer, your salary, your sector of activity, etc. Your employer must not pay you compensation if: he gives you notice of dismissal in accordance with the law (or the applicable labor agreement) or if there is no reason for your dismissal.

It does not matter if you have resigned, or if your employer has dismissed you. But you must be in the notice period. question: I ⁇ m in prison, how much unemployment benefit will the person I lived with get?
answer: If you were living with an unemployed person before you went to prison, you are still presumed to be living with an unemployed person for the first 12 months of your incarceration. question: I'm married. Do the property I owned before the marriage become common property?

question: I am married. Do I have to live under the same roof as my spouse?

question: Who is responsible if I have an accident with the company car?

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. Am I affected by domestic violence?

question: I'm applying for family reunification. I'm European. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: If you are a Belgian citizen, you must prove that you have been living with your spouse for at least 1 year before the application; or that you have been living with your spouse for at least 2 years before the application; or that you have been in regular contact by telephone, mail, etc. and that you have met your spouse 3 times during this period; or that you have a child of common origin; or that you are single; or that you have been married for more than 21 years; or that you have been married to a Belgian citizen; or that you can prove that you are entitled to custody of your spouse.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: What decisions can the occupational doctor make after the reintegration evaluation?
answer: Before going any further, since October 1st, the reintegration pathway procedure has been amended. The new rules apply to: reintegration pathway procedures in progress; new reintegration pathway procedures. As of November 28th, the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure is no longer done through the reintegration pathway. For the reintegration contract evaluation, the occupational physician makes one of the following decisions: Decision A. The disability is temporary; Decision A. The worker can resume work later (if necessary, with an adaptation of the workplace).
topics: Work, sickness - incapacity for work, resumption of adapted work during incapacity, reintegration journey

question: How does an expulsion take place in Wallonia?
answer: The eviction can only take place when the landlord has an enforceable title! The enforceable title is usually a judgment of the justice of the peace who pronounces the end of the lease; the eviction of the tenant is a bit different. So you will not be able to be evicted by your landlord after the expiry of the notice of eviction. If he does not have the permission of the judge, here are the different stages of the eviction procedure: The eviction must be requested from the justice of the peace where the tenant is located.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: What happens to the lease in the event of the death of the tenant in Wallonia?
answer: Since September 1, 2018, the lease expires 3 months after the death of the tenant without notice or compensation.Attention, on the other hand, during the 3 months after the death, the heirs, who have accepted the inheritance, must pay the rent, charges and rentals immediately.There are exceptions: If the landlord dies, the lease is considered to be at the end of the month, he is automatically liable for the death of the landlord (within 3 months of the death of the landlord).In the event of the death of the landlord, it is not possible for the landlord to take legal action against the landlord.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Death

question: I receive confidentiality as a professional. Can I be prosecuted for breach of confidentiality if I have recklessly disclosed information?
answer: In principle, no. For there to be a breach of professional secrecy, the disclosure must be voluntary and conscious. It is not enough that the information has been disclosed, you must also have had the intention to disclose it. So there is no criminal penalty if you disclose the information through negligence or recklessness.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: Do you have to make an inventory when a roommate goes to Wallonia?
answer: Yes. If your lease is an official tenancy lease, it is compulsory. Otherwise, it is not compulsory but it is strongly advised. For the departing roommate, it is very important to have a list of places of exit for his private part and for the common parts. Otherwise, the landlord can claim from him the rental damage that occurred after his departure. The list of places of exit of the outgoing roommate can be signed by the replacement roommate.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Departure and arrival of a roommate
Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Location

question: I'm joining a Belgian. I'm in my country of origin. How does the family reunification procedure with a Belgian go?
answer: You must submit your application to the competent Belgian embassy or consulate for your place of residence. If your file is complete, it is sent to the Foreigners' Office (OE). The OOE has 6 months to make a decision. In case of a positive decision, the embassy or consulate gives you a D visa (long stay) and you can go to Belgium. Once in Belgium, you must go to the municipal administration to register in the foreigners' quarter after a residence check done by a registration officer, you receive an F card.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: What is the CPAS? To which CPAS should I go to ask for help?
answer: In principle, you should contact the CPAS of the municipality where you are located, even if you are registered elsewhere, i.e. in the registers of the population or foreigners in another municipality (administrative situation); the CPAS of the municipality where you have your usual and effective residence is competent to help you (except for exceptions). The municipality where you are registered (i.e. in the registers of the population or foreigners) normally corresponds to your usual residence, but not always. For example, you can be registered at an old address elsewhere.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, in principle

question: I want to study in Belgium. Under what conditions can I come to study in Belgium?
answer: Before going any further, this question only applies to non-European students. You can apply for a residence permit for more than 3 months to come to study in Belgium in higher education or to follow a preparatory year for studies in higher education. For more than 18 months, you must meet the following conditions: You must have a valid passport or a travel document in place. For a residence permit for more than one year, please complete our residence permit information sheet. For a residence permit for more than 6 months, please complete the following conditions:
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, student

question: I'm self-employed. My baby has to stay in the hospital after giving birth. What are the consequences on my leave if I'm self-employed?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The independent scheme applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For convenience, the term "self-employed woman" is used here in the broad sense, covering these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the independent scheme. You can ask for your maternity leave to be extended, but only if your baby is hospitalized for more than 7 days after birth. The duration of this extension corresponds to the number of full weeks during which your baby has been hospitalized.
topics: Work, work and parenting, "independent" maternity leave

question: Am I entitled to the Grapa if I live in a roommate?
answer: Before going any further Attention, the rules are not quite the same for older workers, self-employed workers and civil servants.This sheet contains only general information.For more information, contact the Federal Pension Service (SFP).Yes, if you meet the conditions to benefit from the Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly).These conditions concern: your age; your nationality; your main place of residence (it must be located in Belgium) and your resources; this guarantee is granted only to elderly people who do not have sufficient resources.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits, pension
Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: Am I entitled to an old-age pension if I have resources?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for wage earners, self-employed workers and civil servants. This form contains general information. question: I am applying for family reunification. What are the conditions for family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) for an unlimited stay?
answer: 1) The regrouping must have: a residence permit for at least 12 months. This can be proven by: a registered lease agreement or a notarial deed of ownership according to a health insurance that covers the risks in Belgium for himself and the members of the family; or you have a common minor child; or he is recognized as a refugee or beneficiary of subsidiary protection. 2) Proof of sufficient means of subsistence, stable and sufficient to meet the needs of his own family, to avoid his or her leaving the country.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Under what conditions am I entitled to the income replacement allowance (ARR)?
answer: To benefit from the income replacement allowance (ARR), you must meet the following conditions: You must be a Belgian or belong to certain categories of foreigners: foreigners legally residing from a country of the European Union; foreigners registered in the population register: regardless of your nationality; stateless persons; refugees; foreigners benefiting from subsidiary protection status; etc. You must be domiciled in Belgium and actually live there. You must meet this condition at the time of your application; you must live together for the entire period during which you must receive the ARR. There are exceptions to this condition.
topics: Social protection, disability, income replacement allowance (ARR)

question: I'm European. Am I entitled to reimbursement of my health care if I have an E card?
answer: Yes. You can join a mutual in Belgium, either as a holder or as a dependent. You must meet the conditions for joining the mutual. If you are considered an indigent person, you can also apply for help from the CPAS. The CPAS can, under certain conditions, grant you medical social assistance. In this case, the CPAS pays for your health care that your mutual does not cover or if you are not affiliated to a mutual.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and healthcare, Healthcare and residence status

question: Real estate agency management fees: does the student have to pay them in Wallonia?
answer: First of all, check your lease. To find out if your lease is a student lease or a main residence lease, see the sheets: "My cottage lease is a student lease (Wallonia) " and "My cottage lease serves as my home, does this mean that I have a main residence lease (Wallonia) " Hypothesis 1: Your lease is a student lease. Your student lease does not provide that the management fees of the real estate agency are at your expense. You do not have to pay the sheets.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), (un) legal clauses

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I become a legal cohabitant. Can I make a declaration of legal cohabitation if I am in Belgium illegally?
answer: Yes. Check the situation of irregularity of residence is not an obstacle. Registration of legal cohabitation can take place in Belgium if you and your partner have your common habitual residence there at the time of the application for registration of legal cohabitation. If you are in legal residence, the civil registry officer consults the national register; if you are in illegal residence, the civil registry officer asks the neighborhood officer to do a residence check. It is Belgian law that determines the conditions for registering legal cohabitation.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Does my insurance cover my child who never lives with me?
answer: In principle, no. Your child is not covered by your insurance when he causes damage, if he never lives with you. For example, the other parent hosts your child in an "exclusive" or "main" way. Unless your insurance contract provides for coverage. However, you remain personally responsible for the actions of your minor child even if you do not live with him. So you can use your insurance to cover your personal responsibility and to compensate victims for damage caused by your child.
topics: Money, Insurance, Family Insurance, Covered Children

question: How much do I get paid if I get sick while working part-time?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the social inspection of the ONSS (National Office of Social Security) for the social security of part-time workers; your union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. During the first days of illness, you are paid by your employer. This is called the guaranteed salary.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights to social security as a part-time worker

question: Am I covered if my minor child commits an intentional act?
answer: In principle, yes. As a parent of a minor child, you are, in most cases, held personally responsible for the damage caused by your child. So you can, in principle, claim your insurance to cover your own liability.
topics: Money, Insurance, Family Insurance (RC), Covered Losses

question: Do I have to apply for family allowances for every birth in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since 1 January 2019, and since 1 January 2020.For more information on the reform, see the sheet <unk> What is changing with the reform of family allowances? <unk> No. You do not have to submit an application for each new birth. For a first application, you must complete the form <unk>Family allowance application<unk> and send it to your family allowance fund. For example, a dependent person for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent person for the unemployment card or for the help of the CPAS.

answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. During your internship, you will not receive any allowance from ONEM. EURONEM cannot, in principle, punish you. But you still have to comply with certain obligations, including that of being available for the labour market. If the ONEM you have worked for, it will extend the duration of your internship longer than the duration during which you have worked.

question: Under which parent is our child enrolled in the mutual?
answer: Before going any further, your mutual is here to inform you. Contact it. This sheet gives only a few general indications. For more details about health and disability insurance, see the section "Sickness - disability to work" If only one of the parents is a member of the mutual, the child is registered at the expense of that parent. Indeed, the other parent who is a member of the first parent cannot choose the child at his expense, since he is not a member. If both parents are mutual, the parent can automatically choose the child at the expense of the oldest.

question: Who exercises parental authority if neither parent can make decisions?
answer: It is possible that no parent can make decisions about their child for various reasons: either because both parents have died, or because the child has no legal parents (this is very rare), or because both parents are unable to make decisions in a lasting way, for example: mental illness: for example, because a mental illness makes them unable to make decisions; physical: for example, because they have been abroad for a long time without giving any sign of life, or because they have gone into a coma; legal: for example, because a judge has declared them unable to make decisions and has placed them under a legal disability status, for example.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: I am MENA. How is my guardian appointed if I am MENA?
answer: This information is immediately communicated to your guardian and the relevant authorities. In an emergency, you receive the identity of the guardian, and information on the guardianship. In case of extreme urgency, the guardianship service may, on its own initiative or at the request of certain authorities, appoint you a guardian to take care of you, if you appear or meet the conditions to be considered an MENA, but you have not yet been definitively identified.
topics: Foreigners, foreign minors

question: Certain circumstances prevent me from working, am I entitled to RIS?
answer: Yes. The CPAS can exempt you from proving your willingness to work, if this is justified on grounds of health or equity. Example of health reasons: heavy work cannot be considered for a woman who is several months pregnant, or who has medically proven back problems. The reasons for equity are reasons that justify that in the circumstances in which the person is, it would not be "fair" or "fair" to ask him to work. For example: A single mother with several children of low age or disabled, and living in a remote rural area looking for means of communication to get to a job and a nursery.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Willingness to work

The tenant has a choice of the type of rental guarantee. 3) The rental guarantee contract often stipulates that you must pay a monthly guarantee in cash, the choice between 3 types. 2) In this case, the rental guarantee contract usually requires the tenant to deposit a certain amount of money in the same month. 3) In this case, the rental guarantee contract requires the owner to pay a monthly guarantee. 3) In this case, the rental guarantee contract requires the owner to pay a monthly guarantee.

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. I am receiving an inheritance. Does my legal cohabitant also benefit from it?
answer: No. You are the sole owner of all the property you inherit personally, before or during legal cohabitation. You must pay the debts and inheritance rights that arise from it yourself (unless these debts become household expenses). You must be able to prove that this property belongs to you. Otherwise, in the absence of proof, the property is presumed to belong to the 2 cohabitants. So it can be used to pay the debts of the other cohabitant. You can prove your ownership, for example, by a declaration of inheritance, or by a liquidation act.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Should we open a joint account if we are legal cohabitants?
answer: No. You are not obliged to open a joint account. You are free to decide to open a joint account, and/or to keep separate accounts. On the other hand, you must contribute to the expenses of the common life. Common account or not, you must each pay your share in the household expenses, such as rent, groceries, the purchase of a car, children's clothing, gas and electricity bills, etc. In practice, as long as everything goes well, the distribution of the expenses of the common life is spontaneous. But if one of you does not contribute, the other can ask the family court to force him to do so.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: What steps should be taken to apply for medical regularization (9ter)?
answer: You must submit your application by post, by registered mail to the Foreigners' Office (FE). Your application must contain the following elements: proof of your identity (passport, identity card, ...) or proof of your exemption from proof of your identity if you are a refugee; a standard medical certificate dated less than 3 months and completed by a doctor; any useful information or element proving that care is non-existent or inaccessible in your country of origin; your effective residence address; you must have an elected address (the address where the decision will be sent, for example to your lawyer's office).
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and healthcare, Medical stay

question: Can I work as a volunteer if I'm unemployed?
answer: Before going any further, only former employees are entitled to unemployment benefits. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM) or your paying agency (syndicate or CAPAC); Caisse auxiliaire; Payment of unemployment benefits. Yes, you are entitled to certain benefits, but only under certain conditions. As an unemployed person, you must in principle actively seek employment at all times.

question: Where and how should the birth of a child be declared?
answer: You must declare the birth to the municipality in which the child was born, including weekends and public holidays. But if the last day of the 15-day deadline is a Saturday, a Sunday or a public holiday, the deadline is extended to the first working day that follows. For example, if the last day of the municipality is a Saturday, you can still go to declare the birth of the child. Except if it is a public holiday: you can go to declare the birth on Tuesday. If you do not declare the birth, the deadline is based on a certificate received at the time of birth.
topics: Family, Relationship between parents and children, Birth certificate

question: I am an employee. What steps do I have to take to be entitled to paternity leave?
answer: The rules are not the same for public sector workers (civil servants) or for self-employed workers. Officially, paternity leave is called birth leave because same-sex partners are also entitled to it, subject to certain conditions. 1. You must notify your employer: of the birth of your child; and of your willingness to take your paternity leave. 2. If you are no longer married to the child's mother, you must provide your employer with proof of parentage between you and the child.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid paternity leave (birth leave)

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. How will I receive the court's decision?
answer: You, or your lawyer, will receive an e-mail with a copy of the judgment within 5 days after the judgment has been pronounced. So you should not return it to the judge on that date. If the judge has not given a date, he should normally pronounce the judgment within 1 month. You will receive a copy of the e-mail and you will not receive a notification of the judgment. This is the official communication of a judgment by the intermediary of a judge.
topics: Justice, In court, The court decision

question: Does the official tenancy automatically end when it expires in Wallonia?
answer: If your apartment lease is also a main residence lease: It does not end automatically. The tenants or the landlord must send their notice so that the lease ends: the tenant (s) must send their notice at least 3 months before the due date. The landlord must send their notice: 3 months before the due date for a short-term lease; 6 months for a lease of 9 years or more. For more information, see the section "End and termination of the apartment lease"; Attention: All tenants must sign the notice.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Duration and end of the colocation, Duration and end of the official colocation lease (Colocation Wallonia)

question: I have a procedure abroad, what should I do?
answer: Legal aid systems exist in all European countries. The conditions for access and the extent of legal protection are different, but the principle remains the same. You have two possibilities. 1) You can contact a legal aid office in Belgium. It is the conditions of the country of residence that determine the right to legal aid. For example, if you live in Belgium and you have a dispute in France: you can benefit from the services of a French lawyer if you are eligible for legal aid in Belgium.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro deo), Steps to obtain legal aid (ex pro deo)

question: What if I don't get the extension for exceptional circumstances in Wallonia?
answer: You can ask the judge for a grace period, i.e. a final deadline to leave the dwelling. To get the grace period, you must be "unhappy and in good faith". The judge assesses whether you are and decides whether it is necessary to grant you a grace period. For example, if you have had an accident and have a broken leg, the judge could consider you "unhappy and in good faith". This is also the case if: 1.

question: I'm a victim of domestic violence. How does the cycle of domestic violence unfold?
answer: Marital violence takes place according to a cycle. This one is as follows: 1. Phase of building tensions The abuser is dissatisfied and threatens. The victim is afraid and tries to avoid conflicts. 2. Phase of explosion Violence occurs. 4. Phase of honeymoon Victim and abuser reconcile. The victim hopes that the abuser will change. The abuser seeks forgiveness and is cared for with the victim.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence, domestic violence

question: I'm a tenant. Do I have to take out fire insurance if I'm a tenant in Brussels?
answer: The law does not require you to take out fire insurance, so you should check what your lease stipulates. It is often stated in the lease that the tenant must take out fire insurance. If your lease requires you to take out fire insurance, then you are contractually obliged to take out fire insurance. If you do not do so, you are not obliged to take out fire insurance as a tenant.

For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. question: How many times can I ask for an extension for exceptional circumstances in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. Extension for exceptional circumstances can be requested a maximum of 2 times for the same lease: 1 extension request and 1 extension renewal request. For example, your lease must expire on March 1st, but on January 5th, you become seriously ill, you can ask for an extension of your lease. You are granted a 6-month extension. The lease expires on September 1st.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, Extension of the lease in case of exceptional circumstances

question: I'm unemployed and I live in a roommate, how do I keep my unemployment rate isolated?
answer: Before proceeding further, only former employees are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, except if they were employed before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules. This file contains only general information. For more information, please contact: National Employment Office (ONEM) or your payment agency (homeowner or roommate).

question: I'm renting out an apartment or a house. Do I have to terminate the lease to be able to sell my property in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. No, you must not terminate the lease in order to sell your property. The lease will be transferred to the new owner at the same time as the ownership of the dwelling. For the rules for terminating the lease applicable between the new owner and the tenant, see 'What happens to my tenant if I sell my property?' If you wish to terminate the lease before selling, you must comply with the legal rules.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia), Selling your property

question: Am I entitled to childbirth allowance if my child is born abroad?
answer: Yes, if the child is registered in Belgium within 2 months of birth. Otherwise, it depends on the country in which you give birth, the existence of an international directive, a European regulation or an agreement between Belgium and that country, etc. The rules are very complex. The most prudent thing to do is to check with your family allowance fund, or FAMIWAL.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowances, Birth allowance for children born from 1 January 2020

question: End of maintenance of observation at the end of the fixed period?
answer: At the end of the maintenance period set by the justice of the peace (maximum 2 years), the measure ends if no request for renewal has been submitted. 15 days before the expiry of the maintenance period, the treating physician may request the renewal of the measure to the justice of the peace. As for the first maintenance request, this request is accompanied by a medical report. The justice of the peace proceeds with a new examination of the request by visiting the patient and collecting all useful information.
topics: Privacy, Personal protection measures, Protection of the mentally ill, Care in a "family" environment (outside of a hospital environment)

question: What documents should be included in my medical visa application?
answer: Your application for residence for medical reasons must contain at least the following elements: your passport; a medical certificate certifying that treatment is impossible in your country of origin. This certificate must be drawn up by a doctor approved by the Belgian embassy or consulate; proof that you have made a medical appointment in Belgium with a doctor specializing in your illness (doctor's coordinates, date of appointment, planned medical treatment, your identity data, etc.); an estimate of the cost of health care and residence, drawn up by the doctor specializing in Belgium; proof that you have sufficient funds for your medical appointment; proof that you have received a visa from the Belgian Embassy.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and healthcare, Medical stay

question: How do you know if you have a roommate?
answer: When buying, carefully read the deed of sale and the annexes. This can be avoided. If the information is not exclusive, ask the notary for clarifications. If the deed of sale does not specify anything, check the property clause. The clause determines whether the fence is located explicitly on the land of a land or "nearby" on the 2 land plots. The use of an expert facilitates this operation by presumption.

question: What is the value of the agreements obtained in family mediation?
answer: The family mediation agreement remains an amicable agreement. It has no value only between you, even if the mediator has helped you to draft it. If one of you does not respect this agreement, the other cannot force him to do so. For example, you cannot appeal to a bailiff with an amicable agreement. For some social and tax administrations, this agreement has no value. It gives additional strength to your opinion, you must approve it by the family judge. The approved agreement has the same value as a judgment.
topics: Family, Resolving a family conflict, Finding an amicable solution, Family mediation

question: I signed a legal cohabitation declaration. Who owns the property purchased during the legal cohabitation?
answer: In principle, the property you buy alone during cohabitation belongs to you alone. You have to pay all the expenses related to these properties alone, but you only benefit from the income they provide (such as the rent of a building, for example). The property bought by you and your legal cohabitant belongs to all two of you, each for one half or another chosen proportion. So you have to pay together the debts related to these properties, and you benefit together from the income produced by these properties.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I'm getting married. Can the registrar refuse my marriage?
answer: Yes The Registrar of Civil Status (OEC) checks whether you meet the conditions to be able to marry in Belgium. He also checks whether it is a "fake marriage". He checks the intention of the parties: is the purpose of the marriage to create a lasting community of life? Or is the sole purpose of the marriage to obtain a residence permit for one of the spouses?

question: I receive confidentiality as a professional. How do I know if I am bound by professional secrecy?
answer: The criterion is that of the "necessary confidant": if, in order to exercise your function, you must know certain private information and have a relationship of trust with the person who confides in you, you are a necessary confidant. So it is the function that you exercise that is decisive, more than your qualification. Jurisprudence has gradually specified who was or was not bound to professional secrecy by the fact that they exercise such information, or such a function.

question: Can the administrator of property and or the person do everything?
