question: I am a tenant. What is the waiver of appeal clause?
answer: It is a clause in the fire insurance contract of the landlord's rented dwelling. Some landlords wish the tenant to be covered by their own fire insurance. In this case, the landlord incorporates a waiver clause against the tenant, in the lease contract and in his own fire insurance contract. He then waives his right to sue you if you are responsible for a fire. You are as a tenant automatically covered by the landlord's insurance. The advantage of this insurance is that the landlord is sure that his tenant is insured and that the damage caused to his dwelling will be properly covered.

question: When is there a guardianship?
answer: If one parent dies, the other parent exercises parental authority alone. This impossibility must be established by the family court in a judgment. A parent is considered unable to make decisions when he is unable to make decisions for reasons: psychological: for example, a partial illness makes him unable to make decisions; physical: for example, he has been in a coma for a long time without any signs of life.

question: I'm separated from my husband. Do I have to pay alimony?
answer: Yes, it's possible. Every married person has a duty of care and assistance to their spouse. Specifically, the wealthier spouse must help the less wealthy spouse. In principle, this duty of care is carried out in kind by sharing housing, health care, etc. If you are separated, enforcement in kind is no longer possible. The wealthier spouse can claim alimony, i.e. a financial equivalent. The criterion taken into account is the difference in living standards. This alimony can be due for the duration of the separation.

In return, the landlord undertakes to grant him an advantage, for example a rent reduction. The renovation lease can also be decided during the lease. In this case, a renovation lease is made. To make a renovation lease or a renovation lease inspection, all the following conditions must be met: • This is only possible if the lease lasts at least 3 years.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work, Works and improvements, Works by the tenant

question: What are the rights of the person under observation?
answer: During the observation procedure, the person has the right: to be heard before the judge; to be accompanied by a trusted person; to ask the doctor of his choice to intervene on his behalf, or to challenge the decision of the justice of the peace. He also has the right to receive all the information and explanations about his treatment: its advantages and disadvantages, its side effects, as well as possible alternatives. Every patient also has the right to complain about the existing measures of detention of which he would have been the victim. If the measures of detention (such as the isolation room, the torture chamber, the security file) are necessary for the patient's safety and that of his treatment, they do not constitute inhuman and degrading treatment.
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: Does the integration allowance (AI) decrease if I live with other people?
answer: No. Living with other people does not reduce the amount of your integration allowance (AI). You are entitled to it even if you live with other people. The amount of your integration allowance (AI) depends on: your reduced autonomy; your income. The General Directorate for Disabled Persons only takes into account your income. It does not take into account the income of your spouse, legal cohabitant, de facto cohabitant or friend. For more information, see our sheet "Am I entitled to an integration allowance (AI)?
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits
Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: What is the use of the rental guarantee in Brussels?
answer: The rental guarantee is often a sum of money that your landlord asks for if you leave: without paying rent, charges, an end-of-lease indemnity; without repairing rental damage. The rental guarantee is used to cover these costs. These are obligations of the tenant. The rental guarantee is security for your landlord in case of breach of your obligations.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Functioning of the rental guarantee

question: Can we seize my meal vouchers?
answer: In principle, no. Meal vouchers cannot be seized. But there are two exceptions. Meal vouchers are seizable when: granted in replacement of a salary adjustment not paid in cash; replaces in whole or in part the remuneration due, for example, in the case of a salary supplement or salary indexation. In these two cases only, the meal vouchers are seizable.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of income

question: I'm a salaried worker. Does my employer have to pay me during my breastfeeding leave?
answer: No, during this gap you are entitled, under certain conditions, to a mutual allowance. You must hand over to your mutual: a certificate from the occupational doctor; and a certificate from your employer certifying that he has not been able to transfer you to another position; and a medical certificate stating the expected date of delivery, if you have to stop working before giving birth. The mutual pays you an allowance of up to 60% of your last gross salary.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, Resumption of work and breastfeeding, Breastfeeding breaks and breastfeeding leave

question: I'm living with my parents, I'm working, how can I dispute the amount I have to pay?
answer: If you do not agree with the amount of the contributions mentioned on the role-extract warning you have just received, you can: 1. Apply for correction to your tax office. 2. Apply for correction to your tax office. 4. Apply for correction to your tax office. 4. Apply for correction to your tax office.

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. How's the appeal going?
answer: You must file an appeal with the registry of the competent court of appeal. You are not obliged to appeal to a lawyer. But this may be useful. To find out how much an appeal costs, see the sheet "How much does an appeal against a court decision cost?" Once your appeal is filed, you will receive a summons to a hearing. This is the introductory hearing. If you do not receive a date for a hearing, it is likely that there are still appeal fees to be paid.
topics: Justice, In court, The court decision

question: How to arrange accommodation for a minor child in the event of parental separation?
answer: 3 possibilities: 1. Parents can organize the accommodation of their minor child themselves. For homologation, the agreement has the same value as a judgment. They can do it orally, but it is better to write a written agreement. This agreement must take up the precise terms of the accommodation (places, hours of "transfer" from one parent to another, frequency, vacation periods, etc.) Parents can have this agreement approved by the family judge. For homologation, the agreement has the same value as a judgment. 2. If the parents cannot reach an agreement, they can appeal to a family mediator.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, children's accommodation, organization of the accommodation

question: Under what conditions can the contract be terminated for medical force majeure?
answer: Before going any further Since 28 November, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure has been amended. All the following conditions must be met. The worker is definitively unable to perform the function agreed in his employment contract, for reasons related to his physical or psychological state of health. For example, in case of burn-out. The worker's final incapacity for work is ascertained by: the doctor of labour or the social inspector of the General Directorate for Control of Well-being at Work; Since 28 November 2022, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure must be carried out.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: I'm a parent. Do I still have to pay alimony if my 23-year-old son is still in first grade?
answer: It is impossible to answer automatically. Everything depends on the concrete circumstances of the situation and the context surrounding these failures. The studies must be carried out normally, without any particular delay attributable to the child. In this sense, it is accepted that a young person may have had some difficulties in the course, such as being "mistaken" in his choice of studies and reoriented after a first year of studies. In case of conflict, you can contact the family judge. He decides whether the food contribution is always due or not depending on the concrete circumstances of the situation.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: Can the owner come to my house whenever he wants in Wallonia?
answer: No, your landlord can't come to your house whenever he wants. He can exercise a right of visit but this right is limited. The right of visit must allow the landlord to verify that you are fulfilling your obligations as a tenant. To visit the dwelling, the landlord must get your permission and arrange with you to organize the visit (day, time, etc.).

question: What is the role of the seizure judge?
answer: The seizure judge is competent in particular for: claims of a creditor (unilateral request) for custodial seizures the disputes the difficulties that may arise for custodial seizures and enforceable seizures the claims actions (the real owner claims ownership of an object seized from another) the disputes relating to objects and income that cannot be seized informs the interventions of the SECAL service of alimony claims (SPF Finance) He also has the mission to ensure compliance with the law by the bailiffs If he finds negligence, he opposes the King's attorney, who appreciates the disciplinary action.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts

question: I'm separating. How does the separation proceeding go before the family court?
answer: If the case cannot be explained at this first hearing, the parties will have to exchange their conclusions. They will have to exchange in writing the arguments they want to present to the judge, i.e. outside the presence of any other person, except the clerk. Once the case has been explained to the family judge, the judge will make his decision, a few days or weeks later.
topics: Family, situation of couples, Marriage, Judicial separation - urgent and provisional measures, Procedure

question: I'm a parent. My ex doesn't pay alimony. Can I seize his income?
answer: Yes, you have to go to a bailiff for this. You have to provide him with several documents: A copy of the judgment (or your divorce agreement) that provides for the payment of the maintenance contribution by your ex; an account of the sums that your ex owes you. You can recover all the unpaid maintenance contributions for the last 5 years. The sums unpaid for more than 5 years are prescribed, that is, you can no longer claim them. The seizure can also be used to recover the extraordinary expenses unpaid over the last 5 years.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: I'm renting a cottage. What kind of housing does a rental permit require in Wallonia?
answer: In order to qualify as a rental property, a rental permit must be obtained from the owner of the rental property, if there are no more than 2 dwellings and 4 tenants. A rental permit must not be obtained from the owner of the rental property, if there are no more than 2 dwellings and 4 tenants. A rental permit must not be obtained from the owner of the rental property, if there are no more than 1 dwellings and 4 tenants.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting an apartment (Wallonia), Do I need a rental permit in Wallonia

question: What is amicable debt mediation?
answer: Debt mediation is a voluntary procedure. It is at your request that a debt mediator will: analyze your financial situation; negotiate a plan for the payment of your debts with your creditors. Debt mediation should allow you to repay your creditors according to your financial capabilities, while guaranteeing you a life in accordance with human dignity. How much does it cost? This procedure is free of charge. Who can benefit from it?
topics: Money, debts, solutions to fight debt, debt mediation by mutual agreement

question: I'm confiding in a professional. How do I know if my interlocutor is bound by professional secrecy?
answer: The article of the Criminal Code that regulates professional secrecy lists certain professions: doctors, surgeons, health officers, pharmacists, midwives, etc. But it adds: "and all other persons who are depositors, by state or profession, of secrets entrusted to them". This applies to the "necessary confidants": all professionals who need to receive confidential information in order to be able to exercise their profession. Progressive jurisprudence has precisely stated who was or was not held to professional secrecy; The professional secrecy is held by lawyers, professors, ministers of worship (priests, etc.), teachers, nurses, psychologists, dentists, drivers, counselors, judges, etc.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: Does my employer have to explain the reason for my dismissal?
answer: The employer has two months to reply by registered mail. He must explain the reasons for your dismissal to you by registered mail. If he does not reply, he must pay you two weeks' remuneration. He must also prove that the reasons for the dismissal are clearly unreasonable. But if the employer had previously written the reasons for the dismissal, he must not reply to your request, and he will not be sanctioned.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, dismissal

question: I didn't sign a written employment contract, what's the risk?
answer: In principle, your employment contract is valid even if it is not written. But the written contract is legally binding for some contracts: a fixed-term employment contract (CDD); a part-time employment contract; a temporary or temporary employment contract; a replacement contract; for a clearly defined job; for a student employment contract; a contract of employment for a home worker. In these cases, even if no written contract is signed, the employment contract still exists. It is more favorable for the worker. For example: if a CDD is not signed by your employer, it is considered that you have a CDI. If a part-time employment contract is not written, you can see the black box on our website.
topics: Work, salaried work, employment contract

question: What is rent indexation in Brussels?
answer: Before we go any further, from 14 October 2022 to 13 October 2023, there are temporary regulations on rent indexation in Brussels depending on the PEB certificate. Indexation means adapting the amount of rent to the evolution of the cost of living. Indexation can be: negative (rent decreases: this is very rare) or positive (rent increases: this is what happens for most lease contracts). This adaptation is calculated according to a health index published by the SPF economy index and a legal formula.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: Who can apply for the birth allowance?
answer: Before going any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since 1 January 2019, and since 1 January 2020.For more information, see the sheet <unk> What is changing with the reform of family allowances? <unk> For information, the child's mother is applying for the birth allowance. Since 1 January 2019, the notion of recipient has been removed; therefore, the link between the birth allowance and the professional status of the parent who opens the right The child or his right to the birth allowance.

question: Can my landlord freely set the rent in Brussels?
answer: The owner and the tenant must set the rent together, by mutual agreement. However, in practice, the owners impose the amount of the rent, and by signing the contract, you accept the amount. There is no rent scale. The Brussels Capital Region proposes an indicative rent assessment grid. This grid gives a price range depending on criteria such as location and type of housing, size, insulation, number of water rents, etc. The "fair" rent is calculated as an indication that your landlord is free to set a higher or lower rent.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Fixation

question: Who decides to grant the extension for exceptional circumstances in Wallonia?
answer: If the landlord agrees to the extension, you must sign a document stating the terms of your agreement. This is not mandatory, but it is strongly advised to be able to prove that your landlord has agreed. In this document, you can mention the duration of the extension. You can ask the justice of the peace to formalize your agreement in a fixed judgment or in a verbal conciliation proceeding. For more information, see the following documents: "Can I apply for a conciliation to settle a tenancy dispute?" "Can I apply to the justice of the peace to settle a tenancy dispute?"

question: My child is disabled, is he entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Brussels?
answer: If you have a child with a disability, you will be entitled to a supplementary allowance for the disabled. • If you have a child with a disability, you will be entitled to a supplementary allowance for the disabled. • If you have a child with a disability, you will be entitled to a supplementary allowance for the disabled. • If you have a child with a disability, you will be entitled to a supplementary allowance for the disabled. • If you have a disability, you will be entitled to a supplementary allowance for the disabled.

question: I'm hosting a friend or relative. Can I host my friend in exchange for a financial contribution?
answer: Yes, you are free to conclude the agreement you wish with your friend. However, be aware of the legal consequences that this may entail. To avoid any difficulty, write down your temporary accommodation agreement in writing. Please specify that it is a temporary accommodation to help, for example. If the situation is not clear and it is submitted to a judge, he can consider that there is a verbal lease between you.
topics: Accommodation, accommodation, conditions of accommodation

question: I'm married. Can I open a bank account without my spouse's consent?
answer: Yes. You each have the right to have and manage your own bank account, savings book or personal deposit book, and to rent a safe by yourself. However, the bank has an obligation to notify your spouse of the opening of an account or a safe. But it must not specify what is in that account or in that safe.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Does the family RC insurance cover all the damage caused by my child?
answer: Private life insurance covers all property and bodily damage caused to another person by your child's fault, for example, when your child hooks a car with his bicycle, causes his friend to fall off a ski, etc. However, the insurer may refuse to cover certain acts of the insured children. In particular, it may refuse to cover damage caused by an intentional act or by a serious fault. A serious fault is a fault that is so serious that it is inexcusable.
topics: Money, Insurance, Family Insurance (RC), Covered Losses

question: How does the reintegration evaluation take place in the reintegration path procedure?
answer: Since 1 October 2022, the occupational physician invites the worker to a medical examination, to make the evaluation of reintegration. The occupational physician examines whether: the disability is temporary or permanent; the worker is able to work at another job in the same company; or at the same job but by changing it (hours, working hours, tasks, etc.). This explains the conditions that the work and the workplace must meet in order for the worker to be able to resume his work.
topics: Work, sickness - incapacity for work, resumption of adapted work during incapacity, reintegration journey

question: I make sure the landlord has a rental permit. What housing does a rental permit require in Wallonia?
answer: Rental permits are required for small single-family dwellings (less than 28m2) and group dwellings that are: rented as a main residence; or occupied by students. Exceptions: no permit is required for: collective dwellings: located in the building where the owner lives; if there are no more than 2 dwellings and 4 tenants. Co-tenants: in a single-family dwelling; if there is no more than one lease for all tenants; and a maximum of 4 adults live in the dwelling.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: I think my apartment is unhealthy. I contact the landlord. My apartment is unhealthy. Who has to do the work in Wallonia?
answer: If the landlord does not carry out the work despite the rent, you can go to the landlord and ask him to verify that the work is carried out in accordance with the minimum standards of hygiene, for example, if the landlord is obliged to do the work in accordance with the minimum standards of hygiene, for example, if the landlord is obliged to do the work in accordance with the minimum standards of hygiene, for example, if the landlord is obliged to do the work in accordance with the minimum standards of hygiene.

question: What are my employer's obligations?
answer: Your employer has several obligations. To behave properly towards you, to respect you and to act in accordance with good manners. To make you work under the agreed conditions, at the agreed position, at the agreed place and according to the agreed schedule. He must put at your disposal the instruments or materials necessary for the exercise of your function. To pay you your remuneration indicated in the contract and at the time fixed in the contract (often at the end of each month). To ensure your safety at work and to comply with his obligations of health and safety at work.
topics: Work, salaried work, employment contract

question: I'm self-employed. Can I work during my maternity leave if I'm self-employed?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The independent scheme applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For ease of use, the term "self-employed woman" is used here in a broad sense, encompassing these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the self-employed scheme.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: I am a recognised refugee. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: Your spouse or your registered partner (considered equivalent to marriage) if you are both at least 21 years old. The age is reduced to 18 if the bond exists prior to your arrival in Belgium. Your partner registered in accordance with a law (legal group), if you meet the following conditions: Prove that you have a stable and lasting relationship. Your spouse or your registered partner (considered equivalent to marriage) if you have been living together for at least 1 year before the application; or proof that you have been married for at least 2 years before the application; or proof that you are not eligible for family reunification.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: A child born in Belgium of foreign parents. I am a foreigner. What is the procedure if I have to make a declaration of granting Belgian nationality to my child born in Belgium?
answer: You must bring the following documents with you: your residence permit; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate; your child's birth certificate.

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. What is a divorce by mutual consent?
answer: It's one of the two divorce proceedings. To get a divorce by mutual consent, you both have to agree on: the fact of the divorce and; all the consequences of the divorce (house, children, debts, alimony, etc.) You don't have to explain why you're getting a divorce. You settle all the consequences of your divorce yourself in agreements. These agreements are called "pre-divorce agreements by mutual consent". It's already a real divorce agreement, which regulates all aspects of the divorce.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Can the owner require an expert to draw up the state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: In theory, the parties choose together how they will establish the state of the premises. In practice, the landlord generally enters into the lease contract how the state of the premises will be realized, for example via an expert. The tenant is free to negotiate the terms of the contract and to sign it or not. This freedom is obviously very relative, given the shortage of housing in some areas. Therefore, if the lease designates the expert xxx as being appointed by the landlord and the tenant to realize the state of the premises, it is this expert who performs the state of the premises.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), Location

question: Am I obliged to take my leave at my new job, which was paid for by my former employer (leave allowance)?
answer: Yes, you can't give up your vacation days. Besides, since your remaining vacation days were paid by your former employer (leave allowance), you can't be paid twice for those days. But if you work for your new employer during your remaining vacation days, you will be paid a second time, so you are obliged to take the vacation days covered by your employer's allowance. When you leave a job (resignation or dismissal, it doesn't matter), you receive a simple leave allowance.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, holiday allowance

question: I am a landlord. How are the fire insurance amounts calculated in Brussels?
answer: The valuation of the insured property, mainly the building and its contents, is very important. This valuation is normally the responsibility of the insured. The insured and the insurer can agree to entrust this task to an expert. Determining the correct value is a very important step.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance, renting an apartment, in Brussels

question: Am I entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo) if I am a foreigner?
answer: In order to obtain legal aid for this type of procedure, it is necessary to produce a "proof of residence" document, demonstrating: this is your residence permit (for example your residence permit); this type of procedure or procedure, carried out or at the point of your residence; for example: the right to leave the territory; the summons to the CGRA (General Commissioner for Refugees and Stateless Persons); the request for housing assistance addressed to the Council of State, etc. The Legal Aid Bureau may, however, automatically refuse to provide you with a "proof of residence" document.

question: I receive confidences as a professional. Should I report a situation of child abuse?
answer: No, you are not obliged to report a situation of abuse. You can lift the confidentiality if you meet all the following conditions. question: Am I responsible if I injure someone while working?
answer: In principle, no. But as a salaried worker, your liability is limited. You are covered by your employer: it is he who has to pay for the damage you cause to other people (third parties) or to your employer. For example, if you habitually commit a minor offense, you are liable. For example, you can be liable if you see a customer; for example, if you listen to a cassette tape every week; or if you frequently commit minor offenses, you are liable for damages.

question: Is my cohabitation lease also a main residence lease in Brussels?
answer: If you are not a tenant, you must indicate in the lease agreement that you are the tenant and that you are establishing your main residence there; for example, because your parents have signed the lease in your place. For example, the domicile in the building is sufficient to demonstrate the main residence. This is not necessary. The judge decides solely on the basis of concrete circumstances.

question: I am applying for family reunification. I am joining a foreigner authorised to stay in Belgium. Can I apply for family reunification directly in Belgium?
answer: Yes, but only in certain cases. In principle, you must submit your application for family reunification from your country of origin to the Belgian embassy or consulate competent for your place of residence. In some situations, you can submit your application directly from Belgium to the municipal administration of your place of residence. These are the following cases: Either you already have a residence permit of more than 3 months in Belgium. Either you are admitted to stay for less than 3 months in Belgium and you are in one of the following situations: you are exempt from the visa requirement; you have a visa to return to another country of the European Union; you have a visa for a minor child or a consular cohabitation.

question: I'm self-employed. How much will I receive during my "self-employed" maternity leave?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women.While using your maternity leave, you also receive a weekly lump sum allowance.This amount is regularly indexed to 11.11 from 1 December 2022, for example, the following allowances apply: Full-time maternity leave, part-time maternity leave, 4 weeks, EUR 8673, EUR 5879, EUR 757, EUR 379, for the first week.In case you have not seen the information on this website, you can also pay your maternity allowance.In addition to your maternity allowance, you are entitled to maternity allowance.

question: How does the naturalization application process go?
answer: Once your file is complete, you will receive an acknowledgement of receipt of your application. If you have addressed your application to the civil registry officer, he will forward it to the House of Representatives within 15 days of receipt. The House of Representatives will give you an acknowledgement of receipt of your application. Within 5 working days of receipt of the application by the House of Representatives, a copy of your publication will be sent for opinion to the King's Prosecutor, the Foreigners' Office and the State Security.

answer: Yes, your employer has to pay you your vacation allowance, even if you're sick, but only for the days of vacation that you haven't taken yet. question: How does the reintegration process go?
answer: Since 1 October 2022, the reintegration trajectory procedure has been amended. The new rules apply to: ongoing reintegration trajectory procedures; new reintegration trajectory procedures. As of 28 November 2022, the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure is no longer done via the reintegration trajectory procedure. There are 4 steps to the reintegration trajectory procedure: the introduction of the application; the reintegration evaluation carried out by the occupational doctor; the drafting of a reintegration plan by the employer; the decision of the worker.

question: I live somewhere other than my home. I'm going to study abroad for a while, what happens to my home?
answer: If you are going abroad to study and you are over 16 years old, you can declare your departure to your municipality. In this case, there is no time limit for your temporary absence, if it is not the duration of your practical studies, few students make this declaration and they remain domiciled at the address of their parents.

question: Am I entitled to my pension if I'm in prison?
answer: You keep your retirement pension for the first 12 months in prison. After 12 consecutive months in prison, you no longer receive your retirement pension. You also no longer redeem your retirement pension after 12 consecutive months in detention: preventive; leave of absence; prison leave; limited detention. It does not matter whether you are employed or self-employed. You can recover the pensions you did not receive during pre-trial detention, if you prove that: you have been acquitted; or there has been a decision of non-prosecution or dismissal.

question: Can I choose the language of my international protection procedure?
answer: Since 2018, the asylum procedure is called the international protection procedure. The asylum seeker must therefore submit an application for international protection. This procedure can allow him to obtain: refugee status; or subsidiary protection status. Only if you are fluent in French or Dutch. If you are not fluent in these languages, you can choose the language of the procedure.

question: I live elsewhere than at home. Can I leave my home for a few months and retain my residence ("temporary absence")?
answer: Yes, you can leave your home for several months if you intend to return, for example to travel abroad. If you leave for more than 3 months, you must declare your departure to the municipality. If you do not make this declaration, you risk being expelled from the municipality. If you have declared your departure to the municipality and filled out the appropriate form, you keep your home but the municipality indicates in the register that you are temporarily absent. So you can stay in your home even if you do not live there.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: What are my remedies if I disagree with the ONEM's decision?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were wage-earners before becoming self-employed, subject to certain conditions. Civil servants have their own system, except for the personnel service. This sheet contains only general information. For more information, please contact the National Employment Office (ONEM); or your payment body (union or CAPAC <unk> Auxiliary Fund for the payment of unemployment benefits).
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits
Social protection, unemployment

question: I'm separating. Can I claim alimony before the divorce?
answer: Yes. The family court can order one spouse to pay alimony to the other if the separation leads to a decrease in their standard of living. Even if you are not in need, the family court can set an amount of alimony by comparing your standard of living with that of your spouse. You must apply for this alimony at the time you file your application for urgent and provisional measures with the family court.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, judicial separation - urgent and provisional measures

question: Under what conditions can I regularize my stay ("request 9bis") ?
answer: If you have been in Belgium for more than one year, you can apply for a residence permit at the Belgian Embassy. If you are already in Belgium without a residence permit, you can apply from Belgium, if you invoke "exceptional circumstances". This application is called "regularization 9", "demoralization 9" or "humanitarian situation". You must submit this application to the municipality where you live. The circumstances are then reviewed by the Foreigners' Office. The Foreigners' Office decides whether your application is admissible (reasonable) and justified (well-founded).
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, humanitarian regularization (9bis)

question: Am I exempt from looking for a job if I take care of a sick relative?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. You can apply for an exemption for caring for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the first pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age. You must: be completely unemployed; and provide in an effective, permanent and regular manner: either palliative care; either care for a member of your household or family (parent or ally) up to the 2nd degree, who is seriously ill; or care for a disabled child (at least 4 points in the 1st pillar of the medical-social scale), under 21 years of age; You must apply for this exemption; You must complete a new application for the exemption from unemployment benefit.
topics: Private life, close caregivers
Social protection, unemployment, unemployment after a job

question: Am I entitled to my Grapa if I ⁇ m in prison?
answer: No. You do not receive your Grapa if you are in: detention; pre-trial detention; release permit; prison leave; limited detention. You can recover allowances that you did not receive during pre-trial detention, if you prove that: you have been acquitted; or there has been a decision of non-prosecution (end of prosecution) or dismissal. On the other hand, you keep your Grapa if you are in: parole; provisional release; electronic surveillance.
topics: Social protection, detainees and former detainees, pensions and Grapa

question: I'm renting an apartment or a house. Who has to pay for the eviction proceedings in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. If the tenant is convicted, the costs of the eviction proceedings will be borne by the evicted tenant. On the other hand, it is you, the landlord, who advances all the costs. The eviction proceedings are expensive and sometimes the tenants are insolvent. Therefore, the landlord remains with an unpaid debt.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting an apartment (Wallonia), Conflict with the tenant

question: I am applying for family reunification. I am joining a foreigner authorised to stay in Belgium. I am in my country of origin. What steps should I take to make a family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) authorised to stay in Belgium?
answer: You must apply for a family reunification visa (D visa) at the competent Belgian embassy or consulate in your country of residence. You must submit several documents: your passport; a copy of the title of residence of the family member residing in Belgium (the reunification document); proof of the family file (marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.); proof of payment of the administrative tax. For more information on the amount of my residence permit, see our "How does it cost to apply for a residence permit?"
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I have a low income, am I entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Brussels?
answer: Yes, it's called the social allowance. You're entitled to it if your annual income is less than EUR EUR EUR EUR EUR EUR 46,722,61; or between EUR EUR EUR 36,722,61 and EUR EUR EUR 36,307. You're entitled to this allowance for each child. You're entitled to it for each child.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Family allowances in Brussels, Amount and supplements of family allowances (Brussels)

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. How can I detect domestic violence?
answer: You will find below a list of warning signs. From a legal point of view, only some of them can be decisive. The behaviors described, taken in isolation, do not necessarily imply the existence of marital violence. It is the set of these and their recurrence that often reveals these situations. On the author's side: Physical aggression (punishments are possible); The following facts can theoretically be prosecuted (psychological violence is Blissable) but the evidence is often very difficult to bring; Crisis, insults, punishments and humiliations; Cancellation; Denial; Excessive jealousy; Minimization of his violent acts; Outings; and so on.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence, domestic violence

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. Do we have to go to a lawyer or a notary to write our divorce agreements?
answer: No. You are not obliged to consult a lawyer or a notary. But it is useful to inquire and have good advice. Indeed, these agreements regulate, for the rest of your life, your relations with your ex-spouse, in relation to your property, and in relation to your children. A notary or the lawyer can also play the role of conciliator. To agree on all these points, each of the concessions must be indicated. A neutral third party can help you to reach a balanced agreement. If you decide to agree to a notary or a notary, you can choose one or the other.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Is the state of affairs mandatory in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. Yes, the status of the premises is mandatory, at the entrance and at the exit. It is an imperative rule. It is also mandatory in the case of sublease. The status of the premises must be annexed to the lease contract and must be registered with the lease with the Legal Security Office of the place where the dwelling is located.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Location

question: What can I do if I don't agree with the plan?
answer: The decision on the judicial settlement plan can be appealed within 30 days of the notification of the judgment (the court registry sends the judgment by post).
topics: Money, debts, solutions to combat debt, collective settlement of debts (RCD), pending proceedings

question: I'm a self-employed worker. What should I do in the early stages of pregnancy if I'm a self-employed worker?
answer: For example, a dependent for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent for unemployment, nor for family allowances. The only steps you have to take are social security (indemnity during maternity leave, family allowances, birth allowance, etc.). These steps are to be taken with your employer and your social security mutual. For the rest, there is no specific step to be taken, except to replace your absence, etc.
topics: Social protection, Pregnancy and birth, During pregnancy

question: I'm applying for family reunification. I'm separating. Can I have my residence permit revoked?
answer: If you are a Belgian or a non-European citizen residing legally in Belgium, the Immigration Office may withdraw your right to stay in Belgium if you no longer meet the conditions for this right. This is also the case if: you divorce, or; your cohabitation ends and you divorce, or; your marriage is annulled (e.g. if you are married or cohabited), or; your legal residence ends, or; your spouse dies, or your spouse is a Belgian or European citizen; there is no common origin (e.g. if you change your name to a Belgian citizen).
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Am I entitled to a supplementary family allowance if my child's father or mother is deceased in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since 1 January 2019, and since 1 January 2020.For more information, see the sheet <unk>What is changing with the reform of family allowances?<unk> <unk>Yes. You are entitled, under certain conditions, to increased family allowances for orphans. <unk>But for this: one parent or two parents must be online; and the surviving parent must not be remarried or cohabiting (in a couple with a new partner) <unk>Unless the child becomes an orphan after 1 January 2022 (death or death from 1 January 2019); or the surviving parent no longer has any contact with the child, and does not contribute much to the family allowance. <unk>These family allowances can be paid via the Internet.
topics: Social protection, Family allowances and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born before 1 January 2020 (Wallonia)

question: I'm a salaried worker. Am I suspended from work if I breastfeed?
answer: The rules are totally different for public sector workers (public servants). Only if: your work is considered dangerous for your health and that of your baby; and you are breastfeeding; and your employer does not know how to arrange your working conditions and hours, nor to transfer you temporarily to another position. This is called prophylactic isolation, in case of high-risk work. If the occupational doctor believes that your work poses a danger to you and your baby, your employer must take preventive measures to prevent these risks.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, Resumption of work and breastfeeding, Breastfeeding breaks and breastfeeding leave

question: What charges do I have to pay as a tenant in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. You are free to decide the distribution of the costs with your landlord. See the contents of your lease. If nothing is provided for in the lease, the expenses are divided according to whether they weigh on the property (for the owner) or on the use of the housing (for the tenant).
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, expenses and insurance, Charges, Distribution of expenses

question: I receive confidences as a professional. What if I'm not sure I'm in a position to lift the confidentiality?
answer: In addition, apart from cases of non-assistance to a person in danger (which are rare), and apart from the obligation to denounce officials (which is not punishable by criminal law), it is a possibility to lift the secret under certain conditions. This is not an obligation to speak or to denounce. In other words, people subject to professional secrecy, but do not have to, can reveal certain information, under certain conditions.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this sheet applies only to salaried workers and the unemployed. For the self-employed, see our sheet "What are the conditions under which I am entitled to maternity leave if I am a self-employed person?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.
topics: Social protection, Pregnancy and birth, Maternity leave, Conditions and procedures for entitlement to maternity leave

question: What procedure does the CPAS have to follow to recover the RIS?
answer: The CPAS must then notify you of a decision in writing (notification). This decision must include the following elements: the finding that undue amounts have been paid; the total amount of what has been unfairly paid and the method of calculation; the content and references of the legal provisions in violation of which the payments have been made; the statute of limitations taken into account; the possibility for the CPAS to waive the recovery of unfairly paid amounts, and the procedure to be followed to ask the CPAS to waive; the possibility of submitting a proposal for reimbursement.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), if the RIS has to be reimbursed

question: Can the employer punish me if I have lost my company mobile phone?
answer: It depends on the circumstances in which you lost your GSM. The rules are the same if you have your GSM stolen, or your company laptop stolen. In principle, you are not responsible for the damage you cause to your employer in the course of your work. If you lose your GSM is part of this damage because you have committed a serious or intentional fault (specifically the "lose" or "lose" if you were negligent). But be careful, the employer covers you only for your minor and occasional mistakes.
topics: Work, salaried work, remuneration and benefits in kind, GSM

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. Do I have to hire a lawyer?
answer: No, you are not obliged to hire a lawyer, except: when the law requires you to do so - it is quite rare - (for example, minors must be represented by a lawyer); when the judge requires you to do so: he can do so if he believes that the context makes the discussion impossible. However, in some proceedings, it is advisable to get insurance from a lawyer: either because the case is complicated at the legal level; or because the procedure is special; or because you do not have to go in person to the hearings; or because the issue of the dispute (the amount of the conflict) is important.
topics: Justice, In court, Introduction of the application

question: Can I ask for a reduction in rent or terminate the lease if the landlord is doing major work in Wallonia?
answer: It depends on the type of work and its extent. You have to support urgent work that lasts up to 40 days without compensation. Since September 2018, you also have to support energy-saving work for a maximum of 40 days without compensation. Energy-saving work is work that gives you the right to a premium from the Walloon Region: insulation work on roofs, walls or floors, replacement of the boiler or water heater by a high-performance system, work to comply with housing health standards, and an energy audit.
topics: Housing, Rental in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Repairs, maintenance and works, Works and improvements: consequences

question: My legal partner has died, am I entitled to a survivor's pension?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for wage earners, self-employed workers and civil servants.This sheet contains only general information.For any details or additional questions, please contact: The Federal Pension Service (SFP) for wage earners and civil servants, the National Institute of Social Insurance for Self-Employed Workers (INTIAS).
topics: Social Protection, Pensions and Grapa, Survival Pension

question: What is an Individualized Social Integration Project (PIIS) (RIS)?
answer: Before going any further, the PIIS is mandatory for all new beneficiaries of social integration income (RIS) since 1 November 2016. The individual social integration project (PIIS) is a "contract" that the CPAS concludes with the aid applicant. Specifically, it is an agreement that the aid applicant and the CPAS sign. The aid applicant undertakes to make certain efforts or steps to improve his situation, for example, to follow a training, find housing, repay his debts, perform community service, etc. The CPAS undertakes to help him and to accompany him with certain measures. For example, help to find housing, work, etc.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social integration income (RIS), signing an "individualized social integration project" (PIIS)

question: I have been summoned to court. What is the joint request (formerly called voluntary appearance)?
answer: The parties agree to ask the judge to settle their conflict (without necessarily agreeing on the solution to their conflict), and to appear together before him. Specifically, the parties must write a joint petition, in which they explain to the court and specify each their position. The petition must be dated and signed by the two parties. The petition can be: either filed in the registry in your own hands; addressed to the registry by registered mail; filed on e-deposit.
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Summoning to hearing

question: The parents are married together. What steps should I take to be recognized as the father of my child if we are married?
answer: There is no special procedure to be followed. If you are married when your wife gives birth, you are automatically considered the legal father of that child. This is called the legal presumption of paternity. You, or your wife, or the maternity hospital, must declare the birth of your child to the municipality of the place of delivery, within 15 days of the birth. Normally, the hospital (or midwife) sends a birth certificate to the municipality.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

question: Can we terminate the CDD during the first half of the contract?
answer: Only under certain conditions. The employer and the worker can terminate the CDD before the due date, without having to pay severance pay, under certain conditions: the CDD has been concluded from 1 January 2014; and the notice is notified in accordance with the rules that apply to a CDI. For more information, see our CDDs. What is the duration of my notice of termination?
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, termination of the fixed-term contract (CDD)

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. Can I have the perpetrator of domestic violence evicted from the family home?
answer: Yes, but only if you have an enforceable title (judgment, notarial deed, etc.) that grants you the exclusive ownership of the family dwelling. If you are the sole owner or tenant of the dwelling, the eviction still requires proceedings before the justice of the peace. If you are married or cohabiting legally, there is a preferential allocation of the dwelling in favor of the victim of marital violence.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: Who can apply for family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: To be entitled to family allowances in the Walloon system, the child must: be domiciled in Wallonia (i.e. not in Flanders, Brussels or the German-speaking Community), or actually reside there; and be Belgian, or a foreigner in legal residence, or be born to two stateless parents. If at least one of the parents is European, the child does not have to meet these conditions. But most of the time it is the parents who ask for them.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born from 1 January 2020 (Wallonia), Applying for and receiving family allowances (Wallonia)

question: Am I entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo) if I am disabled?
answer: Yes in theory, if you receive income replacement allowances (ARR) for disabled people. In principle, if you receive ARRs, you are presumed to have insufficient financial resources and you are entitled to legal aid. However, the Legal Aid Bureau (LAB) can refuse you legal aid if it proves that you have enough "resources" or "means of subsistence" (this term covers your income, those of your household members, your savings, third-party assistance, your cars, buildings, etc.). In practice, the BAJ checks all your income and that of your household, as well as other "means of subsistence". For the latter, the LAB applies the maximum income thresholds explained in the following sheets:
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Situations related to a social category

question: Can I accept a part-time job of half a day a week?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the Social Inspectorate of the ONSS (National Office of Social Security) for the social security of part-time workers; your trade union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. No, unless a royal decree or a collective agreement (CCT) authorizes it; for your sector of activity or for the company that offers you part-time work.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights at work as a part-time worker

question: Can the CPAS help me create a rental guarantee in Brussels?
answer: Yes. The purpose of the CPAS is in particular to help people who are unable to provide a rental guarantee (right to housing). The social worker assesses whether you are entitled to help from the CPAS to provide your rental guarantee. He checks different criteria by carrying out a social survey.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Aid to create a rental guarantee, Aid from the CPAS

question: What are the penalties if I work undercover?
answer: You risk two types of penalties. You risk an administrative penalty if you work undercover and if you know that you work undercover. This administrative penalty is a fine of EUR 10 to EUR 100 (to be multiplied by 8 additional decimals). To be sanctioned, you must meet the following conditions: you work in a subordinate relationship with the contractual employer: he has authority over you, he gives you orders to do the work; you know that the work is not declared; the employer has not made an immediate declaration of employment (DEMNA); a lawsuit has been filed against the employer for this undeclared work.
topics: Work, salaried work, the risks of undeclared work (<unk> in the dark <unk>)

question: Can I ask to be heard before the CPAS makes a decision on the RIS?
answer: Yes. You can ask to be heard by the Social Action Council of the CPAS before it makes a decision. The CPAS must inform you clearly and in writing of this possibility of being heard during the examination of your application. It must mention it in the acknowledgement of receipt of your application. If you ask for an hearing, the Council is obliged to hear you. It is interesting for you to be heard by the Council, to explain your situation. An hearing is sometimes a quick way to unblock a situation.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social integration income (RIS)

question: I have a procedure in Dutch, what should I do?
answer: Your address does not determine the jurisdiction of the legal aid office (BAJ). You can apply to any BAJ you want. But this BAJ can only appoint lawyers from the same judicial district. You have two possibilities. You can apply for an appointment to the nearest Dutch-speaking BAJ. This BAJ can appoint a Dutch-speaking lawyer who is registered in the judicial district of this BAJ. You can apply for an appointment in the district where your proceedings take place (that of the court seized). The advantage is that your lawyer knows the BAJ and the sensitivity of the judges in that district where the proceedings take place.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro deo), Steps to obtain legal aid (ex pro deo)

question: Can my landlord increase the rent after work in the cot in Wallonia?
answer: For example: a clause in the lease agreement may allow the landlord to impose a rent increase even if he has done work in the cottage. For example: a clause in the lease agreement may allow the landlord to do work to install a kitchen, provide for a reduction in the rent; during the work, then provide for an increase in the rent because the comfort of the cottage has increased.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Loyer, charges and insurance, Loyer

question: I'm separating. Can I ask the judge to take urgent and provisional measures regarding our property?
answer: Yes. The family judge can take measures concerning, for example: the objects you have in common (a car, furniture, a computer, a pet, etc.): allowing one to keep the car (for his professional activity for example), prohibiting the sale of these goods, moving them or using them, etc. The repayment of common debts, for example the mortgage of the family home. At this stage, the measures are always temporary. The distribution of property and debts is final only at the end of a possible divorce.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, judicial separation - urgent and provisional measures

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I become a legal cohabitant. In what cases can the registrar refuse to register a declaration of legal cohabitation?
answer: This file has been updated more than a year ago. The civil status officer checks two things: Do you meet the legal conditions to be able to make a declaration of legal cohabitation? Are you not married or not bound by another legal cohabitation? Are you able to contract? Is it not a legal cohabitation of compulsion or a legal cohabitation of compulsion? We are talking about a legal cohabitation of compulsion if the sole purpose of the cohabitation is to obtain a registration of money for one of the parties.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Can my rent be indexed in Brussels?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. Your landlord has the right to ask to index the rent if your lease: is written; and does not prohibit it; and is registered (new since October 14, 2022); and the landlord has communicated to the tenant the PEB certificate (new since October 14, 2022); and the PEB certificate is A, B, C, D or E (only from October 14, 2022 to October 13, 2023)?" Attention: If your lease is oral, your landlord cannot index the rent.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I'm a professional and I'm aware of a situation of domestic violence.
answer: To report acts of domestic violence, you must contact the competent public prosecutor's office for your judicial district. You can find which King's Prosecutor (PR) is competent here. Each public prosecutor's office appoints a "reference magistrate". This is the King's prosecutor competent to deal with cases of domestic violence. You report domestic violence to this magistrate. When reporting, you only transmit the information strictly necessary to the PR so that he can react appropriately.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: Can my administrator forbid me to drive?
answer: In principle, no. If you are no longer able to drive, you must spontaneously surrender your driver's license to the municipality. To recover it, you must pass a driving test. If you are domiciled in the Walloon Region, contact the Department of Driving Ability (DAC). If you are domiciled in Brussels or the Flemish Region, contact the Centre for Driving Ability and Vehicle Adaptation (CARA). If you do not surrender your license, your property or person administrator cannot force you to do so.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), The task of the administrator of property and or of the person

question: Can the landlord terminate the unofficial cohabitation lease before it expires in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. If your lease is also a main residence lease: The landlord can terminate the lease under certain conditions: to occupy the dwelling <unk> personally <unk> or to do important work; or by paying an indemnity. For more information, see the section <unk> End and termination of the main residence lease <unk>. If your lease is not a main residence lease: You must check whether the lease provides that the landlord can terminate the lease before the due date or not.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Duration and end of co-location, Duration and end of the unofficial co-location lease (Co-location Brussels)

question: Can my landlord take the initiative to put my furniture on the street in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet deals with eviction in the context of a lease. When a tenant conflict arises, the judge decides whether the eviction is appropriate. There are other possibilities for punishing the tenant. For example, sentencing him to pay the landlord's rent, damages, etc. For eviction is common, but it is not automatic. The judge cannot act "in the wild". To evict a tenant, he must be authorized by a court. Even if he does not physically get a lease, he can file a complaint with the landlord.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: Is a written lease mandatory in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, any lease for the main residence entered into from 15 June 2007 must be in writing. This written agreement must contain certain information: 1. The exact identity of the tenant (s) and the landlord (s) (i.e. their name, first two surnames, address, place and date of birth); 2. The date of commencement of the lease, i.e. the date on which the dwelling is made available (which is often different from the date on which the contract was signed); 3. The designation of the rented premises; 4. The amount of the lease; 4. If the lease is entered into by a company (e.g. a real estate agency), the landlord must indicate its identity and its business number.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: What are the conditions for family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) on a limited stay?
answer: 1) The grouping must have: sufficient housing to receive the family member or members. This is proven by: a registered lease agreement or a title deed. Health insurance covering the risks in Belgium for himself and his family members. This can be proven by: a nominative certificate from the mutual confirming the possibility of affiliating the family members as soon as they arrive on Belgian territory; or a private insurance contract signed by a stable, regular and sufficient means of subsistence; to provide for his own and his family's needs, and to avoid becoming a burden for the public.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: I'm European. I'm European. Am I entitled to urgent medical care?
answer: Before going any further, the right to social integration income (RIS) and social assistance for European citizens depends on several elements: the right of residence: E-card, RIS card, annex 19, annex 19ter, annex 20, annex 21 or annex 35.The date on which the residence permit was issued.Yes, the reason for residence: European worker (and his family members), European jobseeker (and his family member), other categories of European citizens (student, holder of a card A. It is always necessary to distinguish between the right to RIS and the right to social assistance); If you are a member of a family, you are entitled to social assistance.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and CPAS aids

question: Can the owner require an expert to draw up the condition of the premises in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. In theory, the parties choose together how they will establish the state of the premises. In practice, the landlord usually enters in the lease how the state of the premises will be realized, for example via an expert. The tenant is free to negotiate the terms of the contract and to sign it or not. This freedom is obviously very relative, given the shortage of housing in some areas.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Location

question: What kind of decisions are within the parental authority?
answer: Parental authority allows parents to make important decisions about their children. It stems from the bond of parentage. These decisions must always be made in the best interests of the children. Parental authority has three aspects: decisions related to the child's person: decisions related to the physical custody of the child: decisions related to the child's place of residence; decisions related to everyday life (hygiene rules, waking and going to bed times, urgent decisions related to health, etc.); decisions related to the adoption of the child; decisions related to the inheritance of the child; decisions related to the adoption of the child.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: What is the role of the CPAS in the event of expulsion in Wallonia?
answer: The CPAS is notified only if: the eviction concerns a building rented through a main residence lease (or a commercial lease) and this building is the tenant's home or residence. The objective is to enable the CPAS to help the tenant in the procedures he will undertake (justice; amicable settlement, relocation, financial assistance, etc.).
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: I'm MENA. When is "MENA" recognized?
answer: You are not accompanied by a person exercising parental authority or guardianship over you (unless you are married): even if you are accompanied by a member of your family who has no rights over you (in the sense of Belgian law, i.e. who has no parental or legal guardianship over you), such as an aunt who was your "guardian" in your family without anything being regulated at the legal level, you are not considered as "accompanied by a member of a European country".
topics: Foreigners, foreign minors

question: I am a non-European student. Will my student residence permit be withdrawn if I apply for financial aid from the CPAS?
answer: To find out if you are entitled to social integration aid and social assistance, you must meet certain conditions (related to your willingness to work, your resources, your state of need or your actual residence in Belgium). To know these conditions, see the sheets <unk> Aide du CPAS <unk> in <unk> Social assistance <unk> Yes to find out if you are entitled to help from the CPAS, you need to know your risk status. There is a card, but it is not an automatic one of the conditions of the student stay; it is to have sufficient resources (670 per month for the academic year 2020-2021). You must meet the conditions imposed on your stay for the duration of your studies, to renew your application for social assistance.

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). What is the amount of my maternity allowance if I am an employee?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this sheet applies only to salaried workers and the unemployed.For the self-employed, see our sheet "How much will I receive during my independent maternity leave?"For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.For civil servants, please check with your personnel department.It is not your employer who pays you during maternity leave.For the duration of your maternity leave, you receive a certain amount of gross allowance.For civil servants, the gross allowance is a percentage of your actual salary.For civil servants, the gross allowance is calculated on the basis of your unemployment allowance.For civil servants, the unemployment allowance is calculated on the basis of your unemployment allowance.

question: I'm getting married. Are we obliged to make a marriage contract?
answer: No, you are not obliged to make a marriage contract. You have a choice. In any case, you must respect the basic rules of marriage (primary regime): the duty of cohabitation, the duty of fidelity, the duty of relief, etc. These rules apply to all married couples, whether you make a marriage contract or not. Other rules are not the same for all married couples (secondary regime of community or separation). These rules concern your marital property. If you do not make a marriage contract before a notary, you are corresponding under the legal regime of community of property.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I am self-employed. How much will I receive during my "self-employed" maternity leave?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women.While using your maternity leave, you also receive a weekly lump sum allowance.This amount is regularly indexed to 11.11 from 1 December 2022, for example, the following allowances apply: Full-time maternity leave, part-time maternity leave, 4 weeks, EUR 8673, EUR 5879, EUR 757, EUR 379, for the first week.In case you have not seen the information on this website, you can also pay your maternity allowance.In addition to your maternity allowance, you are entitled to maternity allowance.
topics: Work, work and parenting, "independent" maternity leave

question: If I don ⁇ t pay my consumer credit, can we take back my car?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet relates to car credit and the possibility of taking back the financed car. The lender who gave you the car credit can take back the financed car from you? Yes. Most consumer loans contain a retention of ownership clause. The retention of ownership clause transfers ownership of the financed object when the credit is repaid. In case of default, the lender can recover, at any time, the car you bought through the credit.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a consumer credit

question: What are the conditions for family reunification with a recognised refugee?
answer: The family member who wants to join the recognised refugee (or beneficiary of subsidiary protection) in Belgium must attach to his file: a medical certificate certifying that he does not suffer from a disease that could endanger public health; an extract from the criminal record if he is over 18 years old; proof of family status; proof of refugee status or beneficiary of subsidiary protection from abroad.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: When can a dismissal be considered an abuse of rights?
answer: Since 1 January 2018, the rules on unfair dismissal no longer apply, following a judgment of the Constitutional Court. Since 1 January 2018, for all workers in the private sector, the rules on the motivation for dismissal and the prohibition of unreasonable dismissal (provided for by the collective agreement (CCT) no. 109°) have been applied. Jurisprudence and doctrine have developed the notion of the right to dismiss, applying the general theory; of the abuse of law. This principle is simple: an employer cannot abuse his right to dismiss a dismissed worker. But in practice, it is more complex, because the abuse of union law can manifest itself in different ways:

question: Can I choose my family allowance fund?
answer: In principle, yes, but it depends on where you and your child are domiciled. In Wallonia, from 1 January 2021, all parents can choose their family allowance fund. Before 1 January 2021, all parents can choose their family allowance fund. Before 1 January 2019, all parents can choose their family allowance fund. Before 1 January 2019, all parents can choose their family allowance fund. Before 1 January 2019, all parents can choose their family allowance fund.

question: How does the doctor assess my disability for the allowance for elderly care in Brussels?
answer: Your disability is assessed by a doctor at the Centre for the Assessment of Autonomy and Disability. He assesses your disability based on several criteria. The disability assessment form that you filled in when you submitted your application for the elderly assistance allowance (APA). The medical questionnaire completed by your doctor, the evaluation of the disability and disability assessment centre. The doctor at the Centre for the Assessment of Autonomy and Disability can call you for a medical examination. He asks you questions. During the medical examination it is important to prepare for the medical examination.

question: I'm a parent. I've heard of the Renard method. What is it?
answer: The Renard method is a method for calculating the amount of food contribution that has been developed by a sociologist and is sometimes used by judges. This method makes it possible to make the calculation of food contributions more objective. But judges are not obliged to follow this method. Indeed, it is not imposed by law. Each judge is free to use it or not. The Renard method proposes to fix the calculation of food contribution, based on a computer program, in two stages: assessing the cost of the child, and then distributing the financing of this cost among the parents.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: If the CPAS is convicted and appeals, will they pay me the aid?
answer: It depends. If the judgment is "enforceable by provision" (provisional enforcement), the CPAS has to pay you the aid. The CPAS has to enforce the judgment and pay you the aid to which it was sentenced, even if it appeals. It has to pay you the aid during the entire appeal procedure. In principle, all conflicting judgments are enforceable by provision, unless the judge has decided otherwise in his judgment.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, challenging the decision of the CPAS, appeal to the labour court

question: Am I entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo) if I am over-indebted?
answer: If you are a debtor, the legal aid office may refuse you legal aid if it proves that you have sufficient "resources" (this includes your income, the income of your household members, your savings, the income of third parties, your car, your property, etc.) In practice, the legal aid office can automatically deduct all your income and the income of your household, as well as your other income.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro Deo), Conditions for legal aid (ex pro Deo), Situations related to a social category
