question: I ⁇ m a parent. What is SECAL?
answer: The Service of alimony claims (SECAL) is a service that depends on the SPF Finance. It has two main missions: to recover unpaid alimony claims (alimony contributions and alimony pensions) and to pay advances on future alimony claims. Special conditions apply to these two types of claims. To know the conditions related to the different types of claims, consult the website of the SPF Finance, see the scheme attached in the "documents-type" of this sheet or see the sheets in this section. Once the SECAL is in charge of the recovery of alimony claims, you can no longer begin to try to recover them.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: I'm separating, can I continue to support my ex for the mutual?
answer: For example, a dependent for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent for disability benefits, or for unemployment only; if you are employed, and under certain conditions you must, in principle, be dependent on your spouse or ex-spouse. If you are divorced, you can no longer be dependent on your spouse. There is a possibility for divorced couples to be legally separated, but not legally separated, but in some cases you are considered a dependent by your spouse and ex-spouse.
topics: Social protection, Health and illness, Reimbursement of health care

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am a foreigner. Can my child's recognition be revoked?
answer: Yes. The King's Prosecutor may ask the family judge to annul the recognition of paternity. The family judge annuls the recognition of paternity if he considers that it is a fraudulent recognition. In this case, the judge considers that the recognition is made solely for the purpose of obtaining a right of residence for the father, mother or child. If you have obtained a right of residence by the fraudulent recognition of your child, it may be withdrawn from you.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: The child's father and mother live together. Who can make decisions about the child if the parents live together?
answer: Parents who live together make all the decisions that concern their minor child together. This is the principle of joint parental authority: the parents decide together. Each parent must ask for the consent of the other parent before making a decision about the child (enrollment in school, authorization for an operation, placement of a sum of money from the child, etc.). This rule also applies if the parents live separately. At any time, the parents must try to agree on the decisions to be made alone. If they do not agree, they can appeal to a mediator to help them find an agreement; or to a judge to decide in their place, in the interest of the child.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: I'm a parent. My ex hasn't paid alimony contributions set by a judgment for ten years. Can I still claim them?
answer: Yes, in part. It is not possible to go back 10 years. However, you have to be very careful in the calculation of the 5 years. Indeed, some events interrupt or suspend the statute of limitations. This is the case for example if your ex has signed an acknowledgement of debts, if you have made a subpoena to recover the arrears, if a bailiff has withdrawn his fee, etc.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: Can my rent be indexed if my lease is not registered in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further From 14 October 2022 to 13 October 2013, there are temporary rules on rent indexation in Brussels depending on the PEB certificate. No. Your landlord cannot index the rent if the lease is not registered (new since 14 October 2022). This applies to the lease that has an index date (annual index date that follows the start date of the lease) after 14 October. If your landlord has registered the rent before 14 October 2022, it is valid. This condition of registration does not apply to index your lease.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I live somewhere other than my home. I live in a roommate, do I have to live there?
answer: Yes, you have to live at the address of your accommodation. But if the municipality finds that you live mainly on its territory, it can change your main residence. So in practice, you have to declare this change to the municipality. In practice, if you do not live at the address of your accommodation, the municipality has little means to ascertain your default. In this case, you remain at the previous address. But if the municipality finds that you live mainly on its territory, it can change your main residence. In practice, it calls you to hear your position.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I become a legal cohabitant. Can I come to Belgium to make a declaration of legal cohabitation?
answer: Yes. If you live abroad, you can join a Belgian or a foreigner who has a legal residence permit in Belgium, to make a declaration of legal cohabitation with him. To do this, you must apply for a C visa at the Belgian embassy or consulate in your country of origin. This corresponds to a request for a short stay (maximum of 3 months). Your file must contain the following documents: a valid national passport; proof that your relationship is lasting (e.g. contacts by mail, telephone, dating, etc.); a document that proves that you and your partner have been living together for a maximum of 21 months; a copy of your residence permit in Belgium; proof that you already have a legal residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: Can my furniture be seized for my roommate's debts?
answer: Yes, if your roommate is domiciled in the dwelling. If your roommate is domiciled at the address of the dwelling, the bailiff may come and seize some of the furniture in it. These are presumed to belong to the debtor (your roommate). In order for your furniture not to be seized, you must prove that they belong to you. You can present to the bailiff, for example: an invoice in your name; a will; bank statements; an inventory made by a notary; etc.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of movable property

question: I do not agree with the state of affairs, what to do in Brussels?
answer: This means that it must be drawn up by the two parties (or their respective representatives) or by the expert chosen by mutual agreement. If the state of the premises is drawn up by you and your landlord (or your respective representatives): If you do not agree, especially if you do not sign! To propose changes and if your landlord refuses, ask him to appoint an expert whom you jointly choose. Each of the parties can normally make comments on the state of the premises before signing it. If the landlord refuses, do not sign and then send comments to the expert.

question: Who decides to grant the extension for exceptional circumstances in Brussels?
answer: If the landlord accepts the extension, you can sign a written document stating the terms of your agreement and especially the duration of the extension. This is not mandatory but it is strongly advised to be able to prove that your landlord has signed his agreement. In this case, you can mention the duration of the extension. For more information, see the question <unk> Under what conditions can I get an extension? <unk> If your landlord refuses your request, go to the justice of the peace.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, Extension of the lease in case of exceptional circumstances

question: Can I refuse medical or paramedical care?
answer: Yes, you can always: refuse care; stop care, in other words, withdraw your consent at any time. The caregiver must respect your choice. He can't perform an intervention or maintain treatment if you object to it. You can refuse orally, that's enough. But you (or the caregiver) can ask for it to be written in your patient file. In case of refusal of care, the caregiver must ensure the continuity of care. For example, he must transfer you to another care institution or to a service more suited to your needs; The caregiver can for example offer you palliative care or another type of treatment.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, Patient's rights and medical records

question: Is my cohabitation lease also a main residence lease in Wallonia?
answer: This is the case if: you, your roommates actually live in the dwelling; it is your main dwelling; it is you, your roommates who signed the lease. If this is not the case (e.g. because your parents signed the lease for you), it must be indicated in the lease that you are the occupant and that you rent your main dwelling there. The domicile in the dwelling is often sufficient to demonstrate the main dwelling. However, this is not necessary.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Location (Wallonia)

question: A child born in Belgium who is or is at risk of being stateless. Does my child born in Belgium who is or is at risk of being stateless automatically receive Belgian nationality?
answer: Yes. Your child can automatically receive Belgian nationality in the following cases: he was born in Belgium and would be stateless if he did not receive Belgian nationality; except if he can receive another nationality by making an administrative procedure with the consulate of the country of which you are a national; he is stateless and born in Belgium before December 2006; he is a newborn who was found in Belgium; he is presumed to have been born in Belgium.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, minors (under 18 years of age)

question: I'm a salaried worker. I'm pregnant, can I take time off from work to see the gynecologist?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for salaried workers, self-employed workers and civil servants. Yes, under certain conditions. During your pregnancy, you must regularly consult doctors and undergo examinations. You may, under certain conditions, be absent from work for this care, without reducing your remuneration. You must meet all these conditions: medical examinations cannot take place at any time other than during working hours; you must notify your employer in advance; if a work regulation or a collective agreement (CCT) provides for this, or if your employer requests it, you must give him a medical certificate.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, During pregnancy

question: Can I get back this year the vacation days I didn't take last year?
answer: But before we go any further, this sheet only covers holidays for salaried workers. The rules are not the same for public sector workers (civil servants) or for self-employed workers. But normally, you have to take your holidays in the months following the fiscal year. But you can't take your holidays in the following year. But if you don't take your holidays at the end of the year, they're all lost. But you can't take your holidays in the following year.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, annual leave

question: What if you disagree with a judge's decision to put you under observation?
answer: If you do not agree with the decision of the justice of the peace, you can appeal the decision, whether you are: the sick person, or; the one who requested the measure, To appeal, you must file a request with the family court. The deadline for filing this request is 15 days from the notification of the judgment, i.e. from its sending by registered mail by the justice of the peace.
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: I'm a tenant. Do I have to take out fire insurance if I'm a tenant in Wallonia?
answer: If you don't have fire insurance when your lease requires it, your landlord can ask the judge to force you to take out fire insurance. He could also ask for a breach of the lease for negligence. If you fail to pay your lease after September 1, 2018: Fire insurance is a legal obligation. As a tenant, you must pay for fire insurance; You must pay for fire insurance.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance

question: I'm renting a cottage. I'm the owner. Should I install fire detectors in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. All housing in Wallonia must be equipped with a fire detector by their owner. You assume the purchase and installation. The tenant assumes the maintenance and change of batteries. He must check if the detector is working and warn you if it is not. The detector must be of the optical type and be certified by an accredited body. The number of detectors to be placed depends on the living area: Below 80 m2, only one detector is mandatory; Above 80 m2, 2 detectors are mandatory. If the rented housing is a house, this rule applies to each floor.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia)

question: I want to get a divorce because of irreparable separation. How do I set the amount of alimony?
answer: Different criteria are taken into account in determining the amount of alimony to be paid by an ex-spouse after divorce due to irremediable disunity: The amount of alimony cannot exceed one-third of the income of the spouse who has to pay it; The amount of alimony must cover at least the state of need of the applicant spouse, i.e. at least his basic needs; The state of need of the applicant spouse must be involuntary.
topics: Family, maintenance obligations, alimony (between spouses or ex-spouses), alimony in divorce proceedings, divorce proceedings on the grounds of irreparable separation

question: I'm staying with a friend. My friend has his reference address at my house, can we seize my furniture?
answer: In principle, no. The person who has a reference address with you cannot live with you. Indeed, the purpose of the reference address is to allow a person who no longer has a residence in Belgium to maintain an administrative existence and social rights. For more information, see the section "Reference address". Your furniture cannot be seized to pay his debts since, logically, they do not belong to your friend justice But, if your friend has stored furniture with you, the creditor can ask the judge for permission to seize your home. This hypothesis is very rare.
topics: Housing, accommodation, accommodation and debts

question: I have a low income, am I entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Wallonia?
answer: You are entitled to this social supplement if: your gross annual income is less than EUR 31,603.68; or your gross annual income is between EUR 31.603.68 and EUR 51.000; or you benefit from the increased intervention (statutory BIM). The supplement will be granted to you for all the months for which you benefit from the increased intervention, regardless of your gross annual income. You are entitled to this social supplement for each child. If you are disabled (loss of earning capacity), and if your income is less than EUR 41.603.68 or EUR 31.49 (for the first quarter), your supplement will be increased.
topics: Social protection, Family allowances and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born from 1 January 2020 (Wallonia), Amount and supplements of family allowances (Wallonia)

question: The apartment I'm renting has been sold. Can the new owner put me out in Wallonia?
answer: The answer varies depending on whether or not your lease has a "certain date" before the date of the sale. The new landlord has to comply with all his rights and obligations. So the lease has a certain date. You do not have to sign a new lease because the old lease continues to apply. This rule applies even if your lease provides for eviction in the event of the sale of the lease. The new landlord can terminate the lease in the same way as the old lease.

question: I'm self-employed: how much do I get paid if I get sick?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only some general information. For more information, go to your mutual. See also the INASTI website. Be careful of two things. The rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. The notion of a dependent person is not the same in the different social security schemes. For example, a dependent person for disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent person for disability benefits, nor for unemployment.

question: What voluntary activities are allowed for the unemployed?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, except if they were employed before becoming self-employed, subject to certain conditions. Civil servants have their own regime, except for:

question: Can a minor child make decisions on his own?
answer: In principle, no, but there are exceptions. In principle, parents make all decisions for their child up to the age of 18. This is called parental authority. The minor child is considered "incapable" legally. For example, if a minor signs a credit, he or his parents could ask for its cancellation. The minor can make certain decisions that concern him personally, depending on: his age (he must have sufficient discernment, i.e. the ability to understand what he is doing and the consequences of his decisions); and the subject of the act (for example, the high price of housing when he buys it).
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: I'm a close aide: am I entitled to help from the CPAS?
answer: Yes. You can apply for social integration income (RIS) or social assistance if you meet the conditions. To be entitled to RIS, you must "be willing to work". The CPAS can therefore ask you to prove that you are actively looking for a job. If you do not do so, the CPAS can withdraw your RIS. To be entitled to financial social assistance, the CPAS can ask you to actively seek work. This condition is optional: the CPAS is not obliged to ask you to prove your willingness to work.
topics: Private life, close caregivers

question: What are the conditions for family reunification with a Belgian?
answer: In this case, the Belgian adult must meet the following conditions: Have stable, regular and sufficient means of subsistence to provide for his own needs and those of his family card, and to avoid them becoming a burden on the public authorities; Have sufficient means of subsistence to allow him to receive the members of his family (property, lease, title, birth certificate, etc.); Have a sickness certificate that covers the risks for himself and for the members of his family.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: What if I need urgent care but I can't go to the CPAS beforehand?
answer: In some cases, the illness is very serious or the illness needs to be treated quickly. You can go directly to the hospital or the doctor, without first going through the CPAS. You must then notify the healthcare institution (hospital or doctor) as soon as possible of your irregular stay and your inability to pay the bill. The CPAS decision can have a retroactive effect for up to 60 days. As soon as the hospital is informed of your irregular stay and your state of need, it must immediately notify the CPAS that it is competent.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and health care, Urgent medical help

question: What happens if I stay in the apartment after the expiry of the short-term lease in Brussels?
answer: Before you go any further, it's not because you've signed a short-term lease that you're still in a short-term lease! If you haven't signed a short-term lease, there are two possibilities. Before you terminate the lease, you need to check what term of the lease you're in. For more information, see the question: Is my lease a short-term lease? If you've signed a renewal, see the question: Can you sign multiple short-term leases? If you haven't signed a short-term lease, there are two possibilities.

question: In what cases can the CPAS recover social assistance from my parents?
answer: If you are an adult and no longer receiving family allowances, the CPAS cannot recover the aid from your parents. To "receive family allowances" does not mean that you should receive it from your parents. For more information, see the section "Family allowances". If you are a minor or still receiving family allowances, if you are an adult and no longer receiving family allowances, the CPAS cannot recover the aid from your parents.

question: I ⁇ m quitting my job in the middle of the year, am I entitled to the holiday allowance?
answer: It depends on whether you're a worker or an employee. 1. If you're a worker, the vacation fund pays you your vacation allowance between May 2 and June 30 of the following year (so in May-June 2023 you'll be able to pay a proportionate portion of that amount). 2. If you're an employee, your employer pays you your vacation allowance.

question: I'm renting an apartment or a house. Can I kick my tenant out in Wallonia?
answer: This file was updated more than a year ago. You can ask a bailiff to put your tenant out of the house only if you have an enforceable title! This enforceable title is usually a judgment that pronounces the termination of the lease and the eviction of the tenant. You can not evict your tenant by changing wildly, even if he no longer pays or if the notice you gave him has expired. Indeed, no one can do justice to himself! The justice of the peace appreciates whether eviction is the most relevant solution.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting an apartment (Wallonia), Conflict with the tenant

question: Who controls the operation of the guardianship?
answer: There are two "controllers": the surrogate guardian and the justice of the peace. Every time the justice of the peace appoints a guardian, he also appoints a surrogate guardian. His main role is to oversee that the guardian performs his duties well. The guardian must collaborate with the surrogate guardian, to enable him to carry out this control mission. The justice of the peace of the child's home controls the functioning of the guardianship.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, guardianship, supervision by the guardian

question: I'm starting life. I'm living in a roommate. I'm working. How can I dispute the amount I have to pay?

Apply to the Advocate General. 4. You can file a complaint with the Advocate General in charge of the establishment of income taxes.

question: Am I entitled to maternity, paternity and parental leave if I work part-time?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the Social Inspectorate of the ONSS (National Office of Social Security) for the social security of part-time workers; your union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. Yes, for these homosexuals, no condition is linked to working time. If you meet all the other conditions, you are entitled to it. You are also entitled to maternity leave of the same duration, to the same maternity protection, etc.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights to social security as a part-time worker

question: I work under article 60, can I move?
answer: Yes. If you move to another municipality, the CPAS that granted you the aid remains your employer. But the CPAS of your new place of residence can take over the contract and become your employer. If you want to apply for another aid from the CPAS, you must apply to the CPAS of your new place of residence. If you move abroad, in principle your contract continues. The CPAS can keep the subsidy even if the worker's situation changes or if he moves.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid, work under Article 60

question: How much unemployment benefit will I receive if I have only worked for a few months?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were previously self-employed, under certain conditions. Civil servants have specific rules. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM); or your paying body (Syndicat or CAPAC). Nothing more, or nothing at all. To be entitled to unemployment benefits, you must have worked a certain number of days in a reference period.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment after a job

question: I am a student. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: Yes. If you are a student, you can bring some members of your family. To find out who are the members of your family who are entitled to family reunification, you have to ask yourself the following questions: What is the nationality of the person you meet in Belgium: Belgian, European or outside the European Union? What is his residence permit? Why? Because the people you can bring and the conditions depend heavily on your residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: I'm the owner. My insurance doesn't pay for all the repairs, is that normal in Brussels?
answer: To know the terms of compensation of your insurance contract, read your contract. There are several limitations of compensation. The deductible Some contracts provide that in the event of an accident, part of the amount owed is covered by the insured. This amount is called EUR EUR. The insurer will only pay the costs in excess of this amount. You have a deductible of EUR 500 and the value of the kitchen that burned was EUR 10,000, so your insurer will pay you EUR 300,000 The deductible is proportional to the amount insured.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance, renting an apartment, in Brussels

question: I live in a roommate with friends, how much unemployment benefit will I get?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were employed before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. Both civil servants have specific rules.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. How do I file for divorce once our agreements are written?
answer: You must file your divorce petition with the family court. You can choose which family court you want to go to. You file your divorce petition by petition: you write a petition (some family court registries have sample petitions to complete); you attach your divorce agreements by mutual consent; you attach other documents (excerpts from birth and marriage certificates, extracts from children's birth certificates, etc.).
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I live in a roommate with a friend, how much will I receive for my allowance for helping the elderly in Wallonia?
answer: Living with a friend does not make you lose your right to the elderly care allowance (APA). On the other hand, the amount of your APA can change depending on: 1. Your reduction in autonomy The amount of your APA depends on your reduction in autonomy. The doctor of your mutual assesses your reduction in autonomy according to two criteria. For each criterion, the doctor grants you a certain number of points. Depending on the number of points you have earned, you are classified in one of our 5 categories.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Wallonia

question: If I am a Belgian, does my adopted child automatically receive Belgian nationality?
answer: If you are a Belgian, your adoptive child under the age of 18 automatically receives Belgian nationality in the following cases: he was born in Belgium; he was born abroad but does not have another nationality; he was born abroad but you were born in Belgium. In these cases, there is no special administrative procedure. Your adoptive child receives Belgian nationality from the day the adoption is effective. On the other hand, your adoptive child does not automatically receive Belgian nationality if you and your adoptive child were born abroad.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, minors (under 18 years of age)

question: How do you make an inventory in Brussels?
answer: If you're not sure what you're looking for, you can check out the Rule No. 1: The state of the premises must be in writing, dated and signed by the landlord. Rule No. 2: The state of the premises must be clear; either it's done for free by the landlord and the tenant and each party has signed the report. Rule No. 2: It must be done by representatives of the landlord and the tenant. Rule No. 2: When it's done by the landlord and the tenant, it can be done by an expert. Rule No. 2: The state of the premises must be written by an expert.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Location

question: Where can I get my criminal record?
answer: You can request your criminal record extract from the municipality where you are domiciled. You can request it: online, via a secure electronic platform; or by applying at the population service counter. The municipality may ask you to pay the cost. Apply online You must apply on a secure electronic platform via the website of your municipality. You receive your criminal record extract in digital version. The criminal record extract is available for 6 months on the secure electronic platform.
topics: Justice, Judicial Record, Contents of the Judicial Record and Excerpts from the Judicial Record

question: I'm renting a cottage. What does my fire insurance cover in Wallonia?
answer: The following risks are covered unless your insurance contract excludes them: fire; damage caused by lightning, explosion, implosion, fall or impact of aircraft or objects. The objects or animals that fall or are projected to fall (e.g. car, pigeons, etc.) are normally also covered by fire insurance: damage caused by bombings, damage caused by labour conflicts, damage caused by storms, damage caused by natural disasters. Other risks are regularly covered: electricity; smoke; water; civil liability.
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia), Insurance

question: Can an elderly person who has lost his whole head be put under observation?
answer: No, being elderly or senile cannot justify being placed under observation. To be placed under observation, three criteria must be met: 1. to suffer from a mental illness: this concept is not defined in the law. Mental illness is defined on a case-by-case basis by the doctor in his medical report. 2. to pose a danger to oneself or others.
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: What if I can't agree with the other parent on a decision about our child?
answer: If this is not possible or if it has failed, you can appeal to an external mediator to help you reach an agreement. For example, if the other parent wants to enroll your child in a school that is not suitable for you, you can appeal to a counsellor or a mediator in the first place. If this does not work, you can ask the family judge to resolve this conflict.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: I'm not European and I have an unlimited stay. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: To apply for family reunification if you have unlimited residence, you must have been legally resident in Belgium for at least 12 months. Yes, you can apply for family reunification if you are 18 years old or older. Yes, you can apply for family reunification if you are 18 years old or older. Yes, you can apply for family reunification if you are 21 years old or older. Yes, you can apply for family reunification if you are 21 years old or older.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. What happens if one of the legal cohabitants dies?
answer: The death of one of the two legal cohabitants ends the legal cohabitation. Some consequences of the legal cohabitation remain. You have the right to use the family dwelling and the furniture that is in it. Without being the owner, you can continue to live in the place, or decide to rent this dwelling. Be careful, if you are a descendant of the deceased person, this protection does not apply.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: Can I be asked to reimburse the allowance for helping elderly people in Brussels?
answer: Yes, if you have received an elderly care allowance (APA) to which you were not entitled. This is called the repayment of the Hindu (what you received in excess). Iriscare <unk> Service APA can recover what it paid you unjustly. Iriscare <unk> Service APA can go back: 3 years (we count the 3 years from the payment of the Hindu); 5 years if you have made false returns or false returns (for example; you did not declare income, or you did not say that you live with another person, etc.). 1 year back if it is a mistake <unk> Iriscare <unk> Service APA <unk> Service APA cannot recover the debt.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Brussels

question: I am a foreigner and have lived in Belgium for more than 5 years, am I entitled to the RIS?
answer: Before going any further, from 2021 onwards, electronic residence cards will be replaced by new models. Registration in the population register is not an automatic card: you must apply for it; you are registered in the population register if you have a right of permanent residence in Belgium. The right of residence is also granted to non-European foreigners: those who are or are allowed to stay in Belgium for a limited period of time and who can prove their status as an uninterrupted resident in Belgium.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Being Belgian or belonging to certain categories of foreigners

question: What is a consumer credit?
answer: A consumer credit is a credit: concluded between a borrower (consumer) and a lender (a registered company); for an essentially private purpose whose object is the loan of a sum of money or a reserve of money to finance consumer goods or services; with a facility or a payment deadline.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a consumer credit

question: I'm an employee (or unemployed). I'm giving birth earlier than expected. What are the consequences on my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see this sheet "What are my conditions for maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. Please check with your personnel department. If you give birth earlier than the date announced by your doctor, your mandatory prenatal leave is less than 1 week. You cannot postpone the days of mandatory maternity leave that you have not taken.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: The occupational doctor has declared the worker permanently incapacitated at the time of the incapacity assessment.
answer: Before going any further, since November 28th, the procedure for termination of the contract for force majeure has been amended. Yes. If the worker does not agree with the decision of the occupational doctor who declared him permanently incapacitated during the assessment of incapacity, he can contest it. He must file an appeal: to the social inspector doctor of the General Directorate of Work Welfare Control; within 21 calendar days from the day after the day he received the decision of the occupational doctor; by mail; He must inform his employer that he is filing an appeal. The procedure for termination of the contract for force majeure is suspended.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: I'm married. Should I help my spouse if he's in need?
answer: Yes. If you are married, you have an obligation to help each other if one of you is in need. It is the duty of relief and assistance. You must contribute together to the expenses of the marriage. If one of you has no income (professional or other), the other must provide for his needs. Specifically, the spouse who has income must pay the expenses of the household, housing, medical care, clothing, etc. If he does not do it voluntarily, the spouse who is in need can ask the court of the family to condemn his spouse to bear these expenses.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: How much is my Grapa if I live with my child or parent?
answer: You receive the Grapa at the increased rate if you live with: one or more minor children; one or more adult children who are entitled to family allowances; your parents or direct online allies: father, grandmother, grandfather, child, grandchild, step-parent, step-child. For more information, see the website of the Federal Pension Service (SFP). But you receive a maximum of 1.460.03 per month (increased amount; increased on 1 January 2023). The Grapa deducts some of your resources from this amount, including: your pension; your professional income; replacement income (mutual, unemployment, etc.); and your pension paid by your spouse.
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: Am I entitled to unemployment benefits if I live in a roommate?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage-earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were previously self-employed, subject to certain conditions. Civil servants have a separate scheme.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. How does the divorce by mutual consent process go?
answer: First, you must draw up your divorce agreements by mutual consent: either to the two of you, to each other, or with the help of a professional (lawyer, family mediator, notary, etc.). (2) Then you must go to the registry of the family court to file: (1) your agreements; (2) a request for divorce by mutual consent (the registry usually has a standard form to fill out); (3) administrative documents (extract from the birth certificate, extract from the marriage certificate, etc.).
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: What is the risk if I hand over a fake diploma or CV to my future employer?
answer: First of all, you don't start the relationship with your future employer under ideal conditions. If he realizes that you are lying about your qualifications, he will no longer trust you. In addition, lying about his professional experiences or his diplomas can lead to the nullity of the employment contract for lack of consent (EUR). But EUR, it is not automatic. The employer must prove that he would not have signed the employment contract if he had known the truth about your CV; public qualifications In this appreciation, an advantage of a little more than a fake diploma is less punishable.
topics: Work, salaried work, hiring interview

question: My landlord demands a three-month rental guarantee. Is that legal in Wallonia?
answer: Depending on the type of rental guarantee chosen, the landlord can claim: the equivalent of 2 months' maximum rent (excluding charges) if it is in a blocked account in your name; the equivalent of 3 months' maximum rent (excluding charges) if it is in the form of a bank guarantee (via the CPAS or directly via the Bank).
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rental guarantee, Creating a rental guarantee

question: I am applying for family reunification. I am joining a foreigner authorised to stay in Belgium. I am in Belgium. How can I apply for family reunification with a foreigner (outside the EU) authorised to stay in Belgium?
answer: Before going any further, please note that you can only apply for family reunification from Belgium in a limited number of situations. If your card is valid, you will receive a certificate of registration from the Office. In principle, you have 9 months to make a decision. This period can then be extended 2 times for a period of 3 months. In most situations, the procedure and requirements for applying for residence in Belgium are different: if you are a tourist, you will be granted a residence permit and if you are a spouse of a foreigner, you will receive a temporary residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: As legal cohabitants, what are the consequences of our separation on our property?
answer: As long as you haven't made a declaration of termination of legal cohabitation in the municipality (alone or together), all the rights and duties of legal cohabitators continue to apply to you. This means that even if you are the sole owner or tenant of this building and the other cohabitant no longer resides in it, you can't do what you want. For example, you can't decide on aspects of the lease alone or sell it for rent. You must have the permission of the family judge.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: I'm married. We're married. Can one of us kick the other out?
answer: No. Your family housing is protected: one spouse cannot kick the other out. This protection of family housing implies that one spouse cannot: break the lease alone (even if he signed it alone); kick his spouse out (even if he is the sole owner of the house or apartment); sell the housing without the other spouse's consent (even if the other spouse does not own it); the furniture that is in your housing is also protected. The owner of the furniture must obtain the other spouse's consent to sell, give or rent it. Family housing protection applies only to the building in which the family has established its main residence.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: What is meant by  ⁇ repairs made necessary by obsolescence, normal wear and tear or force majeure ⁇  in Brussels?
answer: For example, paint deteriorates after 15 years of occupation. The tenant is not responsible for the wear and tear or the height of a painting, if they are normal. The lifespan of the paintings and their depreciation depend on many factors such as: the nature and quality of the paint, the characteristics of the paint, the depreciation of the paint. Contrary to what can sometimes be systematically depreciated after 9 years of occupation.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work, Distribution of repairs and maintenance between tenant and landlord

question: I'm renting a cottage. Do I have to say how much the rent is in the ad in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, in principle, any landlord who rents a property used for housing is required to include in all public or official communications (newspapers, website, posters, etc.) the following: the amount of the rent excluding charges; the nature of the private and common charges (for a fee or provisions); the amount of any proposed common charges; the amount of the private charges if they are flat-rate; certain indicators of the energy performance certificate such as: the unique code (certificate PEB identification number); the total energy consumption; primary energy consumption in kWh per year; the specific consumption of primary energy, in kWh per m2 per m2 and the energy label or the (see also the energy label)
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia)

question: Why are there sometimes two guardians for a minor child?
answer: The justice of the peace can "divide" the guardianship and appoint two guardians. This must always be done in the best interests of the child. For example, in some cases the property to be managed requires specific skills, and therefore the intervention of specialists. These specialists are not necessarily able to take care of the child and educate him. The justice of the peace then appoints two guardians: a guardian "to the person", who deals with the child's education, school choices, health decisions, etc.; and a guardian "to the property", who deals with bank accounts, money and property.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, guardianship, role of the guardian

question: My friend has his reference address at my place, can we seize my furniture?
answer: In principle, no. The person who has a reference address with you cannot live with you. Indeed, the purpose of the reference address is to allow a person who no longer has a residence in Belgium to maintain an administrative existence and social rights. For more information, see the section "Reference address". Your furniture cannot be seized to pay his debts since, logically, they do not belong to your friend justice But, if your friend has stored furniture with you, the creditor can ask the judge for permission to seize your home. This hypothesis is very rare.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of movable property

question: I didn't sign a lease. What are my rights in Brussels?
answer: However, your lease may be considered a main residence lease if: you occupy the lease as your main residence and your landlord has indicated his agreement (even orally or implicitly as long as you can demonstrate it). For more information, see our sheet "Is my lease a main residence lease?" In principle, you would have to sign a written contract. This is a legal lease since June 15, 2007.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Writing and signing
Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Scope

question: Under what conditions can I apply for citizenship if I have lived in Belgium for 10 years?
answer: You can apply for Belgian nationality if you meet all the following conditions: have an unlimited right of residence; prove an uninterrupted legal residence of at least 10 years; A, B, C, D, E, E+, F, F+ and Annexes 8 and 15 are taken into account in the calculation of the 10 years. The period on the orange card (attestation of reception) of 3 or 6 months can be equated with legal residence in two cases: in the context of an asylum application that led to the application for recognition of refugee status in the European Community with a family group; in the context of a family group; in the context of a reference community of at least 3 years; etc.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, adults (over 18 years of age), persons residing in Belgium for at least 10 years

question: What should I do if my children prevent me from seeing my grandchildren?
answer: There are two possibilities: try to get an amicable arrangement - possibly with the help of a family mediator; a negotiated solution is often more likely to be respected by all concerned, rather than a decision imposed by the judge. If no amicable solution is possible, you can ask the family court to grant you the right to have personal relationships with your grandchildren.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Rights of grandparents

question: Can I be deported in the winter to Brussels in Wallonia?
answer: It depends, if you are a tenant of a private dwelling or a social dwelling. Yes, if you are a tenant of a private dwelling, you can be evicted during the winter. However, the justice of the peace has a discretion. He can "delay" the eviction.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: How can I prove that I am not responsible for the actions of my minor child?
answer: In order not to be held responsible for the damage caused by your minor child, you must prove that you have not committed any fault or negligence in your duty of supervision and upbringing. As a parent, you are presumed to be responsible. So you must "reverse" the presumption of responsibility that applies.
topics: Family, Relationship between parents and children, Responsibility

question: I'm an employee. I'm pregnant, can I refuse to work overtime or night work?
answer: Attention, the rules are not quite the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. Under certain conditions, some work is prohibited for the pregnant worker. This is part of maternity protection, and welfare and safety at work?" 1. In particular, you cannot be forced to work overtime. In principle, hours are considered overtime when they exceed 9 hours a day, or 40 hours a week, or when they exceed the lower limits set by the collective bargaining agreement (CCT).
topics: Work, Work and parenting, During pregnancy

question: My short-term lease is not registered. Can I leave without notice or compensation in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, but under certain conditions. This special opportunity to terminate the short-term lease has only been available since September 2018. It applies to all leases that are in effect at that time. Be careful before terminating the lease! First of all, check if your lease is not registered. If your landlord has signed a lease with you for more than 2 months and has not registered it with your landlord, you will get the necessary information, either from the legal security office (formerly the registration office) of the place where your home is located.
topics: Housing, Rental in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, End of short-term lease (less than or equal to 3 years), By the tenant

question: I ⁇ m a tenant. What happens if I don ⁇ t have fire insurance?
answer: Before the accident If your lease obliges you to take out fire insurance and if you do not do so, you are not fulfilling your obligations. Your landlord may ask the justice of the peace: - to order you to take out fire insurance, under penalty of penalty (condemnation to pay a sum of money per day of delay); - to terminate the lease for lack of insurance. But if you comply with your rental and rent payment obligations, it is very rare for the justice of the peace to take out the lease at your own expense.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance

question: We are separated or divorced, who receives family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, the reform of family allowances has changed some things since 1 January 2019, and since 1 January 2020.For more information, see the sheet <unk>What changes with the reform of family allowances?<unk> In principle, nothing changes. Your separation or divorce has no effect on the payment of family allowances, nor on the amount of family allowances (except in the case of recombined families). See the question <unk>In the case of a recombined family, does the amount of family allowances increase?<unk> For more information, see the sheet <unk>Family allowances continue to be paid to the child's mother, by the same family allowance fund as before the separation.<unk> But if the child is already married, the family allowance is paid to another parent.
topics: Social protection, Family allowances and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born before 1 January 2020 (Wallonia)

question: What does it mean to assert one ⁇ s rights to other social benefits for the RIS?
answer: This means that you are only entitled to social integration if you cannot receive other social security benefits (unemployment, mutual, disability allowance, etc.) in Belgium or abroad. The right to social integration is a residual scheme. So you must commit to take all the steps to receive the other social security benefits, if you are entitled to them. The CPAS can intervene while waiting for these steps to be completed.
topics: Social protection, help from the CPAS, social integration income (RIS), enforce your rights to other social benefits (unemployment, mutual, disabled, etc., )

question: Am I separating? Are the urgent and provisional measures taken by the family judge limited in time for legal cohabitators?
answer: Yes. If the urgent and provisional measures taken by the family judge concerning your common children, they apply until a judge makes a new decision. For other urgent and provisional measures, the validity of the measures depends on when they are taken: before or after the end of legal cohabitation. If the request for measures is introduced before a declaration of cessation of cohabitation is made to the municipality: the judge fixes the duration of the measures in his decision.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation, separation before the judge

question: I am an employee. What is the amount of my maternity allowance if I am an employee?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this sheet applies only to salaried workers and the unemployed.For the self-employed, see our sheet "How much will I receive during my independent maternity leave?"For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.For civil servants, please check with your personnel department.It is not your employer who pays you during maternity leave.For the duration of your maternity leave, you receive a certain amount of gross allowance.For civil servants, the gross allowance is a percentage of your actual salary.For civil servants, the gross allowance is calculated on the basis of your unemployment allowance.For civil servants, the unemployment allowance is calculated on the basis of your unemployment allowance.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid maternity leave (or unemployment)

question: Who can see my medical records?
answer: You can consult your medical record. More specifically, you can: ask to consult your medical record, yourself or through an intermediary; check your patient's file; and ask for a copy of your patient's file; If you are still alive or have died, your relatives can also consult your medical record, but only under certain conditions. Some people can only consult the medical record: the deceased, the deceased's spouse or legal partner.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, Patient's rights and medical records

question: I'm staying with a friend or relative. My friend or relative lives in one of my unoccupied apartments. Do I have to sign a lease?
answer: For example, if your friend is staying longer but the occupancy is completely free, you can enter into a loan agreement. If your friend is staying longer, he must pay a price (in cash or not) and he occupies the accommodation as the main residence, re-establish the principle of the main residence only by the tenant, for example, by indicating the names, description of the tenant, duration of the lease, the amount of the lease, etc.
topics: Accommodation, accommodation, conditions of accommodation

question: If we separate, can the family court make decisions about our social housing lease in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. Yes. The family court has jurisdiction to order temporary measures during a separation. For example, the judge may provide that Mrs. temporarily occupies the family home alone with the children (even if Mr. is the only one who has signed the lease) until the divorce judgment. As compensation, the judge may provide that Mrs. must pay an occupancy allowance to Mr. (especially if he continues to pay the rent to the SLSP).
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Social housing (Wallonia), Conflicts of tenure

question: What can the occupational doctor decide after the assessment of incapacity?
answer: Before going any further Since 28 November 2022, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure has been amended. After the assessment of incapacity, the occupational doctor makes one of these decisions. The worker is temporarily unable to perform the agreed work. The procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure ends. The worker remains permanently unable to perform the agreed work.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: When can it be considered that there is a serious breach of the employment contract?
answer: There is no exhaustive list of serious faults admitted to break the employment contract without notice or compensation. The law gives only a general definition; each situation is examined according to the particular circumstances. For there to be a serious fault, all these conditions must be met. There must be a serious fault. The fault can be committed by: the worker; or the employer; or a person who acts at the request of the worker or the employer.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, immediate termination for serious misconduct

question: Can the family judge order one of us to leave the family home?
answer: Yes. It doesn't matter who is the tenant or owner of the family home. Indeed, the family home is protected: you and your spouse must agree to be able to rent it, sell it, terminate the lease, etc. You and your spouse must agree to be able to rent it, sell it, terminate the lease, etc.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, judicial separation - urgent and provisional measures

question: Can all the roommates together terminate the unofficial roommate lease before it expires in Wallonia?
answer: It depends on the type of lease. If the lease is also a main residence lease: The tenants can terminate the lease all together if they want to leave the apartment at the same time. They must comply with two conditions: send a three-month notice to the landlord. The three-month deadline only starts on the first day of the following month. All tenants must sign the notice if they are solidarity tenants.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Duration and end of the colocation, Duration and end of the unofficial colocation lease (Colocation Wallonia)

question: I'm working overtime, am I getting paid double?
answer: Not necessarily double, but you're paid more than for the hours of normal working hours. In principle, you can't work more than 8 hours a day, and 38 hours a week. However, these hourly limits can be exceeded in some cases and under certain conditions (e.g. for transportation work, or in the event of extraordinary overtime), with a maximum of 11 hours a day or 50 hours a week. In principle, all hours worked beyond 9 hours a day or 40 hours a week (except for other limits in certain sectors of activity) are overtime entitling to overtime.
topics: Work, salaried work, remuneration and benefits in kind, salary

question: How does the CPAS check what rate I'm entitled to for the RIS?
answer: To find out at what rate you are entitled to social integration income (RIS), the CPAS must carry out a social survey. It must examine your personal and family situation. The CPAS must examine your concrete situation. It must in principle meet you. It can also meet the people who live with you provided that: you agree; it is interesting to meet them to understand your situation. question: The parents are not married to each other. Can I be obliged to establish a relationship of parentage with my child if neither I nor the mother is married?

question: I'm confiding in a professional, is everything I say to the professional covered by professional secrecy?
answer: Yes procedures If your interlocutor is bound to professional secrecy, this secrecy also covers everything you tell him, in the exercise of his profession. Secrecy does not only cover what you have expressly confided to him. It also covers everything he may have seen, known, learned, noticed, discovered or even surprised, in the exercise of his profession. For example, if you consult a doctor because you feel tired, and when analyzing the results of a blood test he discovers that you suffer from a serious illness; this information is also covered by professional secrecy.

question: What if the landlord abuses the right of visit in Wallonia?

The right of visitation is not regulated by law, so it is the contract, the customs and the principle of equity that will guide the judge's decision. question: Will I receive less Grapa if I take in a young person who is not part of my family and who stays with his parents?
answer: As soon as a young person lives under your roof, you receive the Grapa at the basic rate (maximum 973,36 EUR per month - increased on 1 January 2023). Even if the young person does not settle with you. It is the de facto residence that counts. But if you had the increased rate before the arrival of the young person you are welcoming, you move to the basic rate and the amount of your Grapa decreases, the Federal Pension Service (SFP) deducts some of your resources from this amount, including: your pension; your professional income; your divorce income (mutual, unemployment, etc.); and your pension paid by your ex-spouse.
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: I ⁇ m sick, do I have to notify my employer?
answer: Before we go any further, please note that there are special and complex rules for occupational diseases, i.e. diseases that you suffer from because of your work. This sheet only covers ordinary diseases, not occupational diseases. Yes, if your illness makes you unable to work. You must notify your employer immediately, i.e. on the very day of your disability, except in exceptional cases. For example, if you are hospitalized in an emergency, you do not necessarily have to notify your employer in writing.
topics: Work, salaried work, illness and accident

question: What's the point of professional secrecy?
answer: The purpose of professional secrecy is to protect the relationship of trust, which is necessary for certain professionals (doctors, lawyers, psychologists, social workers, etc.). Without professional secrecy, professionals cannot help citizens who need help. If you consult a doctor, or if you ask for help from the CPAS, you must often tell him about your private life, your privacy, your health. You do not want these elements to be known to everyone. If you fear that this professional will tell others what you confide in him, you will not tell him. Or you do not ask for the necessary help to survive and that of your family.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: I was adopted. Can I benefit from family reunification?
answer: Yes. Children who can benefit from the right to family reunification vis-à-vis their parents are the only children for whom a filiation bond is established between them and their parents. This filiation bond can be biological or adoptive. The filiation bond is proven by: the birth certificate, if the members of the filiation are biological the adoption act, if the filiation bond is adoptive. If there is no filiation bond, family reunification is not possible. For example, Kafala in Moroccan law, does not create a filiation bond between the 'adoptive parent' and the child.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: What are the conditions to benefit from the Grapa?
answer: To be eligible for the Grappa, you must be 65 years of age or older. You must be a Belgian citizen. But some foreigners may also be eligible for the Grappa, provided they have worked in Belgium for at least 312 full-time days (as an employee, self-employed or civil servant).
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: How long is a tenancy in Wallonia?
answer: If the lease is between three and nine years, it is considered to be a nine-year lease. The law sets strict termination rules. The lease never ends automatically. A tenant or the landlord sends his notice so that the lease ends.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Duration and end of the colocation, Duration and end of the unofficial colocation lease (Colocation Wallonia)

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I'm getting married. Can I come to Belgium to get married?
answer: This corresponds to a request for a "short stay" visa (maximum 3 months). If you live abroad, you can come to join a Belgian or a foreigner who has a legal residence permit in Belgium to marry him. For this, you must, in principle, present the following documents: a copy of your passport; a medical certificate issued for a maximum of 6 months; an extract from your criminal record dated for a maximum of 6 months; proof that you have sufficient resources; a copy of your marriage certificate; a copy of your birth certificate dated for a maximum of 3 months.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and family, Marriage

question: I am a self-employed person. Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave if I am a self-employed person?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The self-employed scheme applies to: self-employed women; self-employed caregivers; self-employed wives. For ease of use, the term "self-employed woman" is used here in a broad sense, covering these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the self-employed scheme.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: Can I live at my cat's address in Wallonia?
answer: In practice, the neighborhood policeman comes to your home to check different elements (which may vary from one municipality to another), for example: your name on the mailbox the number of your toothbrushes and clothes etc. So you can in principle check your residence in your apartment if you actually have the main center of your activities there.

question: Some guidelines for student work?
answer: For example: the student cannot work more than 600 hours a year. Before the ceiling was 475 hours a year; he must be enrolled in a study program including a certain number of credits; etc. If all these conditions are met, the social security contributions that the employer must pay are lower than for an ordinary employment contract. To make it easier for students who want to hire students and employers who want to hire students, the National Office of Social Security has created a website.
topics: Work, salaried work, student work

question: Am I entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo) if I am a minor?
answer: Yes. You are entitled to legal aid if you are under 18 years of age. All you have to do is present yourself with your identity card: to a lawyer who practices legal aid; or to the legal aid office (BAJ). You do not have to pay anything. If you become an adult during the intervention of the lawyer, it is necessary to check whether you meet other conditions to be entitled to legal aid: either you belong to a particular category (help from the CPAS, collective debt settlement, stay abroad, etc.).

Civil servants have their own system, except that.

question: Is my cot lease a student lease in Wallonia?
answer: Your lease is a student lease if: The dwelling is rented by or for a student. Either the student signs the lease and is the tenant. Either someone else signs the lease (example: parents) but it is the student who occupies the dwelling. The lease has been concluded or renewed since 1 September 2018. The student must provide a certificate of enrollment for secondary or higher education. You must also provide the certificate of enrollment when signing the lease. The registration must cover the entire duration of the lease or a significant part of it. In this case, you can replace the certificate of enrollment with a copy of your application.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), What to know about the student cot lease

question: How much will I receive in family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: The amount of your family allowance depends on the number of family allowances that are part of your household. The basic amount is EUR 2 higher for the 2nd child, and even higher for the 3rd child and the highest monthly allowances at the ordinary rate indexed as of 1 December 2022. For more information, see the website for the following basic amounts. For parents, parents and information sheets, see the website for the 2nd child, see the website for the 2nd child, see the website for the 2nd child, see the website for the 3rd child, see the website for the 3rd child, etc.

question: What happens after the application for international protection is filed?
answer: Before going any further, since 2018, the asylum procedure is called the international protection procedure. Your lawyer cannot be present at this hearing, so you must submit an application for international protection. This procedure can allow you to obtain: refugee status; or subsidiary protection status. For the first step, it is a short hearing at the Foreigners' Office. This hearing can take place: on the day of your application; or at the latest within 30 days of your application. Your lawyer cannot be present at this hearing.
topics: Foreigners, International protection (formerly called asylum), Apply for international protection (formerly called asylum)

question: What does it mean to have your effective residence in Belgium for the RIS?
answer: This means that you must be in Belgium to be entitled to social integration income (RIS). You are not entitled to it if you live abroad. You must reside "usually and permanently" in Belgium. You do not necessarily have to have a domicile in Belgium. It is your de facto residence that counts. So you can be entitled to RIS: even if you do not have a home in Belgium, and even if you are not registered in the population registers in Belgium. But you must be legally resident in Belgium.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), having his effective residence in Belgium

question: How can I find the addresses and hours of the legal aid offices?
answer: You can: consult the website avocats.be. question: How to extend or renew a student lease in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further, since 1 January 2018, there is a student lease in Brussels, with specific rules for terminating the lease. The rules of the student lease apply to your lease only if: the apartment is located in the Brussels area; you have signed or renewed your lease after 1 January 2018; you have explicitly chosen to use the student lease. This sheet has been updated more than one year ago. Your student lease is extended for one year and under the same conditions if: neither you nor your landlord has given you leave at the end of the initial lease.
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, Kot (Brussels), student lease

question: Can a person under the administration of property and or the person sign a lease in Wallonia?
answer: A person placed under the administration of property and or of the person can no longer perform all acts relating to his property and or to his person himself. The decision to put him under protection contains the list of acts that he cannot do alone. Depending on this decision, it is either the person himself or his administrator who can conclude the lease. If the protected person has concluded the lease while he was unable to do so, he or his administrator can request the cancellation of the lease to the justice of the peace.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: I ⁇ m the owner. I ⁇ m the owner. Should I install fire detectors in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. All housing in Wallonia must be equipped with a fire detector by their owner. You assume the purchase and installation. The tenant assumes the maintenance and change of batteries. He must check if the detector is working and warn you if it is not. The detector must be of the optical type and be certified by an accredited body. The number of detectors to be placed depends on the living area: Below 80 m2, only one detector is mandatory; Above 80 m2, 2 detectors are mandatory. If the rented housing is a house, this rule applies to each floor.
topics: Money, Insurance, Fire insurance, Renting a house, In Wallonia
Money, insurance, fire insurance, protecting your home

question: I receive confidentiality as a professional. I work in a CPAS, do I have to respond to requests for information from the King's Prosecutor to fight terrorism?
answer: Since 1 September 2017, there is a new exception to the principle of professional secrecy. Social security institutions must respond to requests for information from the King's Prosecutor, but only under certain conditions and in certain cases. But the CPAS is part of the social security institutions covered. Only under certain conditions. The King's Prosecutor can only ask you for information about terrorist offences (defined in Article 137 of the Criminal Code).
topics: Privacy, Professional secrecy, Professional secrecy and the fight against terrorism

question: Can I sign several short-term leases in Brussels?
answer: Yes. You must comply with 3 conditions: the renewal must be done in writing. The writing can be: a new lease; a renewal clause in the initial lease. We are talking about tacit renewal. Then you must not sign a new contract; an amendment to the lease. Be careful when the lease is made. The terms of the lease must remain the same. For example: the rent. Except for the duration that may be different.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, End of short-term lease (3 years or less)

question: Can my landlord do repairs while I'm living in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. Yes, but only in certain cases. Your landlord can make urgent repairs. These repairs must not be postponed until the end of the lease. If this is the case, you must undergo them free of charge. If the work lasts more than 40 days, you can have a reduction in rent proportional to: the duration of the work; the part of the housing from which you are deprived. This rule is supplementary: you are not entitled to a reduction in rent if the lease is terminated.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work, Works and improvements, Works by the owner

question: Will I receive less compensation from the mutual if I move into a roommate?
answer: For more information, please contact your cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse or cohabiting spouse.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits

question: Can the landlord claim the entire rent from a single roommate in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, if the roommates are in solidarity. The solidarity of the roommates allows the landlord to ask one of them (usually the most solvent) to fulfill the entire obligation. In particular, it allows him to claim the full rent from the chosen roommate, who is then obliged to pay the full amount. If you have signed an official solidarity lease, the roommates are in solidarity.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Loyers, charges and insurance

question: I'm a grandparent. Do I have the right to contact my grandchildren?
answer: Yes. Grandparents have the right to personal contacts with their grandchildren. This is what is called the right to personal relationships of grandparents. Other people than grandparents can also ask for it: brothers and sisters with each other; other people if they justify a special bond of affection with the child (for example, grandparents, uncles, aunts, godparents, etc.). But this right is not automatic.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Rights of grandparents

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. What is a discount?
answer: This is the postponement to a later hearing. At the first hearing, and under certain circumstances, the parties may decide by mutual agreement to request that the case be referred to a later hearing. If he agrees, the judge may set a new hearing date for: hearing the pleadings, or checking the status of this case, i.e. seeing where the parties are in their exchange of files, and seeing if a pleading date can be set.
topics: Justice, In court, Conduct of hearings

question: I'm married. We're renting a house. Who has to pay the rent?
answer: For example, if one of you pays the rent and groceries, the other spouse can take care of the children and household chores in proportion to his or her abilities. If you have signed the lease with two, you both have obligations to the landlord. The landlord can claim the rent. If the landlord leaves the house without formalities and is exonerated, the landlord can claim all the rent, even if the tenants are insolvent.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Do I have to be personally present at the hearings before the family court if I have initiated the proceedings?
answer: Everything depends on the type of hearing. You must always be present: at the introductory hearing, it is the first hearing; at all hearings concerning your minor children. Only exceptional circumstances may justify your absence. If you do not come personally to the hearings, the judge may postpone the hearing or dismiss your request. For other hearings, you may be represented by a lawyer. The other party must also be present at these hearings. In the absence, the judge may render a judgment by default or postpone the hearing to another date.
topics: Family, Resolving a family dispute, Appeal to the family judge

question: I receive confidences as a professional. I work in a CPAS, do I have to spontaneously communicate to the King's Prosecutor suspicions of radicalization or terrorism?
answer: Since September 1, 2017, there is a new exception to the principle of professional secrecy. Social security institutions must respond to requests for information from the King's Prosecutor, but only under certain conditions and in certain cases. The CPAS is one of the social security institutions covered. No, unless you meet the conditions of an exception to professional secrecy (state of necessity or non-assistance to anyone in danger). The Constitutional Court has canceled your active information obligation (order of March 14, 2019).
