question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. We are legal cohabitants. Who has to pay back the mortgage?
answer: It depends on who signed the mortgage loan contract with the bank. If the mortgage loan is signed by you two, this debt is a "solidarity" debt: you both have to repay it. Creditors can seize all your property if you do not repay the loan. Each of you may have to pay the entire debt. The one who pays can then ask the other to repay his share (half or another agreed proportion).
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: What do we mean by "sufficient housing" to bring your family?
answer: If you are a Belgian or a non-European foreigner, you must have "sufficient" and "decent" housing to accommodate your family members. The housing must meet the conditions of a rental property as a main residence; the basic requirements of safety, health and habitability. Your file must contain: a notarized title deed; or a registered lease contract.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: How do you know if someone is placed under administration of property and or person?
answer: 1. The decision is published in the Belgian Monitor. All decisions to put the person under the administration of property and persons are published in the Belgian Monitor. By clicking on <unk> search <unk> and then <unk> list <unk> you get the list of decisions of the justice of the peace concerning that person. The decisions are classified in chronological order. This publication also contains the name of the administrator and his contact details. 2. The administration is also communicated to the mayor.
topics: Private life, measures for the protection of persons, administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), the consequences of the administration of property and or of the person

question: How can I terminate my student lease in Brussels?
answer: Before the deadline: You can terminate the lease at any time. You must then send a notice to your landlord 2 months in advance. The 2 month deadline begins on the first day of the month following the month in which you send the notice to your landlord. Be sure to keep proof of the date of dispatch (recommended). Be careful, if the lease is for three months or less, you cannot terminate it before the deadline.
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, Kot (Brussels), student lease

question: Do I have to leave before the expulsion is implemented in Wallonia?
answer: No, but it is strongly advised that you are not obliged to leave the premises before the execution of the eviction, but it is preferable if you want to avoid additional costs. If you do not leave voluntarily, your landlord will call an eviction officer to proceed with your eviction. If necessary, the eviction officer will be assisted by EUR: a locksmith (if you do not open the door), movers (if you do not move your belongings yourself), police officers (if you refuse to leave the premises). If you do not take me with you, you will be charged.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, Expulsion

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Do I have to pay the debts of my legal cohabitant?
answer: No. There is no common property between the legal cohabitants: each of you two must pay his debts on his own property alone. However, as legal cohabitants you must pay together (solidarely) all the debts of the common life: electricity bills, water bills, children's medical expenses, etc. These debts can be paid with your property, even if you have not signed the bail contracts. This is the principle of solidarity debt.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

The rent can be increased in certain cases and under certain conditions only: Type of work Conditions for increasing the rent Energy performance work The tenant has agreed to the increase at least 1 month before the work The tenant has agreed to the increase at least 1 month before the work The tenant has agreed to the increase at least 1 month before the work The tenant has agreed to the increase between the 9th and the 6th day before the end of the current three-year period.

As of November 28th, the termination of the employment contract for medical force majeure is no longer done via the reintegration pathway. Yes. If you disagree with the decision of the doctor of the workplace who declared you permanently incapable during the reintegration evaluation, you can object to it. You must file an appeal with the social doctor.

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. How do I actually get a divorce by mutual consent?

You can establish them yourself, but it is better to ask for the help of a professional (lawyer, notary or family mediator). question: What is the state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: Since September 2018, the regulation provides for the minimum information contained in the state of the entrance and exit points. These contents are not mandatory. The landlord and the tenant may agree not to repeat all this information. For the state of the entrance points, the information provided is: the date of the signature; the identity and quality of the people who make the state of the places; the references of the lease: the coordinates of the parties, the address of the dwelling, the date of the beginning of the lease; a general note or a quick overview; the state of the dwelling, the materials and equipment as well as the general state of the rooms; the description of the dwelling, the state of the dwellings and the other elements of the dwelling; the date of the signature; the author of the dwelling; the author of the dwelling.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Bail of main residence (Wallonia), Location

question: I'm a foreigner, am I entitled to health care reimbursement?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only some general information. For any details or additional questions, please contact your mutual. Attention to two things: The rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. The notion of a dependent person is not the same in the different social security schemes. For example, a dependent person for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent person for disability benefits, nor for unemployment.

question: I am a guardian. What responsibilities can I have as a guardian towards the MENA?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. You are not civilly responsible for the actions of the MENA, as are the parents. But you must be held accountable, and you are responsible for mistakes made in the exercise of your function, for example if you miss a appeal deadline. So it is a professional responsibility that can sometimes be shared with the educators of the center where the Mena lives or with the lawyer of the minor.
topics: Foreigners, foreign minors

question: I'm a parent. Do I still have to pay alimony if my child doesn't do anything in school?
answer: Autonomy is generally achieved when the young person has a diploma or has completed training that gives him access to the labour market. However, the studies must be carried out normally, without any particular delay attributable to the young person. It is accepted that a young person may have had some difficulties in the course, such as "mistaken" in his choice of studies and that he redirects after a first year of studies. The young person is entitled to a level of education and maintenance proportional to the resources and curricula of his parents.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: What if you disagree with a doctor's decision to put you under observation?
answer: If you do not agree with the decision, you may be able to challenge it. It all depends on the type of decision you want to challenge: if it is a decision relating to a transfer to another institution: you must file a request with the justice of the peace within 8 days of sending this decision. If it is a decision relating to the end of hospitalization: only the person who asked for the patient to be placed under observation can challenge it.
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: What is a circumstantial medical certificate?
answer: It is a medical certificate that states that a person is no longer able to manage his property and or his person. It must describe the state of physical or mental health that is the source of this disability, and the impact of this state of health on the ability to manage. The description must be precise and complete. The doctor can fill out the medical certificate after consulting with the patient, or only based on the data of his medical file.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)

question: I ask for family reunification, what do you mean by "stable, regular and sufficient means of subsistence" to bring your family?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this form only applies to family reunification with a non-European foreigner or with a Belgian. For family reunification with a non-European foreigner, the applicant must meet an income requirement (except for minor children or certain refugees). For family reunification with a non-European parent, there must be a stable, stable and sufficient means of subsistence for the person. This means that they must be at least equivalent to 120% of the last month's income. For a Belgian parent who has a child under the age of 22 who is not eligible for a pension, for example. For a foreigner who is not eligible for a pension, for example.

question: Can the landlord index the rent in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, from 1 November 2022 to 31 October 2023, there are temporary rules on rent indexation in Wallonia depending on the PEB certificate. It depends on whether your lease is a primary residence co-location lease or not. 1. If your lease is not a primary residence co-location lease, the landlord can index the rent if it is provided for in the lease agreement. 2. If the lease is not provided for in the lease agreement, the landlord can ask for an automatic indexation.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Loyers, charges and insurance

question: How much social integration income am I entitled to if I live with my partner?
answer: This depends on whether or not you have an unmarried minor child under your roof, knowing that this child does not necessarily have to be yours. If you live with your partner, and with at least one unmarried minor child, you are considered a person living with a dependent family, and you benefit from a higher amount of RIS. If you live only with your partner, you are considered a cohabitant and you benefit from a lower amount of RIS.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits

question: What other aid is possible to constitute a rental guarantee in Wallonia?
answer: 1. some associations help tenants to pay the amount claimed for the rental guarantee. inquire with these associations. for example, the rental guarantee fund of the ProxiRélux network in the province of Luxembourg. for more information, see their website. 2. the municipalities also grant aid for the establishment of the rental guarantee. inquire with your municipality. 3. banks grant bank guarantees in the form of a loan. in practice, few tenants constitute such a guarantee because banks ask for the payment of dissuasive fees. this file is not prohibited by law.

question: Is my lease an official tenancy in Wallonia?
answer: Your lease is an official lease if: the lease was signed or renewed after September 1, 2018; you, your roommates and the landlord all signed one and the same lease; you and your roommates signed a lease between you; the date of signing the lease is indicated in the lease; there is at least one living room or a communal toilet for you and your roommates; you and your roommates are not married or cohabiting legally.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Location (Wallonia)

question: I am a salaried worker. I die after childbirth. What are the consequences on my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see our sheet "Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. Please inquire with your personnel department. If you are employed after childbirth, the father or co-parent may, under certain conditions, recover the remaining days of your post-natal maternity leave.
topics: Work, work and parenting, paid maternity leave (or unemployment)

question: After how long is the payment of my rent prescribed?
answer: The statute of limitations for the payment of your rent and expenses is 5 years from the due date of the rent. For example, the rent for August 2022 must be paid no later than August 31, 2022. You are released from the payment of the rent in September 2027. Beyond this period, the amount due is lost by the landlord. But be careful, the statute of limitations can be suspended or interrupted.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a lease agreement

question: What happens if my income is seized and I have other creditors?
answer: The bailiff will warn your other creditors, all creditors will participate in the seizure. If a seizure of your property is made, the bailiff has the obligation, no later than 15 days after the seizure of your income, to invite all other known creditors to make a declaration of their claim. In other words, if a creditor wants to seize your income, he must take into account all other creditors.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of income

question: Can I have the student lease I signed for next year in Brussels cancelled?
answer: Before going any further Since 1 January 2018, there is a student lease in Brussels, with specific rules for terminating the lease. The rules of the student lease apply to your lease only if: the accommodation is located in the Brussels area; you have signed or renewed your contract after 1 January 2018; you have explicitly chosen to use the student lease contract. This sheet has been updated more than one year ago. Yes. You can already terminate your student lease before entering the lease. You can do so up to 1 month before the due date for the lease, on the condition of just reasons and proof.

question: Can the landlord index the rent in Brussels?
answer: For more information, see our sheets: "Can my rent be indexed (Brussels) " "How to calculate the indexation of my rent (Brussels) " on the Brussels Housing website.

question: Am I entitled to a Grapa if I have the resources?
answer: To benefit from a Grapa, you must meet certain conditions. For more information, see our sheet <unk>What are the conditions for benefiting from the Grapa?<unk> One of the conditions for benefiting from the Grapa is not to have sufficient resources. To determine whether you are entitled to the Grapa and the amount of your Grapa, the Federal Pension Service (SFP) takes into account: your resources; the resources of certain people with whom you share your main residence (i.e. the place where you usually and effectively live).
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: What is a seizure?
answer: Seizure is a way of enforcing a judgment. It is always carried out through a bailiff. It consists of taking the property of a person who owes money to another person. The property is sold in a public auction, and the creditor is repaid with the money of the sale. To carry out the seizure of income, furniture or real estate, the creditor must be authorized by an enforceable title. Moreover, the claim must be certain: i.e. seriously questionable; enforceable, i.e. the creditor can demand payment; liquid, i.e. one can pay (or I guess temporarily).
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts

question: I am a landlord. What does my fire insurance cover in Brussels?
answer: The following risks are covered unless your insurance contract excludes them: fire; damage caused by lightning, explosion, implosion, fall or impact of aircraft or objects. The objects or animals that fall or are projected to fall (e.g. car, pigeons, etc.) are normally also covered by fire insurance: damage caused by bombings, damage caused by labour conflicts, damage caused by storms, damage caused by natural disasters. Other risks are regularly covered: electricity; smoke; water; civil liability.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance, renting an apartment, in Brussels

question: Does the short-term lease automatically end when it expires in Brussels?
answer: Before you go any further, it's not because you've signed a short-term lease that you're still in a short-term lease! Before you terminate the lease, you need to check what kind of lease you're in. For more information, see the question "Is my lease a short-term lease?"
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, End of short-term lease (3 years or less), As scheduled

question: How does an expulsion take place in Brussels?
answer: The eviction can only take place when the landlord has an enforceable title! The enforceable title is usually a judgment of the justice of the peace who pronounces the end of the lease; the eviction of the tenant is a bit different. So you will not be able to be evicted by your landlord after the expiry of the notice of eviction. If he does not have the permission of the judge, here are the different stages of the eviction procedure: The eviction must be requested from the justice of the peace where the tenant is located.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), End and break, Expulsion

question: I'm married. We're married. Should I recognize my child?
answer: No. If you are married and a child is born during the marriage, the mother's husband is presumed to be the father of the child. This is the presumption of paternity. No need for recognition of paternity, filiation is established automatically. At the birth of the child, you must only make a birth declaration to the municipality of the place of birth. If you were separated at the time of the conception of the child, you are still presumed to be the father of the child that your (ex-)wife gives birth to.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Who can I contact if my employer does not comply with its obligations?
answer: You have several possibilities 1. You can report the facts to the social inspection services. Depending on the problem that concerns you, you can go to different services. To the prevention advisor or to the trusted person of your company. This informal way sometimes allows you to solve the problems before going to the complaint. To the SPF Employment Control and Social Concertation: The Directorate controls the welfare at work: for example for work in an unsafe space, the absence of protective tools, harassment, etc.
topics: Work, salaried work

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce because of an irretrievable break-up. How do I file for divorce on my own because of an irretrievable break-up?
answer: You must submit your divorce petition to the family court. To find out which court has territorial jurisdiction, see the schedule in the standard documents. If your divorce petition is based on your de facto separation, you can submit your petition by subpoena. You must submit your petition to the registry office, or send it by registered mail. You must pay a contribution to the legal aid course. You pay the deposit fee at the end of the proceedings.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, only one of us wants to file for divorce

question: I am in Belgium illegally (without papers). I am a foreigner. Can the municipality refuse me to recognize my child?
answer: Yes The civil registry officer (OEC) can refuse to recognize paternity if he suspects fraud. He can refuse to recognize paternity if there are serious indications that: you do not have a parent-child relationship with the child; or your intentions are solely to obtain a file for your right of residence, that of the child or the mother. For example, you have already recognized a child with another woman in "illegal residence" and you do not live with the child you want to recognize. In this case, the OEC can find out that your intentions are not to introduce this child but to the mother of the child.
topics: Foreigners, illegal stay

question: I'm living with my parents, I'm not working, how can I dispute the amount I have to pay?
answer: If you do not agree with the amount of contributions mentioned on the role-extract warning you have just received, you can: 1. Apply for correction to your tax office. 2. Apply to the Advocate General. 4. You can file a complaint with the Advocate General in charge of the establishment of income taxes.
topics: Money, taxes

question: Will I be registered if I am in collective debt settlement?
answer: Yes. You will be registered in two places: 1. in the file of the central bank of loans to individuals The fact of being in collective debt settlement (RCD) is communicated in the form of a notice by the registry of the labour court to the National Bank of Belgium, in the file of the central bank of loans to individuals. You cannot have a new credit, unless authorised by the central bank of loans to individuals. Court notices registered by the central bank of loans to individuals are kept: 12 from the end of the settlement plan, 3 years from the decision to reject the application for a settlement plan, 3 years from the date of revocation of the decision to revoke the settlement plan.
topics: Money, debts, solutions to combat debt, collective debt settlement (RCD), effects of the procedure

question: I'm in Belgium illegally (without papers). I'm in Belgium illegally. Am I entitled to a lawyer?
answer: If you are in a closed centre, you are in principle entitled to a lawyer paid by the State for the procedures related to the acquisition of a residence permit on Belgian territory, i.e. the application for regularisation of residence and the procedures necessary or related to that application; the appeal against an order to leave the territory; the application for international protection (order BA); the application for asylum (order BA) or the procedure before the General Commission for Refugees and Displaced Persons (CGRA), the Council for the Protection of Aliens (CEC), the ECJ (ECJ), etc. To obtain this type of legal aid, you must submit a document stating that you are entitled to a residence permit during your stay abroad.
topics: Foreigners, illegal stay

question: Who can apply for an observation measure when there is an emergency?
answer: In emergency situations, any interested person may apply to the King's Prosecutor. He may order observation. In practice, this emergency procedure is much more frequent than the "normal" procedure before the Justice of the Peace. The King's Prosecutor must be medically justified. The King's Prosecutor can only make his decision after having requested the written opinion of a doctor he or she has designated, if he or she has the right to do so.
topics: Privacy, personal protection measures, protection of the mentally ill, treatment in a hospital environment

question: Before which court to go in case of a problem related to parentage?
answer: The family court deals with all matters of parentage. You should in principle go to the family court of the child's home. To find the address of the court, go to this link. To contact the family court, there are two possibilities: You should in principle make a citation. You should go to a bailiff, who officially transmits your request and summons the other parties (probably the other parent and the child) to the court.

question: I have an accident during a teleworking day, is it a work accident?
answer: Yes, if the accident occurs at the place provided for your teleworking. This place can be provided by the work regulations, by your employment contract, by a text message, an e-mail, an internal mail, etc.; anything authorizing teleworking, in a general or occasional way. If the place is not written by you, presumed teleworking at a place of residence, or at your usual place of teleworking, teleworking comes into force. The hours provided for teleworking during teleworking are not stipulated by the regulations.
topics: Work, salaried work, illness and accident, accident at work

question: I'm staying with a friend. My friend damaged my house, who should do the repairs?
answer: This file was updated more than a year ago. So if the friend you're hosting damaged your home: To your landlord (if you're a tenant yourself): you're responsible for the repairs to be made. It's up to you. To your friend: your friend is responsible for the damage he's doing. So you can ask him to make the repairs or to reimburse you for the repairs you've done.
topics: Accommodation, accommodation, end of the accommodation

question: I'm unemployed and I live as a couple. I live as a couple, how much unemployment benefit will I get?
answer: Before going any further, only ex-employees are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed couples are not entitled to unemployment benefits, except if they were employed before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. But civil servants have specific rules.

question: Can I ask the CPAS to reconsider its decision?
answer: Yes. It is sometimes interesting, in certain circumstances, to ask the CPAS to review its decision rather than applying to the labour court. The CPAS can withdraw a decision it has made and replace it with another. You will save time compared to filing an appeal in court. The CPAS must review its decision: when it comes to agreeing, refusing or revising the social integration income (RIS), the individual social integration project (PIIS), or social integration through employment; if your situation has changed; if the rules have changed; the CPAS has made a mistake; if your statements were incorrect or incomplete.
topics: Social protection, Aid from the CPAS, Challenging the decision of the CPAS, Asking the CPAS to review its decision

question: Am I entitled to the birth allowance if I have a miscarriage or if my child is stillborn in Brussels?
answer: Yes, if your pregnancy has lasted at least 180 days, you are entitled to the birth allowance. You must send the birth certificate drawn up by the municipality to your family allowance fund. On the other hand, if the miscarriage occurs before the 180th day of pregnancy, you are not entitled to the birth allowance. The duration of the pregnancy is calculated from conception. The birth allowance is always paid to the child's mother.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowance, Birth allowance Child born before 1 January 2020, Child born in Brussels

question: What income is taken into account for legal aid if I live with other people?
answer: Before proceeding further, check first whether you are in one of the categories entitling you to legal aid based on your specific situation, without examining your income. The BAJ also calculates your monthly income: you have to pay unemployment benefits, sickness benefits, pension, etc. The BAJ also calculates your monthly income: unemployment benefits, sickness benefits, pension, etc.

question: I'm getting married. What documents do I need to submit in order to get married?
answer: In order for your declaration of marriage to be registered, you must present the following documents to the municipality (to the civil registry officer): a certified copy of your birth certificate; proof of your identity; proof of your nationality; proof of your celibacy, and if you have been married in the past: proof of divorce or annulment of your previous marriages; proof of your registration in the population registers, the current register of foreigners or the waiting register, and proof of your illegal residence in Belgium or, in the absence thereof, proof of your habitual residence in Belgium for more than 3 months; any other authentic document that can prove that you are married.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Can my landlord evict me if I'm in arrears in Wallonia?
answer: No, a simple late payment does not lead to eviction. To evict you, your landlord must have an enforceable title. Automatic breach of lease and automatic eviction are not allowed. For example, your lease cannot provide that 3 late payments end the lease and the landlord can throw you out. If you do not pay your rent, your landlord must go to the justice of the peace and ask him:

question: Should I ask for my Grapa or is it automatic?
answer: The Federal Pension Service (SFP) automatically checks whether you are entitled to the Grapa if you: submit an application for a pension in Belgium; already receive a pension from the SFP; receive a disability allowance and are 65 years of age; receive a social integration income (RIS) and are of retirement age. In all other cases, you must submit an application. You can choose your application: online via mypension.be; you submit your application by telephone; call 1765 and then type 3.1.
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: I am a self-employed worker. What steps should I take in the early stages of pregnancy if I am a self-employed worker?
answer: For example, a dependent for sickness and disability insurance is not necessarily a dependent for unemployment, nor for family allowances. The only steps you have to take are social security (indemnity during maternity leave, family allowances, birth allowance, etc.). These steps are to be taken with your employer and your social security mutual. For the rest, there is no specific step to be taken, except to replace your absence, etc.

question: I am a European. Will my right of residence be withdrawn if I apply for social assistance as a European citizen?
answer: Before going any further, the right to social integration and the social situation of Europeans depend on several elements: The right to social integration card (CPF card, annex 19, annex 19ter, annex 20, annex 21 or annex 35. The date on which the residence permit was issued. The reason for residence: European worker (and his family members), European jobseeker (and his other family members), categories of Europeans (student, the holder of the CPF card considers sufficient resources). It is always necessary to distinguish between the right to social integration card (CPF card) and the right to social assistance.

question: Can my employer terminate my CDD if I am unable to work?
answer: Only under certain conditions. Your employer can terminate your CDD before the scheduled date, if you are unable to work (illness or accident), in 2 cases. If the CDD is for less than 3 months, your employer can terminate it: after the first half of the contract; and after 7 consecutive days of incapacity for work (7 days without interruption). Dismissal can therefore only take place from the 8th day of incapacity. If the CDD is for 3 months or more, your employer can terminate it after more than 6 months of incapacity for work (6 consecutive months).
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, termination of the fixed-term contract (CDD)

question: What is the withdrawal clause in Brussels?
answer: It is a clause in the fire insurance contract of the landlord's rented dwelling. Some landlords wish the tenant to be covered by their own fire insurance. In this case, the landlord incorporates a waiver clause against the tenant, in the lease contract and in his own fire insurance contract. He then waives his right to sue you if you are responsible for a fire. You are as a tenant automatically covered by the landlord's insurance. The advantage of this insurance is that the landlord is sure that his tenant is insured and that the damage caused to his dwelling will be properly covered.

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce by mutual consent. When will we be officially divorced (divorce by mutual consent)?
answer: You are not yet officially divorced at the time when the family court issues its divorce judgment. You will be officially divorced after the mention of the divorce in the database of civil status acts (BAEC). You must wait until the appeal deadline has passed (one month from the date of the divorce judgment), and neither of you must appeal (including appeal or opposition). After this deadline, and if there is no appeal, the divorce judgment is entered by the clerk in the BAEC.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I'm renting a cot. My insurance doesn't pay for all the repairs. Is that normal in Wallonia?
answer: To know the terms of compensation of your insurance contract, read your contract. There are several limitations of compensation. The deductible Some contracts provide that in the event of an accident, part of the amount owed is covered by the insured. This amount is called EUR EUR. The insurer will only pay the costs in excess of this amount. You have a deductible of EUR 500 and the value of the kitchen that burned was EUR 10,000, so your insurer will pay you EUR 300,000 The deductible is proportional to the amount insured.

question: To which maintenance debtors can the CPAS require me to apply for RIS aid?
answer: Only to: your spouse or ex-spouse; your first-degree ancestors and descendants (i.e. your parents and children, but not your grandparents or grandchildren); your adoptive and adoptive parents. Particularity for your ex-spouse: the CPAS can only refer you to him if he still has a maintenance obligation to you. Your ex-spouse no longer has an obligation to you: if the divorce proceedings are definitively closed before your application for assistance to the CPAS; and if the divorce judgment does not grant you a maintenance benefit.

question: Can I take a vacation if I'm unemployed?
answer: Yes. You are entitled to 4 weeks of "paid" leave per year (24 days, knowing that 1 week equals 6 days, so including Saturdays). You receive your unemployment benefits during these days of leave, unless they are covered by a vacation allowance or an exit allowance paid to you by your last employer. You can take these days whenever you want, but you must at least take a period of 6 consecutive days (including Saturdays) over the year. You do not have to notify ONEM before taking your vacation, nor get its authorization.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment after a job

question: I think my dwelling is unhealthy. I am contacting the Walloon Region or the competent municipality to have a health and overcrowding survey carried out. What are the consequences of the result of the survey in Wallonia?
answer: Before going any further, from 1 November to 15 March 2022, in Wallonia, evictions are prohibited. Except for exceptions, for more information, see our section "Expulsion". The conclusions of the inquiry are expressed on the hygiene and non-overcrowding of a dwelling according to the regional criteria (Walloon Sustainable Housing Code). Depending on the case, the dwelling is qualified as: without failing to comply with the minimum criteria of hygiene (unhealthy) to the habitable-improvable inhabitable-improvable non-improvable overcrowded.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: What if the amount of the administrator's fees and fees seems exaggerated?
answer: 1. You can ask the administrator for a detailed statement of his costs and fees. The administrator must be able to justify all expenses. His remuneration must always be validated and authorized by the justice of the peace. 2. In case of disagreement, you, or; your trusted person, or; any interested person, can contact the justice of the peace. The justice of the peace is responsible for controlling the administrator. He will be able to check in the reports whether the costs claimed by the administrator seem exaggerated or not with regard to the work carried out.

question: Can I sign several replacement contracts with the same employer?
answer: Yes, but the total duration of successive replacement contracts cannot exceed 2 years. With a replacement contract, you are in a precarious situation. You do not know when your contract will expire because you do not know when the worker you are replacing will return. For example, in the event of illness, you do not know when the sick worker will be cured and able to return to work. When the worker you are replacing returns, your contract expires without notice or compensation, or with a reduced notice. For this exception to the rights of the worker, the replacement contract must be valid.
topics: Work, salaried work, employment contract

question: What are my rights (remuneration, hours, etc.) if I resume part-time work after a reintegration journey?
answer: The reintegration plan provides for: your duties and tasks; your working hours; your remuneration; your holidays; the duration of the reintegration plan; etc. You keep your original employment contract. This contract is not amended, neither by an amendment nor by a new contract. But the reintegration plan is applied during the duration of the plan. Some information is therefore adapted, such as your schedule or your remuneration. All part-time working hours are possible, as long as they fall within the "frame" of hours set in the work regulations. Your remuneration is proportional to your working time, if the reintegration plan provides for part-time work.
topics: Work, sickness - incapacity for work, resumption of adapted work during incapacity, reintegration journey

question: What charges do I have to pay in Wallonia?
answer: In the case of multi-dwelling properties, if the amount of the charges is not a lump sum, you should check the rules for the calculation of the charges and the distribution of the charges; the existence of individual or collective tenants, etc. This rule does not clarify the last clause of the lease, which prohibits the foreclosure of the lease.

question: Is an orphan child entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Brussels?
answer: Yes. Amounts of the orphan child supplement (Brussels) 1 deceased parent 2 deceased parents 50% of the basic family allowance 100% of the basic family allowance If the child has only one known parent, and if that parent dies, he is entitled to the supplement for 2 deceased parents. If a parent is declared absent, he counts as a deceased parent for family allowances. The child is entitled to the orphan supplement If the child is adopted, and if one of the parents survives, the child is entitled to the orphan supplement.

question: Can the landlord terminate the unofficial tenancy agreement before it expires in Wallonia?
answer: If your lease is also a main residence lease: The landlord can terminate the lease under certain conditions: to personally occupy the dwelling; or to do important work; or by paying compensation. For more information, see the section "End and termination of the main residence lease". If it is not a main residence lease: You must check whether your lease agreement provides that the landlord can terminate the lease before the due date or not.

question: What should I do if there is no state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: You are presumed to have received the rented property in the state in which it is when you return it if: there is no state of the premises at the entrance; and your lease is post-December 31, 1983. This presumption applies even if your contract contains the clause: "the building is in a good state of maintenance". These clauses are not valid. This presumption is favorable to you, as a tenant. If your landlord believes that there is damage, he can try to report proof, by any means, that this state of the premises was different at the entrance.

question: How many times can I ask for an extension for exceptional circumstances in Wallonia?
answer: For example, your lease is due to expire on 1 March, but on 5 January you fall seriously ill, you can apply for an extension of your lease. You are granted a six-month extension. The lease will therefore expire on 1 September. If you have a relapse, you can apply again for a final extension. For more information, see the sheet "Under what conditions can I get an extension for exceptional circumstances?"

question: If my landlord is doing some work on my apartment, can I apply for a rent reduction in Brussels?
answer: This lease reduction is proportional to the duration of the work and the part of the dwelling from which you are deprived. Note that many lease agreements provide that the tenant is not entitled to the rent reduction and it is legal to rent. So your lease is valid. So if your landlord is doing energy performance improvement work and they are planning to do it for more than 60 days, this rent reduction is proportional to the duration of the work and the part of the dwelling from which you are deprived.

question: What are my rights as a patient?
answer: As a patient, you have the following rights: to receive quality care; to freely choose your caregiver. Emergency, circumstances or the caregiver's specialization may limit this choice; to be informed about your health condition and its likely evolution. The information must be clear and oral. It may also be accompanied by a written one. To consent freely and informally to each intervention or treatment. You must accept an intervention or treatment. You can also refuse an intervention or treatment. Consent must be given for each step of a treatment.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, Patient's rights and medical records

question: I have signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Should I help my legal cohabitant if he is in need?
answer: In principle no. Legal cohabitants do not have a duty to help and assist (unlike married couples). But you must contribute to the household expenses in proportion to your faculties. Each of you must use your own resources as a priority for the household (rent, food, electricity bill, etc.). The contribution to the household expenses can be either financial or in kind (child rearing, household chores, etc.). The concrete distribution of household expenses can result in the one with more income helping the other financially.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: What are the conditions for being entitled to youth leave?
answer: You must have completed your studies during the year of your vacation. You must have worked for at least 1 month during the year of your vacation. This month can be provided under one or more employment contracts, with one or more employers. It can be full-time or part-time, but you must have worked for at least 13 days in total. You must be entitled to less than 20 days of legal leave since this system is a complement to your legal leave, not an alternative. You must be employed by an employment contract at the time you take your legal vacation.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, youth holidays

question: How to create a rental guarantee through the CPAS in Wallonia?
answer: You must submit an application to the CPAS of the municipality where you live at the time you make the application. Except for exceptions. For more information, see the question "I am leaving a reception centre, which CPAS should I contact for a rental guarantee agreement?" If your application is accepted, the CPAS can grant aid in three forms: in the form of a letter of guarantee (or so-called "credit") : the CPAS commits itself in writing to the landlord to intervene in case of a problem at the end of the lease.

question: Can my employer fire me for any reason?
answer: No 1. First of all, the employer cannot abuse his right to dismiss. Abuse of the right to dismiss is punishable. For more information, see our sheet <unk> When can a dismissal be considered abusive? <unk> 2. In the meantime, your employer cannot dismiss you in a "manifestly unreasonable" way. The dismissal is manifestly unreasonable if the following conditions are met: you are employed for an indefinite period of time; the dismissal is based on reasons: <unk> that have nothing to do with your aptitude or your conduct; or that are not based on the necessities of the company, the establishment or the social service. <unk> If they are manifestly unreasonable, you must prove it.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, dismissal

question: My lease is not a student lease. Can I terminate the lease before the term stipulated in the contract in Wallonia?
answer: To find out if your lease is a simple residential lease, and to find out if you can terminate the lease before it expires, and how to proceed, see the section entitled "End and termination" in the section devoted to the obligations of the main residence in Wallonia. To find out if your lease is a common residential lease, confirm the associated questions. To find out if your lease is a simple residential lease, see the related questions. In principle, you cannot terminate the lease before the lease expires, unless this possibility is foreseen by the parties.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), End and termination of the lease

question: Can the landlord claim the entire rent from a single roommate in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. Yes, if the tenants are in solidarity. If you have signed an official lease, the tenants are in solidarity. It is the law that provides for this. If you have signed an unofficial lease, it is necessary to see if your signature provides for a solidarity clause. This is often the case. The solidarity clause can appear in several places in the contract: at the beginning of the contract, when designating the tenants.
topics: Accommodation, rental in Brussels, shared accommodation (Brussels), rentals, charges and insurance

question: How to avoid deportation in Brussels?
answer: To avoid eviction: Before your landlord starts proceedings If your lease expires: If your landlord has sent you notice but you haven't found a solution to relocate, you can ask for an extension of the lease for exceptional circumstances. For more information if: your home is in Wallonia, see the sheet "What, to whom and when does the extension for exceptional circumstances serve?" If your home is in Brussels, see the sheet "What, to whom and when does the extension for exceptional circumstances serve you?" If you are in arrears with your landlord: if your landlord threatens eviction, try to find a solution with him.

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. How can I file a complaint for domestic violence?
answer: You have to go to a police station, show up at the front desk and say that you want to file a complaint for domestic violence. You can file a complaint with any police station. Most police departments have a specialized agent who is specifically trained to deal with victims of domestic violence. This officer will ask you for a number of questions. In addition, if it is useful for the continuation of the investigation (for example, if there are obvious signs of beatings), he will ask you if you want to undergo a medical examination or if he can take pictures of your injuries.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: I'm an employee (or unemployed). I'm giving birth later than planned. What are the consequences on my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see this sheet "Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: What is the seizure of furniture?
answer: In order to carry out this seizure and sell the furniture, the creditor must have a judgment condemning you (you, the debtor) to pay him a certain amount of money. If you refuse to pay the debt, or if you do not consider yourself able to execute the judgment, the bailiff must send you a verbal order to pay (last notice) at least 24 hours before the time of the sale. If you do not respond to this order, the bailiff may be surprised by an inventory of your property.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of movable property

question: How to create a rental guarantee through the CPAS in Brussels?
answer: If your application is accepted, the CPAS can grant aid in three forms: in the form of a letter of guarantee: the CPAS undertakes in writing to the landlord to intervene in the event of a problem at the end of the lease. In this case, it is not a rental guarantee (because there is no remittance of money) but a guarantee. So you can ask your landlord in advance for the amount of the guarantee: the CPAS returns the money.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental guarantee, Aid to create a rental guarantee, Aid from the CPAS

question: What happens if the protected person moves?
answer: In this case, the move does not change the protection measure; the protected person moves out of the canton of the justice of the peace. In this case, the justice of the peace can decide to: transfer the case to the justice of the peace competent for the new residence, or keep it. This decision cannot make the case the subject of a single appeal. Attention: the protected person can no longer travel alone. Click here to decide the case of the protected person moving abroad.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and/or the person (judicial protection), Amendment or termination of the measure for the administration of property and/or the person

question: What to do in case of abuse of the right of visit in Brussels?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. If your landlord abuses his right of access, you can go to the justice of the peace to settle this conflict and set the terms of the right of access. The right of access is not regulated by the law. So it is the contract, the customs and the principle of fairness that will guide the decision of the justice of the peace. If the justice of the peace believes that your landlord abused his right of access, he can punish him civilly (for example, sentenced him to pay damages and interest).
topics: Accommodation, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Visit of the accommodation

question: Will I lose my survivor's pension or my transition allowance if I remarry?
answer: Before going any further, please note that the rules are not exactly the same for wage earners, self-employed workers and civil servants.This sheet contains only general information.For any details or additional questions, please contact: Federal Pension Service (SFP) for wage earners and civil servants, National Institute of Social Insurance for Self-Employed Workers (INTIAS).Yes. But the transition allowance and survivor's pension are, in principle, abolished in the event of remarriage.
topics: Social Protection, Pensions and Grapa, Survival Pension

question: I ⁇ m being summoned to court. What is a discount?
answer: This is the postponement to a later hearing. At the first hearing, and under certain circumstances, the parties may decide by mutual agreement to request that the case be referred to a later hearing. If he agrees, the judge may set a new hearing date for: hearing the pleadings, or checking the status of this case, i.e. seeing where the parties are in their exchange of files, and seeing if a pleading date can be set.
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, Conduct of hearings

question: Where can I find a debt mediation service (friendly)?
answer: To find out which debt mediation service is closest to you, go to the website of the credit observatory. Only accredited persons or institutions can be debt mediators. Anyone can't improvise as a debt mediator. Most CPAS have a debt mediation service. You can contact the CPAS in your place of residence. Some ASBL also do debt mediation.
topics: Money, debts, solutions to fight debt, debt mediation by mutual agreement

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). Can I receive maternity allowance and paid leave at the same time?
answer: For civil servants, the rules on maternity leave vary depending on the department or institution in which you work. Please inquire with your personnel department. No. Maternity benefits cannot be combined with certain other income, including: paid leave; a notice allowance (in the event of dismissal); deferred remuneration paid to temporary teachers; other legal benefits.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: Do I receive an allowance for helping the elderly in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. If your health condition worsens, you can ask for a reassessment of your disability to the APA service of your mutual. You must ask for your file to be reviewed. You must: connect to Wal protect; click on <unk>Report a change or ask for a revision<unk>. Find out more before submitting your application. You can ask for help from: your municipality; your mutual; your CPAS; a service or an association that helps the disabled (the DGPH resumes some of these services on its website); a social worker from a disability contact: you can find the list of disabled on the Wiki FAQ.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Wallonia

question: Can I take a picture of a person without their consent?
answer: No. This is called the right to the image. An image is: a photo; a video; a portrait; a medium on which the person is reproduced. The right to the image is the right to take the image; to broadcast the image. This means that you must have the person's consent to take the photo and broadcast the photo. You must ask for these agreements separately. It is better to ask for the person's written consent to have the proof. There are 3 exceptions. The person is not easily identifiable on the image.
topics: Privacy, Respect for privacy

question: I'm a self-employed worker. My child was stillborn, am I entitled to maternity leave?
answer: Yes, if your pregnancy has lasted at least 180 days from conception. It does not matter if you are: a salaried worker; or unemployed; or self-employed. If you give birth before 180 days of pregnancy, you are not entitled to maternity leave. But you can be incapacitated if you meet the conditions.
topics: Work, work and parenting, "independent" maternity leave

question: My financial situation has improved. Am I still entitled to legal aid?
answer: If your financial situation improves during the period when your lawyer is working on your case, you must inform the legal aid office (BAJ) about it. If your income exceeds the threshold amount, legal aid will be withdrawn from you. To know this threshold amount, see the explanatory diagram in the standard documents. If your financial situation improves, you must inform the BAJ. If your lawyer learns that your financial situation has improved, he will inform the BAJ and he will file a request for withdrawal of legal aid.
topics: Justice, Lawyer, Legal aid (ex pro deo), During and after legal aid (ex pro deo)

question: The landlord doesn't reimburse me for the excess of my charges.
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. If your landlord does not reimburse you for the sums paid in excess, send him a writ of habeas corpus specifying clearly and precisely what you are claiming. In principle, only the sums paid in the last 5 years before the letter was sent can be claimed. If your landlord does not reimburse you, you have 1 year, from the sending of the letter, to seize the justice of the peace.
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Charges, Calculation of charges

question: What steps should be taken with the employer to resume adapted work during the disability?
answer: The employer must agree to take you back part-time, under the conditions set by the doctor-advisor of the mutual (adjustment of your position, your hours, your tasks, etc.). This is a negotiated resumption of work. The employer is not obliged to take you back. If he refuses, you remain under the mutual and you receive your allowances. If he agrees, and if your adapted work is temporary, you can sign a contract with your employer specifying the nature of your work; the hours of work; the adapted work; the adapted work; the remuneration of the adapted work; the validity of the agreement.
topics: Work, Illness - incapacity for work, Resumption of adapted work during incapacity, Resumption of partial work with the authorization of the mutual (<unk> half-time medical <unk>)

question: What steps should be taken for a 9bis regularization?
answer: If you are an applicant for international protection, or if you can prove that it is impossible to prove your identity, you must provide proof of your residence permit, your place of residence, your place of residence (i.e. the place where your application for a residence permit will be filed), the date of your application for a residence permit, the date of your application for a residence permit, the date of your application for a residence permit, the date of your application for a residence permit, etc. If you are unable to provide proof of your residence permit, you must provide proof of your residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, humanitarian regularization (9bis)

question: What is the guardianship of a minor child?
answer: Guardianship is a measure of protection of a minor child. It is put in place when parental authority cannot be exercised by his parents. This is the case: if both parents are deceased, or; if the parents are not able to exercise parental authority. For example because they are the subject of a measure of administration of the person. The purpose of guardianship is to protect the person and the property of the child.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, guardianship

question: I'm self-employed. My baby has to stay in the hospital after birth. What are the consequences on my leave if I'm self-employed?
answer: Before going any further, the rules and conditions of maternity leave are not exactly the same for female employees as for self-employed women. The independent scheme applies to: self-employed women; caregivers of self-employed women; wives of self-employed women. For convenience, the term "self-employed woman" is used here in the broad sense, covering these three categories of women who benefit from maternity leave in the independent scheme. You can ask for your maternity leave to be extended, but only if your baby is hospitalized for more than 7 days after birth. The duration of this extension corresponds to the number of full weeks during which your baby has been hospitalized.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: I'm married. Should I help my spouse's parents who are in need?
answer: Yes, you must help your spouse's parents financially if they are in need. Marriage creates many rights and obligations for spouses. Among your obligations, you must provide for your spouse's needs AND his or her parents if he or she cannot help them himself. It is said that you are a debtor of alimony, or a debtor of alimony, to your in-laws. However, the aid is not automatic. Several conditions must be met: You must be a good ally.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Can you force your neighbor to repair the neighborhood wall?
answer: You can force your neighbor to have the neighborhood fence repaired at common expense. This sharing is justified by the utility that each of you derives from it. By "neighborhood fence", we mean: the walls, hedges, palisades, fences or any other element. All these elements presume the indication that each of the neighborhood owners owns half of the neighborhood fence. Each neighbor-owner has a right of co-ownership on the neighborhood fence. If the fence is your neighbor's, the neighbor who wishes to make repairs must consult the other neighbor and get his consent to do the work.
topics: Accommodation, neighbourhood, neighbourhood

question: I'm married. Do I need my spouse's consent to pursue a profession?
answer: No. You can choose your profession: without the consent of your spouse; even if the exercise of this profession has financial consequences. However, if this profession is likely to cause serious damage to the material or moral interests of your minor children or your couple, your spouse may ask the family court to prohibit you from exercising the envisaged profession. If you wish to use your spouse's name in your professional activities, his consent is essential.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: Which minors are equated with adults for the RIS?
answer: In principle, you have to be of legal age to be entitled to social integration income (RIS). But some minors are treated as adults, and are therefore entitled to RIS. These are: the minor who proves that she is pregnant; the minor who has one or more children under her care (not necessarily her own children); the minor emancipated by marriage. Attention, the extended minority no longer exists since 1 September 2019.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), Being of age or assimilated to an adult

question: Does my adult child who lives with me still benefit from my insurance?
answer: Yes, there is no age limit for your children to be covered by your family insurance, even if your child is of legal age.
topics: Money, Insurance, Family Insurance, Covered Children

question: I'm married. Can my spouse force me to work to cover household expenses?
answer: No. But you both have to contribute to the household expenses according to your faculties. You and your spouse have to contribute financially to all the expenses related to the community of life created by marriage, depending on your level of personal resources. You cannot escape this obligation. This means that you cannot provide, by marriage contract, for not contributing to the household expenses. Nor can you, in the event of separation or divorce, demand the repayment of your investment from the other (as long as each of you contributes in proportion to his faculties).
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: The parents are not married together. The child's mother is married to someone other than the father. Can I recognize my child before it is born if the mother is married to someone else?
answer: No, in principle, you can't recognize your child's father before he's born. For example, even if the child's mother and her husband have been officially residing in different homes for a long time, it's in principle from the time of birth that the spouses' places of residence must be examined to determine whether the legal presumption of paternity applies. So there's a good chance that the court will refuse to recognize a portion of your child's DNA until it's proven.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

answer: Before we go any further, the contents of this sheet do not apply if your lease is a main residence lease. In the majority of kots lease contracts, it is the lease that indicates the amount and type of money you need to create for the lease. This is provided for freely. Please specify what is provided for in the lease. If your lease does not come to fruition, you cannot apply for a lease.

question: I'm an employee, is my maternity leave extended if I'm expecting twins?
answer: For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. Please inquire with your personnel department. Yes. The duration of maternity leave is extended to 19 weeks in the case of multiple births, i.e. in the case of twins, triplets, etc. You must choose one. You must take a mandatory maternity leave of one week, a mandatory postnatal leave of one week, a mandatory postnatal leave of two weeks, a mandatory postnatal leave of two weeks, a mandatory postnatal leave of two weeks, a mandatory postnatal leave of one week, a mandatory postnatal leave of two weeks, a mandatory postnatal leave of one week, a mandatory postnatal leave of one week, etc. You can choose these rules.

question: What is the insertion contract in the Walloon Region?
answer: Attention, the internship in the company, called the integration internship in the Walloon Region, is abolished from 1 January 2019. The integration contract is a full-time employment contract, carried out with an employer but paid by the ONEM (work allowance for a maximum of 12 months, etc.). To be entitled to it, you must meet several conditions. This must be a full-time employment contract, for a fixed or indefinite period, but for a minimum of 12 months. The young worker must be under 25 years of age at the time the contract begins.

question: I want to get a divorce by mutual consent. How do I determine the amount of alimony?
answer: Divorce by mutual consent means that you both agree to divorce and that you settle on your own, in agreements, all the issues related to the end of your marriage. These agreements are called "pre-divorce agreements by mutual consent". They represent a real divorce contract. When drawing up these agreements, you must agree and set the amount of alimony together.
topics: Family, alimony, alimony (between spouses or ex-spouses), alimony in divorce proceedings, divorce by mutual consent

question: I have been summoned to court. Can I contact the judge directly?
answer: No. You can't contact the judge directly. You can't make an appointment directly with the judge of your choice, either by phone or otherwise. You can, however, contact the clerk, who is sort of the clerk of the judge. If you want to ask the judge to settle your dispute, you have to "seize" him. You have to ask him officially to take care of your dispute: either you file a petition; or you file a joint petition with your attorney (also called a voluntary appearance); or you send an objection.

question: I'm joining a Belgian. I'm in Belgium. How does the family reunification procedure with a Belgian go?
answer: You can also submit this document at a later date if you no longer have it in your possession. You must submit your application to the municipal administration of your place of residence. Your application must contain the following documents: The document proving that you are a member of a family (marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.); A proof of identity (identity card or passport). You can also submit this document at a later date if you no longer have it in your possession. The identity card of the family member with whom you are moving.

question: I'm a student in Belgium. Can I stay in Belgium after I finish my studies?
answer: Before going any further, this question only concerns non-European students. At the end of your studies, you can no longer extend your residence card as a student. If you want to stay in Belgium, you can get a 12-month residence card to look for a job or to start a business. The conditions for obtaining this 12-month residence card are: having a valid passport; obtaining a higher education diploma in Belgium or in another country of the European Union if you are a "mobile student"; having a health insurance card in Belgium; having sufficient resources or not being a danger to public order and security.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, student

question: How long does it take for my water bill to expire in Brussels?
answer: Your water debt is prescribed after a period of 5 years. The period begins to run at the payment deadline indicated on the invoice (usually 5 to 15 days after the consumption of the previous month). But be careful, the prescription can be suspended or interrupted. For more information, see "Can I invoke the prescription not to pay my debt?"

question: Can my landlord claim arrears of rent indexation in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. If you do not pay the indexed rent when your landlord has asked you to do so, your landlord has 1 year to ask the justice of the peace to order you to pay the indexation arrears. After that, the indexation arrears will be prescribed. The landlord can no longer ask for them.

question: My child is stillborn, can I declare his birth?
answer: Yes, if the pregnancy lasted 140 days or more. The duration of the pregnancy is calculated from conception. If the pregnancy lasted 180 days or more, the declaration of your child is mandatory. The municipality makes a lifeless child act when it receives the medical certificate proving the birth of your child. This certificate is sent to the municipality by: the head of the hospital or institution where you gave birth; or the doctor or midwife who gave birth to you outside the hospital (for example if you gave birth at home).

question: Recommendations before signing a lease in Wallonia?
answer: Before signing a lease, it is very important to: visit the dwelling so as not to sign a lease if the dwelling does not meet the minimum sanitary criteria. If it is a small dwelling or a charcoal dwelling or a light dwelling, make sure that the landlord has obtained a rental permit. For a main residence lease: If the dwelling or the dwelling does not meet the sanitary criteria, the dwelling must not be rented.

question: I am a recognised refugee or I have been granted subsidiary protection status, am I entitled to the RIS?
answer: Yes. Recognised refugees and foreigners who have obtained subsidiary protection status are entitled to social integration income (RIS). Please note: applicants for international protection who are awaiting recognition of their status are not entitled to RIS. During the procedure, they may be entitled to material assistance (organised by Fedasil). For more information, see the Fedasil website. Recognised refugees usually have a certificate of registration in the register of foreigners: A card valid for 5 years.

question: Who has to pay for the last illness expenses?
answer: In principle, the costs of last illness are paid with the money of the estate. But when there is not enough money in the estate and all the heirs have renounced the estate, do they have to pay the funeral expenses? If the deceased was married, the surviving spouse has to pay the last illness expenses, even if he renounced the estate. Married couples are in solidarity for the debts of the household, and for the duration of the marriage.

question: Can the CPAS ask me to find a job in order to be entitled to social assistance?
answer: Only if you apply for financial assistance. For all other types of assistance, the CPAS cannot ask you to prove that you are willing to work. If you apply for financial social assistance, the CPAS can therefore ask you to prove that you are actively looking for work (such as for social integration income (RIS)). This condition is optional: the CPAS is not obliged to ask you to prove your willingness to work. It can grant you financial assistance without requiring you to prove your willingness to work. CPAS: If you are unable to work for health or equity reasons, the CPAS cannot ask you to prove your willingness to work.
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social assistance, willingness to work

question: I receive confidences as a professional. Does professional secrecy cover what the person did not confide in me?
answer: Yes, if you are bound by professional secrecy, this secrecy covers everything you learn in the exercise of your profession. Secrecy does not only cover what the person has expressly confided to you. It also covers everything you have seen, known, learned, observed, discovered or even surprised in the course of your profession. Professional secrecy also covers facts of public notoriety. Everyone knows that this is not an excuse for secrecy. Indeed, disclosure by a professional can give more force to certain information than a simple rumor.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: Where should I submit my application for income replacement allowance (ARR)?
answer: You must enter your application for income replacement allowance (ARR) on the website of the Directorate-General for the Disabled (DGPH). You must log in to "My Disability for Citizens" with your identity card and your identity card reader, Itsme, etc. The ARR application is made at the same time as the application for integration allowance (AI). You must enter only one application for these 2 allowances. If you need help to enter your application, you can: contact your nearest municipal mutual; your CPAS; a social worker from the DGPH by making an appointment.
topics: Social protection, disability, income replacement allowance (ARR)

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). What is the total duration of my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see our sheet "What are the conditions under which I am entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.
