question: Can I ask the Directorate-General for Disabled Persons not to recover the integration allowance (AI) that I received in excess?
answer: Yes, you have two possibilities to try not to reimburse the disabled. Contest the decision of the Directorate-General for Disabled Persons (DGPH) before the Labour Court. You have 3 months from the receipt of the letter of recommendation sent by the DGPH to contest the decision of the disabled. However, the appeal to the Labour Court does not suspend the recovery procedure. For more information, see our section "Contesting a social security decision". You have to ask the Minister who deals with allowances for disabled people to cancel AI; You have to waive the ceiling of the disabled. If you submit your application within 3 months of receiving the letter of recommendation sent by the DGPH, the recovery procedure is completely suspended.
topics: Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: The parents are not married together. The child's father is married to someone other than the mother. Can I be obliged to establish a father-child relationship if I am married to someone other than the mother?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. If the child has no legal father (i.e. the mother is unmarried, and neither you nor anyone else has recognized the child), an action for paternity can be brought before the family court of the child's home, by: the child himself if he is of legal age; his mother (or his guardian) if he is a minor. Proof of the child's paternity must be established with regard to you. In the absence of paternity, it must be proven that you are the biological father of the child.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

question: I am in Belgium illegally (without papers). I am in Belgium illegally. Can I recognize my child in Belgium?
answer: Yes, as a father, you can recognize your child born in Belgium if you meet one of the following criteria: you are domiciled in Belgium; you are habitually resident in Belgium at the time of recognition; your child was born in Belgium; your child's mother has her habitual residence in Belgium at the time of recognition.
topics: Foreigners, illegal stay

question: Does the judge automatically choose equal housing?
answer: No. The judge is not obliged to choose this type of accommodation in all cases. On the other hand, if one of the parents requests equal accommodation, the judge must examine this possibility. If he decides that equal accommodation is not appropriate, he must explain why. There are many reasons for refusing equal accommodation.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, accommodation of children, decision of a judge

question: I can't work temporarily because of an accident at work, am I paid?
answer: Before going any further, the rules for occupational accidents are very complex. This sheet does not explain them all in detail. This sheet applies only to workers in the private sector (not the public sector). For more information on occupational accidents, you can contact the following services: The prevention advisor of your company's internal and external prevention and protection service; The regional directorate for occupational well-being control responsible for your employer. The General Directorate for the Humanization of the Labor Rate: You can contact it in writing for any question about the consolidation of occupational accidents. The Federal Agency for Occupational Accidents (FARC), which replaces the Occupational Accidents Fund since January 1, 2017.
topics: Work, salaried work, illness and accident, accident at work

question: Can my landlord claim arrears of rent indexation in Brussels?
answer: Yes. If you do not pay the indexed rent when your landlord has asked you to do so, your landlord has one year to ask the justice of the peace to order you to pay the indexation arrears. After that, the indexation arrears will be prescribed. The landlord can no longer ask for them.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I'm getting married. What kind of marriage plan should I choose?
answer: If you choose the community of property system, you can distinguish between your own property and your common property. There are three distinct types of property: your own property. Your own property (or your personal property) includes property that you own before marriage, including property that is of a personal nature (such as memories, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, property acquired before marriage, etc.).
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I am an employer. Can I hire a foreigner who is staying illegally?
answer: Before hiring a foreign worker, you must: verify on his residence permit whether he has unlimited access to the labour market; verify if his residence permit is valid (not yet expired); have EUR EUR 18.00 to prove his residence permit in the event of a social inspection; declare the entry and exit of the worker from the service (DNAIMO declaration). If you do not comply with these obligations, you must also face penalties: imprisonment from 6 months to 3 years; and you must pay a fine of EUR 6,000.
topics: Foreigners, Foreigners and wage labour

question: Is there a maximum amount for the rental guarantee in Wallonia?
answer: It all depends on whether your lease is a main residence lease or not. Hypothesis 1 : your lease is a main residence lease. There is no rule to be followed regarding the lease lease. The amount and type to create the lease lease can be set freely in the lease contract. See what is provided in your lease contract. If the lease contract does not provide for a lease lease, the landlord cannot apply for a lease lease.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Guarantee of accommodation

question: I'm hosting a friend or relative. Do I have to host my child forever?
answer: No, you must not accommodate your child forever. You must accommodate him at least until he reaches the age of majority (18 years). Then you must accommodate him until his education is completed, and until he can pay for his own housing. If cohabitation is no longer possible before that, you can do so elsewhere than at home. But this does not relieve you of your parental obligations: you must provide for your child's needs until he is independent. If your child is accommodated elsewhere than at home, you must help him, for example, by paying a food contribution.
topics: Accommodation, accommodation, conditions of accommodation

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am a foreigner. Does my child born in Belgium automatically receive a residence permit?
answer: It depends. The child receives the same residence permit as his parents. If one of the parents is Belgian: the child is also Belgian. He automatically receives a Belgian identity card. If both parents are foreigners authorized to stay: the child receives the same residence permit as the parents. If one of the parents is illegally residing in Belgium and the other parent is legally residing in Belgium: the child receives the same residence permit as the parent who is legally residing in Belgium.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). What steps do I have to take to be entitled to maternity leave?
answer: Before going any further, please note that this sheet applies only to female employees and unemployed women. For self-employed women, please refer to our sheet "Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules for maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. For self-employed women, please refer to our sheet "Under what conditions are you entitled to maternity leave?"
topics: Social protection, Pregnancy and birth, Maternity leave, Conditions and procedures for entitlement to maternity leave

question: I make sure the landlord has a rental permit (Walloon Region).
answer: The permit must be applied for by the person (physical or legal) who rents the dwelling, i.e. the one who signs the lease agreement (the landlord, the manager, the real estate agency, etc.). To obtain a rental permit, it is necessary: (1) To obtain the documents to be completed. The documents to be completed are available at the municipal administration or on the website of the Walloon Region (see "Original procedure to be completed").
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Safety and health

question: Am I entitled to unemployment benefits if I'm in prison?
answer: No. You are not entitled to unemployment benefits during: detention in prison; pre-trial detention (even if you are subsequently acquitted); an exit permit; a prison leave; a single electronic surveillance arrest where you are deprived of your liberty at your residence during the investigation. During these periods, you are not available on the labour market. You do not meet all the conditions for entitlement to unemployment benefits. You must: notify your CAPAC or your union card, and the FOREM (or Act in Brussels), that you are completing your prison control mandate correctly. You are entitled to unemployment benefits for the days before entering prison.
topics: Social protection, prisoners and ex-prisoners, unemployment

question: When can the landlord ask me to index the rent in Brussels?
answer: Your landlord may ask you to index the rent: once a year; and at the earliest on the anniversary of your lease, i.e. the annual date of the beginning of your lease. If your landlord has forgotten to ask you to index the rent on the anniversary of the lease, he can ask you later, when he thinks about it. But he can ask you for indexation only: for the last 3 months; and for the future. The rest is lost. Your landlord must use the health index that he would have used on the date of the lease (not the one from the moment he asks for indexation in Brussels).
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent Indexation

question: I'm a parent, can we settle the food contribution issue amicably?
answer: Yes, if a dialogue is still possible you can try to find a negotiated solution to the problems related to the food contribution. Different ways are possible: You can start a family mediation. The mediator will help you to dialogue and find a solution together. Mediation is always done on a voluntary basis, the mediator has no power of coercion. If you reach an agreement through mediation, you can submit it to the family judge for him to homologate it, that is, for him to approve the agreement.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: The parents are not married together. The child's mother is married to someone other than the father. What steps can be taken to recognize the child if the child's mother and her husband have been officially residing at different addresses for more than 300 days?
answer: At the time of birth, the mother of the child and her husband have for more than 300 days: been registered at different addresses in the population registers; or been authorized by judgment to live separately without having resumed common life since; or introduced divorce proceedings and it has been certified that they live at different addresses. In these 3 cases, the presumption of paternity of the husband of the mother of the child does not play a role at birth. Unless the spouses make a joint declaration in order for the presumption to be maintained. If, during the declaration of birth, the separation of the spouses is noted, the husband is not recognized as the father of the child and you can ask the court to recognize paternity.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

question: Am I entitled to more days off if I work overtime?
answer: Yes, you are entitled to compensatory rest days. In principle, you can't work more than 8 hours a day, and 38 hours a week. These rules vary depending on the sector of your activity, because they can be provided for by collective bargaining agreements (CCAs). In particular, overtime must be authorized, and the employer must pay a notice. For more information, see our website. For more information, see our website. For more information, see our website. For more information, see our website.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, recovery

question: Who has to pay for repairs to damage caused by a burglary in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. As a tenant, you must in principle repair all damage to the rented dwelling. This will not be the case if you prove that it is due to a case of force majeure. A burglary or attempted burglary constitutes a case of force majeure. The judges have decided this with great difficulty. Damage to the building due to a burglary is therefore not the responsibility of the tenant, but of the landlord.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, expenses and insurance, Charges, Distribution of expenses

question: Can I take my leave during my notice?
answer: Yes, you can take your leave on your notice. If you are dismissed, the notice is suspended during your legal leave. If the notice had already begun before your vacation, it is suspended for the duration of your absence. For example, you must give 3 months' notice. After 1 month's notice, you take 2 weeks' leave. If you return to work on your notice, it will be 3 months and 2 weeks. If you return to work on your notice, you must avoid working too long on the day you are dismissed.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract, during the notice

question: I live with my partner and or my children. My ex-partner is still living with me, what should I do?
answer: You can evict your partner from your home. You have to go to the municipality to declare that he no longer lives with you. Some municipalities sometimes ask that the partner come in person to declare his change of address. If this is not possible, report it. A reporting agent then checks your statement. He conducts his investigation. He checks that your former partner no longer lives with you and looks for his new address.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: I think my apartment is unhealthy. I'm going to the police because I'm the victim of a sleep merchant. What are the sanctions for the owner of a sleep merchant in Wallonia?
answer: On the other hand, the building can be requisitioned and put at the disposal of the CPAS, in order to be restored or rented temporarily. Moreover, if the victim cannot relocate, the competent authorities can provide him with housing, the costs of which will have to be borne by the sleeping merchant. This assumes that initial prosecutions have been filed. If the owners are acquitted, the costs are borne by the competent CPAS or by the State. Unfortunately, these sanctions are not implemented.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: I'm a parent. Can I ask for an increase in the child support if my daughter starts higher education?
answer: Yes, the amount of the alimony contribution is never final. To determine the amount of the alimony contribution, account is taken of: the financial capabilities of the parents and; the age and type of education of the child. As soon as circumstances change, the alimony contribution can be adjusted. For example, a parent who loses his job or a child who starts higher education are reasons to review the amount of the alimony contribution. In agreement with the other parent, you can adjust the amount of the alimony contribution to match the new situation.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: My cottage serves as my home, does that mean I have a main residence lease in Wallonia?
answer: No, not necessarily. The fact that you are domiciled at the address of your cottage or that you live there full-time does not mean that your lease automatically becomes a main residence lease. However, this is an element that can influence the judge who has to decide your rental conflict. If the cottage lease is a student lease, you need an express agreement from the landlord so that the lease is also a main residence lease.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), What to know about the student cot lease

question: Is my employer obliged to take out occupational accident insurance?
answer: Before going any further, the rules for occupational accidents are very complex. This sheet does not explain them all in detail. This sheet only applies to workers in the private sector (not the public sector). For more information on my occupational accidents, you can contact the following services: The prevention advisor of your company's internal and external prevention and protection service.
topics: Work, salaried work, illness and accident, accident at work

question: After how long is my hospital bill prescribed?
answer: For the payment of health care costs (including ambulance transport costs), the statute of limitations is very short: 2 years from the end of the month in which the health care was given. After 2 years, the health care creditor can no longer demand payment. It is said that the prescription is liberating, that is, you are released from payment by the mere effect of the passage of time. But beware, the prescription can be suspended or interrupted.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to health care

question: Am I entitled to legal aid, do I have to pay court fees?
answer: If you are entitled to partially free legal aid, you are generally entitled to partial legal aid. This means that you have to pay a certain percentage of the legal fees, which can represent a large amount of money. Legal aid allows you to pay for: legal proceedings; or extra-judicial proceedings, such as mediation. For the costs covered by legal aid, see the question "Can I get partially free legal aid?"
topics: Justice, In court, Expenses related to the functioning of justice

question: I'm separating. Can I apply to the family court for custody of the children before the divorce is pronounced?
answer: Yes. You can ask the family judge to make all the decisions related to the children. The judge can, for example, take measures regarding: The accommodation of the children: for example, alternate accommodation or main accommodation with one of you, and the frequency of relations with the other parent. You can always propose to the judge a housing solution. The parental authority of the children: for example, if you want to make decisions about your children alone, or certain decisions because you feel that your spouse cannot take them.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, judicial separation - urgent and provisional measures

question: I worked a few weekends: can I make up for it?
answer: Yes, unless your normal working hours include Saturdays and Sundays. In principle, you can't work more than 38 hours a week (but there are exceptions in some sectors of activity). If these hours are divided into 6 days a week, it's normal that you work on weekends. 38 hours can't be recovered. They are part of your normal working hours. On the other hand, if your normal working hours are divided into 5 days, from Monday to Friday, you no longer have to work on Saturdays or Sundays.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, recovery

question: The parents are not married together. The child's father is married to someone other than the mother. Can I claim financial support for the child if I am the biological but not the legal father?
answer: Yes. The mother of the child, or the child itself, can claim financial aid from the man "who had relations with the mother during the legal period of conception". So this is the alleged biological father. This financial aid must be used for the maintenance, education and training of the child. This aid can be claimed: by the mother of the child if he is a minor; or by the child himself if he is an adult. It can be claimed at any time, as long as the child has not completed his training.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Establish a parent-child bond, Establish the father-child bond

question: Can the justice of the peace refuse the choice of guardian made by the parent?
answer: Yes. The justice of the peace verifies that the choice of the parents is in the best interest of the child: if the judge believes that the person chosen by the parents will act in the best interest of the child, he approves their choice: he officially appoints this person as guardian. If the judge believes that the choice of the parents is not in the best interest of the child, he appoints another person as guardian. To make his decision, the justice of the peace hears the minor child if he is over 12 years old.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, guardianship, appointment of the guardian

question: The situation has changed since the judgment, how do I get a new decision from the family judge?
answer: If circumstances change, you can always go back to the family judge for a new decision. For all the following matters, you can send a simple letter to the clerk to reopen the case: separate residences; parental authority and accommodation for minor children; maintenance obligations (contributions or alimony); provisional measures. You must explain why the new circumstance changes your situation or that of the child. For example, a loss of employment, a child starting higher education, a move, etc.
topics: Family, Resolving a family dispute, Appeal to the family judge

question: What appeal against a decision of the legal aid office?
answer: You can appeal to the labour court of the district of the legal aid office (BAJ) that made the decision. You can challenge the decisions: refusal of legal aid; withdrawal of legal aid. You have one month from the notification of the decision. For this appeal procedure, you are entitled to a free lawyer. This appeal is directed against the Bar Association. The labour court makes a judgment. It is sometimes important to exercise this appeal.
topics: Justice, the lawyer, legal aid (ex pro Deo), challenging a decision of the legal aid office

question: I live with my family in Belgium. I have a child in Belgium. I am a foreigner. Am I entitled to a residence permit if my child was born in Belgium?
answer: If your child is a minor and European, you must demonstrate that: you care for the child and provide for his or her maintenance; family reunification is in the child's interest. For more information, see our sheets What are the conditions for family reunification with a Belgian? and What are the conditions for family reunification with a European citizen? Your child is non-European and has a residence permit.
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and family

question: I think my dwelling is unhealthy. I am addressing the mayor. What is the mission of the mayor in matters of unhealthy in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. 1) If the municipality is not competent to establish a health investigation (Walloon Region rules): See the section "I am addressing the Walloon Region where to the competent municipality in order to have a health and overcrowding investigation" To know the list of competent municipalities, see the website of the Walloon Region. 2) If the municipality is not competent to establish a health investigation The action of the municipality is limited to housing problems related to hygiene, safety (for example, a building in ruins) or public health in general.
topics: Housing, unhealthy in Wallonia

question: How does the doctor assess my disability for the allowance for elderly care in Wallonia?
answer: Your disability is assessed by a doctor in your municipality. He evaluates your disability based on several factors. The Autonomy Reduction Evaluation Form that you completed when you submitted your application for the elderly care allowance (APA) is 12 points. The medical examination is 12 points. The doctor in your municipality can call you for a medical examination. He asks you questions during the medical examination. It is important to prepare well for the medical examination.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Wallonia

question: Am I entitled to a disability allowance if I have income?
answer: Yes. You are entitled to a disability allowance even if you have an income. You are entitled to the amount when your income does not decrease by a certain amount. If your income exceeds this amount, it is deducted from the maximum amount. For example, unemployment benefits, family benefits, etc. Having an income does not make you lose your right to a disability allowance.
topics: Social protection, disability, various allowances for disabled people

question: Do I have to notify the APA service of my mutual if my financial or family situation changes in Wallonia?
answer: You must notify the APA service of your nationality of any changes that may change the amount of your elderly care allowance (APA). For example: your income increases; your income decreases; the nature of your income changes: you received income from work and now you receive replacement income; a new person comes to live with you; etc. Depending on the change, you must notify the APA service of your nationality in different ways. Your income or that of your spouse decreases. You must notify the APA service of a new application; the Walloon social security platform (via the Walloon social security platform). You must notify the APA of your income or that of your spouse.
topics: Social protection, disability, allowance for the elderly (APA) in Wallonia

question: My ex is threatening to ask the judge for a money order. What is it?
answer: The delegation of sums is a procedure that allows third parties who owe you a sum of money (your employer, your unemployment fund, your tenant, etc.) to pay directly to your ex the alimony contribution that you have to pay him. Do not confuse the delegation of sums with the seizure of wages. For the delegation of sums, the bailiff does not intervene. The delegation of sums must be provided in a judgment.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to food contributions

question: I'm a landlord. Can my insurance cover my tenants in Wallonia?
answer: Yes For leases concluded before September 1, 2018, you could conclude with your tenant that: The tenant had to take out fire insurance at his own expense to cover his liability or; You inserted in your insurance contract and in the lease a third-party waiver clause. For leases concluded after September 1, 2018, the tenants have to take out fire insurance for the housing they rent. However, there is an exception: if you and your tenant agree otherwise in the lease, you can agree to waive a claim against your tenant in the event of a fire in the rented housing.
topics: Money, Insurance, Fire insurance, Renting a house, In Wallonia

question: I'm renting an apartment or a house. Do I have to indicate the amount of the rent in the ad in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, in principle, any landlord who rents a property used for housing is required to include in all public or official communications (newspapers, website, posters, etc.) the following: the amount of the rent excluding charges; the nature of the private and common charges (for a fee or provisions); the amount of any proposed common charges; the amount of the private charges if they are flat-rate; certain indicators of the energy performance certificate such as: the unique code (certificate PEB identification number); the total energy consumption; primary energy consumption in kWh per year; the specific consumption of primary energy, in kWh per m2 per m2 and the energy label or the (see also the energy label)
topics: Housing, Renting in Wallonia, Renting a house (Wallonia)

question: I am an employee (or unemployed). How many weeks of maternity leave can I take after giving birth (postnatal leave)?
answer: For self-employed women, see this sheet "What are the conditions under which I am entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work. For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.
topics: Social protection, pregnancy and birth, maternity leave

question: Who can be heard by the justice of the peace during the proceedings for the administration of property and or the person?
answer: After the application for the administration of property and or of the person to be protected has been lodged, the justice of the peace shall always summon: the person to be protected; the person who has lodged the application (the applicant). He shall also summon, if they live with the person to be protected: the father and mother of the person to be protected; the legal cohabiting spouse or companion of the person to be protected; the adult children of the person to be protected.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)

question: As a parent, how can I protect myself from the damage caused by my minor child?
answer: You can insure yourself by taking out private life liability insurance, more commonly known as family liability insurance. This insurance is essential, even if it is not compulsory. Private life insurance costs about EUR 70 per year. If your child does something stupid and causes damage, your insurance pays compensation to the victim. For each insured damage, it covers the damage caused to the victims up to a maximum amount.
topics: Family, Relationship between parents and children, Responsibility

question: I didn't sign a lease. What are my rights in Wallonia?
answer: Even if you haven't signed a lease, you have rights. To have rights as a tenant, you have to prove that you're renting the property, that is, you have to prove that you're renting the property, and you have to prove that you're renting the property. You have to prove that you're renting the property. You have to prove that you're renting the property. You have to prove that you're renting the property.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: How to end the measure of administration of property and or of the person?
answer: The justice of the peace may terminate the measure of administration of property and or of the person at any time. The measure of administration of property and or of the person is never taken definitively. The justice of the peace may terminate it: either on his own initiative, if he finds, for example, during an interview, that the measure is no longer justified; or at the request of: the protected person; his trusted person; his administrator of property and connection of the person; the King's Prosecutor; any interested person.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and/or the person (judicial protection), Amendment or termination of the measure for the administration of property and/or the person

question: Can I apply for part-time or full-time?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can ask for it, but your employer can refuse it. So part-time work is not a right, unless this possibility is provided for in an agreement. If your employer refuses, know that there are several possibilities for part-time work: parental leave, part-time credit, etc. For more information, see the Congregation for Parental and Parental Rights.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, Requiring part-time work and/or accumulating several part-time jobs

question: As legal cohabitators, what action can the family judge take on our property and debts?
answer: If you need the car for your professional activities, for example, you can ask the judge to allow you to use it during the separation. Be careful, you may have to pay a sum of money to your cohabitant for this use (as a kind of rent), the day you will definitely share these goods The repayment of common debts: the mortgage of your house, the credit for the regularization of housing (even if the credit is in the name of only one of the cohabitant), energy, etc.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation, separation before the judge

question: Our landlord doesn't do the repairs, what do we do in Brussels?
answer: If the landlord and your tenants cannot take the initiative to act on behalf of the landlord if you want him to pay the bill for the repairs, your landlord may refuse to pay the bill, assuming that: - the repairs were not necessary; - or that they could have been done at a lower cost.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Co-location (Brussels), Repairs, maintenance and work

question: I live somewhere other than my home. What happens if I don't live in my home?
answer: So if the municipality notices that you have left your home without warning or that you have settled on its territory without being domiciled there, it can regularize your situation itself: your old municipality can remove you from office; your new municipality can remove you from office. The municipality does not necessarily have to wait to remove you from office. It can do so immediately if it is certain that you no longer live there (for example, when a new family occupies the housing or when the municipality occupies a new one). Its mission is to keep its population registers up to date.
topics: Housing, domicile, domiciliation and radiation

question: I'm unemployed and I'm living in a roommate. I'm living in a roommate with friends, how much unemployment benefit will I get?
answer: Before going any further, only former wage earners are entitled to unemployment benefits. Self-employed workers are not entitled to unemployment benefits, unless they were employed before becoming self-employed, under certain conditions. Both civil servants have specific rules.
topics: Social protection, unemployment, unemployment, family and cohabitation

question: In what cases can the CPAS ask me to appeal to my maintenance debtors for social assistance?
answer: Only if you ask for financial aid. For all other types of aid, the CPAS cannot require it. Referral to an alimony debtor is optional: the CPAS is not obliged to do so. But in practice, the CPAS do this very often. Referral to alimony debtors is almost automatic when it comes to young people leaving the family home.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid, exercising your right to a maintenance pension

Family, maintenance obligations, maintenance obligations (family solidarity), with a request for intervention from the CPAS, recourse to maintenance debtors at the time of granting the aid (a priori recourse)

question: Who can receive family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: Family allowances are paid to the mother of the child, in principle. But there are special cases. If you have adopted the child, family allowances are paid: to the adoptive mother if the adoptive parents are of different sex; to the eldest adoptive parent if the adoptive parents are of the same sex. If the mother does not raise the child, the person who raises the child receives the family allowances. If the parents are separated, nothing changes in principle, except in special cases.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Family allowances in Wallonia, Child born from 1 January 2020 (Wallonia), Applying for and receiving family allowances (Wallonia)

question: I've been summoned to court, I've appealed the verdict, what happens while we wait for the new verdict?
answer: In some cases, your opponent can already force you to enforce the judgment (forced enforcement) even if you have appealed. This is called provisional enforcement (or enforcement by provision). The judgment specifies whether it is enforceable provisionally or not. But if the judgment does not specify anything: it is enforceable provisionally if both parties were present at the proceedings (adversarial proceedings); it is not enforceable provisionally if you were not present at the proceedings (default proceedings).
topics: Justice, In court, In civil, The court's decision

question: I receive confidences as a professional. As a civil servant, do I have an obligation to report?
answer: Yes, but professional secrecy takes precedence. There is a legal obligation for civil servants to report crimes and misdemeanors of which they are aware in the performance of their duties. This obligation is often invoked in the fight against social fraud. But non-compliance with this obligation is not punishable by criminal penalties.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: I'm staying at a hostel, can my partner kick me out of our apartment if I haven't signed the lease?
answer: This file was updated more than a year ago. It depends. So you have no rights to the landlord and vice versa. You also have no rights to the tenant (the one who signed the lease and pays the rent). You do not benefit from the "protection of the family housing". Since you have not signed anything, you have no rights to the housing, even if you are domiciled there or have children together. So you have no rights to the landlord (the one who signed the lease and pays the rent).

question: My child is disabled and receives a supplementary family allowance, until when is he entitled to Brussels?
answer: If your child is between the ages of 18 and 21, you are entitled to this supplement until August 31 of the year in which your child turns 18 without any conditions. • If your child is between the ages of 18 and 21, you are entitled to this supplement until August 31 of the year in which your child turns 18 without any conditions. • If your child is over the age of 18, you are entitled to this supplement. • If your child is over the age of 18, you are entitled to this supplement. • If your child is over the age of 18, you are entitled to this supplement. • If your child is over the age of 18, you are entitled to this supplement.

question: Is an invoice reminder always free?
answer: In principle no, the reminder of payment for an unpaid invoice is not free. The company can charge you a paid reminder for your late payment. The reminder fee compensates for the damage caused to the company. The reminder fee cannot be abusive. Example an unpaid invoice of 25 EUR. The company claims a reminder fee of 25 EUR. You can consider it an exaggerated reminder fee. You can immediately ask the company for a reminder not to pay the fee.

question: Can my child born in Belgium receive Belgian citizenship if I am not Belgian?
answer: Yes, but only in one case and under certain conditions. Your child (biological or adoptive) automatically receives Belgian nationality in the following situation: your child was born in Belgium; you (or the other parent) were born in Belgium for at least 5 of the 10 years preceding the birth (or adoption) of your child. In this case, you (or the other parent) have your <unk> main residence <unk> in Belgium. If you do not meet all these conditions, your child does not automatically receive Belgian nationality.
topics: Foreigners, Belgian nationality, minors (under 18 years of age)

question: I have a large family, am I entitled to a supplementary family allowance in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, if your income is less than a certain amount. Monthly amounts of the large family supplement (Wallonia) indexed as of December 1, 2022 You can prove this by: the composition of your household; (children domiciled at the same address) another document proving that the children live together, even if they are not registered at the same address. question: Can the CPAS help me create a rental guarantee in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. The mission of the Public Social Action Centre (CPAS) is to help people who are unable to create a rental guarantee (right to housing). The social worker assesses whether you are entitled to help from the CPAS to create this rental guarantee. He checks different criteria by carrying out a social survey. The help from the CPAS for a guarantee is often 'refundable'. It will therefore have to be reimbursed by monthly payments.

question: Do I have to pay anything if I am entitled to legal aid (ex pro Deo)?
answer: This depends on the legal aid to which you are entitled: partially free legal aid, the lawyer asks you for a contribution of EUR 25 to EUR 125 (depending on your income). totally free legal aid, you do not have to pay anything. Please note that if you received legal aid between 1 September 2016 and 21 June 2018, you had to pay a flat-rate contribution to the lawyer, even if you had totally free legal aid.

question: The parents are not married together. What steps should I take to be recognized as the father of my child if the mother divorced less than 300 days before the birth?
answer: You must have the ex-husband's paternity annulled, and then recognize the child. If the mother has divorced the ex-husband less than 300 days before the birth, the ex-husband of the child's mother is presumed to be her father. Indeed, the conception of the child is presumed to have taken place during the marriage. The presumption of paternity of the ex-husband applies as if the child was born during the marriage.

question: When can the justice of the peace terminate the lease in Brussels?
answer: You can ask the justice of the peace to terminate the lease, at any time, if the landlord does not comply with his obligations. The termination of the lease by the justice of the peace is called the "resolution" of the lease. To obtain it, you need the landlord not to comply with one of his main obligations. For example: if he does not do the heavy work or does not let you enjoy the housing peacefully (for example, by keeping a key and entering the premises without permission).

question: Can I be accompanied at a medical appointment?
answer: Yes, you can be accompanied by a trusted person of your choice at a medical or paramedical appointment. This allows the carer to inform you and the person accompanying you of the situation. If you are having difficulty retaining information because of your health condition or stress, this accompaniment can be very useful.

question: What is the duration of my notice of resignation?
answer: The new notice rules apply from 1 January 2014. These rules are the same for workers and employees (except for workers in certain sectors, such as the construction sector). The duration of your notice is calculated in weeks, depending on your seniority in the same company. The table below lists the new notice periods. The notice period runs from the 1st Monday following the notification of your resignation. This notification takes place; The employer takes advantage of the 3rd working day if you notify your resignation by mail (for example, Saturday or Saturday, we take into account all days of the week except Sundays and holidays).

question: What is a food obligation?
answer: It is a mechanism of family solidarity provided for in law. The obligation obliges us to provide financial or material assistance to a person who is attached to us by a family bond and who is in need. We speak of alimony but the aid can cover more than just the simple fact of being able to feed himself. The costs of housing or health care also fall within this obligation. This obligation exists between different people. Depending on the type of obligation, the conditions of this aid vary.
topics: Family, alimony obligations

question: Where and how to submit the application for the administration of property and or the person?
answer: Since June 1, 2021, you must submit the application online, on the website of the Central Registry for the Protection of Persons. This site designates the competent justice of the peace, depending on the place where the person to be protected resides. The application must be made in the name of the organization, and not in your personal name. You must attach to the online application a medical certificate dated less than 15 days.

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. The perpetrator of domestic violence is Belgian or European, will I lose my residence permit?

I am separating myself; Can I be deprived of my EU residence permit? The Office for Foreigners (OFE) can check whether the conditions for family reunification are still fulfilled for a period of 5 years. This period of 5 years begins on the day when the judge learns that the EU residence permit has been revoked.

answer: Before going any further, the rules for work-related accidents are very complex. This sheet does not explain them all in detail. question: Can I go to the justice of the peace to settle a tenancy dispute in Wallonia?
answer: Yes, it is always possible to go to the justice of the peace for a tenancy dispute. But before filing a lawsuit, it is recommended to send a subpoena to the opposing party: to pay a certain amount of money, to finish a job, to have a tree cut down in a neighborhood dispute, etc. Of course, you must keep proof of this dispatch (receipt of the receipt and copy of the letter sent). If you have not received an answer, you must file a lawsuit by filing a lawsuit with the justice of the peace.

question: Can the owner ask me for all kinds of information in Wallonia?
answer: The landlord can only ask you for: your name and surname; a means of communication with you; your address; your date of birth or proof of your ability to enter into a housing contract; a household composition; your marital status if you are married or cohabiting legally; the amount of financial resources at your disposal; proof of payment of your last 3 rents.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: Can I be asked to reimburse the family allowances I received in Brussels?
answer: Yes, in some cases, if you have received family allowances to which you were not entitled. This is called the repayment of the lindu; pay what you received too much. The family allowance fund can recover what it paid you unfairly, i.e. what it paid you when you were not entitled to it. Except if the lindu is due to a mistake of the fund. If it cost you too much fraud because it was wrong, it can not ask you to repay. It can make a new decision to rectify the amount, and then pay the new amount (although high) from the following month.

question: When can the family court intervene for urgent and provisional measures?
answer: The family court intervenes if one or both spouses request it. To file a request for urgent and provisional measures before the family court, it is necessary that: one of the spouses seriously fails to fulfill his or her duties arising from the marriage (for example, one of the spouses is unfaithful, etc.); or the agreement between the spouses is seriously disturbed: this is what is most often invoked, on the basis of different facts (for example, you are already separated, or you constantly argue, etc.).

question: How is the administrator controlled?
answer: 1. At the beginning of his mission, the administrator of the person's property and or property must submit an annual management report to the justice of the peace. He must detail in this report everything that has been done during 1 year (number of meetings, place of residence and family context, measures taken, etc.). He must also present the accounting of the person questioned in this annual report. He must attach a photocopy of the account statements. The justice of the peace can thus control the movements and accounts.

question: What is the state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: As of September 2018, the condition of the premises must include the following minimum information. You can, however, agree with your landlord to put less information in your conditions of the premises. For the condition of the premises, the information provided is: the date of the condition of the premises; the identity and quality of the persons making the condition of the premises; the references of the lease: the coordinates of the landlord (s), the address of the dwelling, the date of the lease; a general overview of the condition of the dwelling, the materials, equipment, the general state of maintenance and the replacement of the rooms; the description of the dwelling, the state of the dwellings and the ownership of the premises; the date of the presentation of the premises; the references of the dwelling; the reference to the location of the dwelling; the reference to the location of the dwelling; the reference to the location of the dwelling; the reference to the dwelling; the date of the dwelling;

question: I'm getting a divorce. Can I get a divorce without my spouse's consent?
answer: Yes, you don't need your spouse's consent to ask for a divorce. You then have to ask for a divorce on the grounds of irreparable separation. If you prove that you have been separated for more than 1 year, it is automatically considered that the separation is irreparable. You do not have to prove anything else. If you have been separated for less than 1 year at the time of your appearance before the judge, he will set a 2nd hearing. To prove that the irreparable separation is irreparable, you can use all the old methods.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage

question: I am filing a complaint with a mediator. The company is located in Belgium. How does the consumer mediator deal with my complaint?
answer: When the Consumer Ombudsman (SMC) receives your complaint, it has 3 weeks to inform you if your complaint is admissible. If the complaint is admissible, the SMC has 90 days to deal with the complaint. First, it informs the company of your written complaint filed against the SMC. The SMC can give its opinion on the conflict.
topics: Money, Consumer protection, Resolving a dispute with a company, Consumer Ombudsman (SMC)

question: I'm getting a divorce. I'm getting a divorce because of irreparable separation. Can I ask for a divorce on my own on the basis of our de facto separation?
answer: Yes. If you have been separated in fact for more than 1 year: Each of you can apply for divorce alone to the competent family court (to find out which is the competent family court, see the scheme in the standard documents). Your separation is presumed to be "irremediable", you do not have to prove anything other than your separation for more than 1 year. If you have been separated for less than 1 year: You can already file your application with the family court. You will have to go back to the judge a second time, so the judge sets a date for a second hearing, at the earliest when the separation deadline is reached, or 1 year after the first hearing.
topics: Family, situation of couples, marriage, only one of us wants to file for divorce

question: I am summoned to court. What is a request?
answer: The petition is a written document, containing certain mandatory particulars, which the applicant submits to the registry to ask the judge to resolve his dispute. The mandatory particulars are: the applicant's contact information (name, surname, address, etc.); the applicant's national or business registration number; the defendant's details (name, surname, address, etc.); an explanation of the facts of the petition to the registry; the date on which the applicant must be summoned to the court; the date on which the petitioner must make an appointment in front of the court.

question: I am working under article 60, can I resign?
answer: Yes. You have the right to resign. You must comply with the same rules as for ordinary employment contracts. You must give prior notice: you must continue to work for a certain number of weeks or months before your contract actually expires. If you do not comply with the notice or its duration, you must pay compensation to your employer: the compensatory advance notice.

question: Can I go abroad and keep my integration allowance (AI)?
answer: Yes You can go abroad for a maximum of 90 days a year and continue to receive your integration allowance (AI) You can take these 90 days at a time or in several times You are not obliged to notify the Directorate General for Disabled Persons (DGPH) of your stay abroad. However, it is advisable to do so You must continue to receive your AI even if you are staying abroad for more than 90 days a year if you are staying: in a hospital or a care facility with a relative or ally who has to stay; You must temporarily live abroad to carry out a mission there or to carry out duties in the service of Belgium (e.g. military, diplomatic agent in an embassy, etc.)

question: I receive an income replacement allowance (ARR), can I apply for a reassessment of my disability?
answer: Yes. If your health condition worsens, you can ask for a reassessment of your disability to the Directorate-General for Disabled Persons (DGPH). You must submit a new application. You must submit your application to the DGPH website. You must log in to My Disability with your ID card and your ID card reader, Itsme, etc.

question: After how long does my consumer credit debt expire?
answer: For the consumer credit contract, there are two statute of limitations: 10 years for the payment of the principal; 5 years for the payment of interest and fees. However, the five-year statute of limitations applies to your entire monthly payment, if: it reimburses both the principal, the interest and the fees, without it being possible to identify with certainty, on the basis of the contractual elements, the interest and the capital. On the other hand, if your credit contract is terminated, the statute of limitations is 10 years.

question: My child is dropping out of school. Will I lose my family allowance in Brussels?
answer: It depends on the age of your child. Your child is under 18 years old. If your child drops out of school, he keeps his family allowance until August 31st of the year he turns 18. Family allowance is due unconditionally until August 31st of the year your child turns 18.

question: What are the appeals against an admissibility decision?
answer: With regard to the admissibility decision, you have requested an RCD and the judge has rejected your request? You are the only applicant who can appeal. The RCD request is a unilateral request, i.e. no one other than the applicant (the over-indebted debtor) is aware of it. question: Can I ask my family allowance fund not to recover the family allowances I received in excess in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. You have two possibilities to try not to repay the family allowances that you have received in excess, i.e. the Hindu. Ask your family allowance fund to give up the recovery of the Hindu, i.e. it does not ask you to repay. Your fund will take into account: your good faith; your household income. The fund can give up the recovery of the Hindu if you are in good faith and you are in a fraudulent interest case; the amount to be recovered is less than EUR 50 and the recovery of the Hindu is not possible; the steps to recover the Hindu will be too expensive compared to the recovery of the Hindu.

question: Can I ask the CPAS not to call on my maintenance debtors for the RIS?
answer: Yes. You can ask the CPAS not to force you to ask for help from your alimony debtors. During the social survey, the social worker examines the financial capabilities of your alimony debtors. He analyzes their income and their expenses. They must provide information about their income and also their role extracted from their last warning. If the alimony debtors do not respond to this survey, the CPAS social worker can consult the Social Security Carrier. This will obtain several useful information on the financial situation of the alimony debtors.

question: Until what age am I entitled to the RIS?
answer: There is no age limit to be entitled to social integration income (RIS). But from the age of 65, you may be entitled to the Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly) and/or a pension. You must apply for this pension or Grapa as a priority. The CPAS will grant you a RIS (or social assistance) only if the amount of your Grapa or pension is insufficient. For more information, see our section "Revenue for the elderly".

question: I want to leave my parents to settle on my own, will the CPAS help me (social assistance)?

question: Under what conditions am I entitled to the elderly care allowance in Brussels?
answer: To benefit from the elderly care allowance (APA), you must meet certain conditions. You must be domiciled in Brussels and you are actually and permanently in Belgium. This means that you must live in Belgium. You must meet this condition: at the time of application; for the entire period during which you receive the APA. There are exceptions to this condition. For more information, see our sheet "Can I go abroad and keep my elderly care allowance (APA) (Brussels)?". You must be over 65 years old. You must suffer from a reduction in autonomy of at least 7 points.

question: The parents are not married together. The child's father is married to someone other than the mother. What steps should I take to be recognized as the father of my child if I am married to someone else?
answer: You must recognize your child in order to establish your paternity. Specifically, you must make a declaration of recognition of your paternity to the municipality. If your child is a minor: if the child is between 0 and 12 years old, you need the consent of his mother; if he is between 12 and 18 years old, you need his consent and the consent of his mother.

question: How to apply for a tourist visa?
answer: You can also apply for a visa to come to Belgium for a maximum of 3 months by applying for a "short-stay visa", also known as a "tourist visa". Most foreigners need a visa to come to Belgium for a maximum of 3 months by applying for a "short-stay visa". Most foreigners need a visa to come to Belgium for a maximum of 3 months by applying for a "short-stay visa", also known as a "tourist visa". question: I'm filing a lawsuit. What if my opponent doesn't respect the court's decision?
answer: You can start by forcing your opponent to comply with the court decision. You can start by forcing your opponent to comply with the court decision (or the conciliation minutes), by registered mail. You inform him that if he does not comply within 8 or 15 days (or more), you will demand the enforcement of the decision. If, after this period, your opponent still does not comply with the decision, you can proceed with the enforcement of the decision. You must be able to apply for the enforcement of the decision.

question: Is a person placed under observation incapable of managing his property?
answer: No. The decision to put the person under observation does not affect the legal capacity of the protected person. The purpose of the measure is to treat a pathology. The protected person is always legally capable. For example, she can pay bills, get married, exercise parental authority over her minor children, sign a contract, etc. This is only if a measure of putting the person under observation and or his legal capacity is pronounced. It is always the justice of the peace who pronounces this measure.
topics: Privacy, measures to protect people, protection of the mentally ill

question: Who can apply for family allowances in Brussels?
answer: In order to be entitled to family allowances in the Brussels system, the child must: be domiciled in Brussels (i.e. not in Flanders, Wallonia or the German-speaking Community) and be Belgian or a legal resident foreigner. Most of the time, it is the parents who apply for them. But another person who is raising the child can apply for them. You can apply online, via the website of your family allowance fund (Famiris or a private fund).
topics: Social protection, family and birth allowances, family allowances in Brussels

question: Who is covered by the family RC insurance?
answer: Overall, it covers your civil liability in your entire private life (i.e. outside of your professional activity) to the exclusion of your marital liability (for example, for your lease contract). Without going into details, we can say that family insurance intervenes to compensate for damage caused: by your fault; by children who are part of your household; by your pets; or by your main residence.
topics: Money, insurance, family insurance.

question: I want to get a divorce by mutual consent. Can I ask my husband or wife for alimony?
answer: Yes, but you have to provide for it in your divorce agreements. Divorce by mutual consent means that you both agree to divorce and that you settle all the issues related to the end of your marriage in agreements. These agreements are called "pre-divorce agreements by mutual consent". In your agreements, you can provide for one of the two to pay alimony to the other.
topics: Family, alimony, alimony (between spouses or ex-spouses), alimony in divorce proceedings, divorce by mutual consent

question: I'm filing a lawsuit. What is a petition?
answer: The petition is a written document, containing certain mandatory particulars, which the applicant submits to the registry to ask the judge to resolve his dispute. The mandatory particulars are: the applicant's contact information (name, surname, address, etc.); the applicant's national or business registration number; the defendant's details (name, surname, address, etc.); an explanation of the facts of the petition to the registry; the date on which the applicant must be summoned to the court; the date on which the petitioner must make an appointment in front of the court.

question: What can the patient's rights representative do?
answer: The representative may exercise the rights of the patient of the person he represents: from the moment when he is unable to do so himself; as long as he is unable to do so. For example, he may accept or refuse an intervention or treatment proposed by the caregiver. He decides on behalf of the person he represents. He must exercise the rights of the patient as the person himself would have done if he were still able. He cannot go against an advance declaration of will of the patient regarding a determined intervention.
topics: Privacy, Patient's rights, the rights of the incapacitated adult patient

question: Before which judge can my creditor have me sentenced to pay my debt?
answer: Either the justice of the peace or the court of first instance. In principle, the justice of the peace is competent for all claims for payment of a maximum amount of EUR 5.000. As soon as the amount exceeds EUR 5.000, it is the court of first instance that is competent. But beware, the justice of the peace is always competent regardless of the amount of the claim (it is said that it has exclusive jurisdiction), for certain matters, and in particular: disputes relating to the lease, i.e. disputes relating, for example, to the payment of rent arrears.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts

question: I'm a salaried worker. I'm giving birth later than expected. What are the consequences on my maternity leave?
answer: For self-employed women, see this sheet "Under what conditions am I entitled to maternity leave if I am self-employed?" For civil servants, the rules of maternity leave vary depending on the service or institution in which you work.
