question: I ⁇ m a landlord. My insurance doesn ⁇ t pay for all the repairs, is that normal?
answer: To know the terms of compensation of your insurance contract, read your contract. There are several limitations of compensation. The deductible Some contracts provide that in the event of an accident, part of the amount owed is covered by the insured. This amount is called EUR EUR. The insurer will only pay the costs in excess of this amount. You have a deductible of EUR 500 and the value of the kitchen that burned was EUR 10,000, so your insurer will pay you EUR 300,000 The deductible is proportional to the amount insured.
topics: Money, insurance, fire insurance, protecting your home

question: Can I refuse a job under section 60?
answer: If you refuse a job under Article 60, the CPAS may consider that you are not willing to work. This is one of the conditions for receiving social integration income (RIS). The CPAS may therefore consider that you do not meet the conditions for receiving RIS. When offering you a job, the CPAS must take into account: your physical and psychological state; your education; your family situation; your living conditions.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid, work under Article 60

question: How much is the birth allowance in Brussels?
answer: If your child is born after 1 January 2020, you receive: EUR 1.288,87 for your first child; EUR 585,85 for subsequent children. If it is your first child, you receive the amount for the first child, even if the other parent already has children from another father or mother. If you have adopted a child, and if you have a first child, you are entitled to the amount for the first child of twins or triplets.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowance, Birth allowance Child born before 1 January 2020, Child born in Brussels

question: How should the employer plan for a suitable job or for another job?
answer: Before going any further Since 28 November 2022, the procedure for terminating the employment contract for medical force majeure has been amended. The employer must draw up a reintegration plan in consultation with: the worker; the occupational doctor; any persons who can help the plan to succeed. The employer must draw up a detailed and as concrete as possible plan. The employer must draw up the plan and submit it to the worker and the worker within 6 months of receiving the incapacity assessment.
topics: Work, salaried work, termination of the employment contract due to medical force majeure

question: Accommodation for friends: can the landlord forbid me to do it in Wallonia?
answer: If the lease agreement limits the accommodation of friends: The lease agreement may provide for restrictions on the accommodation of friends. For example, when the landlord lives in the same building as the tenant, these restrictions cannot be excessive. For example, a total ban on rent-free accommodation would be excessive, as it would prevent you from having a free private, emotional or family life. In case of dispute, it is up to the justice of the peace to assess whether the clause is excessive or not.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Writing and signing

question: My friend lives in my house. Can we seize my furniture?
answer: Yes. If your friend is domiciled in your home, the bailiff can come and seize half of the seizable furniture (e.g. sofa, television, etc.) that is there. These are presumed to belong half to the debtor. But in order for your furniture not to be seized, you must prove that they belong to you. You can present to the bailiff, for example: an invoice written in your name; a will; bank statements; an inventory made by a notary; These evidence are not always sufficient but they can be questioned.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, seizure of movable property

question: After how long does my mortgage debt expire?
answer: For mortgage credit, there are two statute of limitations. Your debt relating to the payment of the principal is prescribed after 10 years. Your debt relating to interest and fees is prescribed after 5 years. Please note that the statute of limitations is also 5 years for your entire monthly payment: if your monthly payment reimburses both the principal, interest and fees, and if we cannot identify with certainty (based on the elements of the mortgage credit) the interest and capital.
topics: Money, debts, debts related to a mortgage loan

question: I just got a social housing, how can I terminate my current lease in Wallonia?
answer: When you get a social housing, you have to react within 7 calendar days to accept the proposed housing and sign the lease with the SLSP. The new lease sometimes starts very quickly. Or to get a social housing does not end your current lease. You risk finding yourself a tenant of 2 housing for a few months. This means that you have to meet your obligations in the 2 housing: payment of rent and maintenance of the housing. In this case, you will also have to pay "a severance allowance from the Walloon Region".
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Social housing (Wallonia), Allocation of social housing

question: What should my cohabitation agreement in Wallonia contain?
answer: Your roommate agreement must provide at least: the rent paid by each roommate (unless it is provided for in the roommate lease contract); the list of furniture and who they belong to; the charges paid by each roommate; the organization for energy, water, internet, etc. For the organization for housing insurance contracts; the rules for the arrival, departure and replacement of a roommate; who creates, pays and releases the rental guarantee; the rules for ending a conflict between roommates.
topics: Accommodation, Location in Wallonia, Location (Wallonia)

question: Under what conditions does the doctor-consultant of the mutual allow the resumption of work?
answer: The consultant doctor authorises the resumption of work only if two conditions are met: the adapted work is compatible with your state of health; and you remain disabled for at least 50% This 50% disability is assessed solely on the basis of a medical criterion. This is not an economic criterion (earning capacity) nor a working time criterion (this does not necessarily correspond to a half-time resumption of work).
topics: Work, Illness - incapacity for work, Resumption of adapted work during incapacity, Resumption of partial work with the authorization of the mutual (<unk> half-time medical <unk>)

question: In what cases can the CPAS ask me to refund the RIS I received?
answer: The CPAS can review your situation and ask you to repay the social integration income (RIS) you received in the event of: circumstances that may affect your rights (e.g. you were isolated and you become a cohabitant, or you receive certain resources); change in the right by a legal or regulatory provision; legal or material error committed by the CPAS (e.g. a misinterpretation of the law or a calculation error); omission or incomplete and inaccurate statements on your part You must make a full declaration of any new elements (e.g. you must have an impact on your right to the CPAS, or on the amount of your RIS).
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), if the RIS has to be reimbursed

question: Am I entitled to benefits from the mutual when I get out of prison (employee)?
answer: Yes, if you meet the conditions. After detention, you must not do an internship (internship exemption) if: you become a holder again within 30 days of pre-trial detention or deprivation of liberty. You are a holder if you pay the mutual contributions yourself; and you met the conditions to be a holder at the beginning of pre-trial detention or deprivation of liberty. For more information, see our section Incapacity to work.
topics: Social protection, Detainees and ex-detainees, Mutual

question: Can I sign several fixed-term contracts in a row with the same employer?
answer: In principle, no. To protect the worker, the succession of fixed-term contracts (CDD) is prohibited. But some exceptions are possible. You can sign several CDDs with the employer only in 4 cases. If the interruption periods are attributable to the worker. For example, for personal convenience, such as a trip. If the employer justifies the succession of CDDs by the nature of the work: For example, a concert, or a subsidized scientific research contract, or even in the field of entertainment; or other legitimate reasons. For example, the unfavorable economic circumstances of the company, or the worker's request to allow him to find a new job.
topics: Work, salaried work, employment contract

question: I am Belgian. Can I bring my family to Belgium?
answer: If you are a Belgian citizen, you can apply for EU family reunification from the age of 18, if you are a Belgian citizen, you can apply for EU family reunification from the age of 18, if you are a Belgian citizen, you can apply for EU family reunification from the age of 18, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a citizen, if you are a Belgian citizen, if you are a citizen.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: Can I stay under my parents' mutual if I move into a roommate?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only some general information. For more information, contact your mutual. Be aware of two things: The rules are not exactly the same for employees, self-employed workers and civil servants. The notion of a dependent person is not the same in the different social security schemes. For example, a dependent person in the sickness and disability insurance scheme is not necessarily a person to benefit from the disability allowance scheme, nor in the unemployment benefit scheme. You can also stay with your parents as a dependent person, even if you no longer live with them.
topics: Social protection, the consequences of cohabitation on social benefits
Social protection, Health and illness, Reimbursement of health care

question: I'm separating. Can my partner kick me out if I'm living in our apartment?
answer: Yes, but only if: you have not signed the lease; and your partner has a decision of the justice of the peace that authorizes him to evict you. In a free union (cohabitation), there is no protection of the family housing (unlike married couples and legal cohabitators). You are not protected in case of separation. If you have signed the lease with your partner at 2, and if you comply with your obligations as a tenant (explained rent, charges, maintenance, etc.), your partner cannot evict you. The justice of the peace cannot intervene, because he is competent to settle a tenancy dispute for the separation of couples.
topics: Family, situation of couples, free union

question: How to submit an application to the Chamber of Friendly Settlement (CRA)?
answer: You have to file a petition with the family court clerk. Either you sign it at 2, or one of you signs it. This petition is free of charge. It's one of the great advantages of this procedure. You explain the points you want to address, and any agreements you've already reached. If there's a legal proceeding going on in the family court, you can at any time ask the judge to suspend the proceedings to try a conciliation before the conciliation chamber (CRA).
topics: Family, Resolving a family conflict, Finding an amicable solution, In the family court: the amicable settlement chamber

question: Can I be put under property administration if I mismanage my money?
answer: Yes. If the judge believes that you are in a state of extravagance, he can order a measure of protection. This measure can only be an assistance to manage your property (so not a representation, and not an assistance to the person). The law does not define the state of extravagance. We use the definition of common language. We target a person who spends his money excessively, endangering his finances. In this case, you need protection, not because of your state of health but only because of your state of extravagance.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or the person (judicial protection)

question: Can my landlord increase my rent during the lease in Wallonia?
answer: For example, if the end of a current three-year lease is December 31st, the rent can be increased between April 1st and June 30th. If the end of a current three-year lease is January 31st, the rent can be increased between May 1st and July 31st, and so on. If the lease is renewed at the end of the following month, the rent will be increased as soon as the lease expires. For example, if the lease is renewed at the end of December 31st, the rent can be increased between April 1st and June 30th. If the lease is renewed at the end of January 31st, the rent can be increased between May 1st and August 31st.
topics: Housing, Rent in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), Rent, charges and insurance, Rent, Rent revision

question: I'm a parent. What's the parent's alimony obligation to their child?
answer: Parents have an obligation to ensure the education, training, maintenance, accommodation, health and well-being of their children at the age of training (minor or adult). This arises from the fact that they are parents. As soon as the bond of filiation exists, the parent has this obligation towards his child. Parents cannot renounce it. This obligation must be done according to the financial capabilities of the parents.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: I'm an employee. Can I be fired because I'm pregnant?
answer: No, you can't be fired just because you're pregnant, but you can be fired while you're pregnant for any other reason. You're protected against being fired: from the time your employer is officially informed of your pregnancy until 1 month after postnatal maternity leave. Your employer can't make the decision to fire you during this period either; if you have a miscarriage, or a voluntary termination of pregnancy, the protection automatically ends. But your employer can't fire you just because of your pregnancy and your pregnancy.
topics: Work, Work and parenting, During pregnancy

question: I am filing a complaint with a mediator. The company is located in Belgium. In what cases can I file a complaint with the mediator on behalf of the consumer?
answer: The Consumer Mediation Service (SMC) is a free service designed to help consumers in their private disputes with a company.The SMC tries to reconcile points of view and proposes amicable solutions.The SMC cannot act if the consumer has not lodged a complaint in writing with the company's customer service or litigation service.The Consumer Mediation Service (SMC) can intervene for all conflicts between a consumer and a company established in Belgium.The SMC is competent for:
topics: Money, Consumer protection, Resolving a dispute with a company, Consumer Ombudsman (SMC)

question: Can I combine the Grapa with a retirement or survivor's pension?
answer: Under certain conditions, yes. You can accumulate the Grapa with a small retirement or survivor's pension, especially if your pension is insufficient to cover your needs. The rules for calculation and accumulation are very complex. For more information, see the website of the Federal Pension Service (SFP).
topics: Social protection, pensions and Grapa, Grapa (guaranteed income for the elderly)

question: I have submitted an application for medical regularization (9th). I have submitted an application 9th. Am I entitled to the CPAS aid?
answer: It depends on the stage of your procedure. The application for medical regularization takes place in two phases: an admissibility phase: the Aliens Office (OP) checks whether your file is complete (standard medical certificate, address of residence, etc.); a substantive phase: once your file is admissible, the OP checks the severity of your illness and whether the care is existing and available in your country of origin. During the examination of admissibility, you are in principle entitled only to the CPAS card, under certain conditions (for urgent social care, the existence of a guarantee, the existence of a medical insurance, etc.).
topics: Foreigners, foreigners and CPAS aids

question: In what cases do I have to sign a PIIS if I receive social assistance?
answer: Only if you apply for financial aid. For all other types of aid, the CPAS cannot ask you to sign an individualised social integration project (PIIS). This is an optional condition: the CPAS is not obliged to ask you to sign a PIIS. It can grant you financial aid without requiring you to sign a PIIS.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid, signing an "individualised social integration project" (PIIS)

question: Our landlord doesn't do the repairs, what to do in Wallonia?
answer: If you are a landlord and your tenants cannot take the initiative to act on behalf of the landlord if you want him to pay the bill for the repairs, your landlord may simply refuse to pay the bill, believing that: - the repairs were not flawed; - or that they could be less costly.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Colocation (Wallonia), Repairs, maintenance and work

question: Is the state of affairs mandatory in Brussels?
answer: This sheet was updated more than a year ago. Yes, the state of the premises is mandatory in any lease of a building (except for the lease). It is therefore also mandatory in the case of cohabitation. At the end of the lease, each roommate and the landlord can demand a state of the premises. The others cannot refuse. The state of the premises is also mandatory in the case of sublease. The state of the premises must be annexed to the lease.
topics: Accommodation, rent in Brussels, accommodation (Brussels), state of play

question: I confide in a professional. What are my remedies if a professional reveals the information I have entrusted to him?
answer: You can report the breach of confidentiality, as a victim of this criminal offence. Even if you have not suffered any damage, you can file a complaint with the police, in any police station. The King's Prosecutor decides whether or not to ask for a financial compensation. It is often better to maintain a dialogue with the professional. If this is not enough, you have several options. If you have suffered damage as a result of the dismissal, you can claim damages and interest to repair your damage.
topics: Private life, professional secrecy

question: Can I recognize a child after I have succeeded in having a relationship with his father annulled?
answer: Yes. If you are the biological father, you can ask the family court to annul the existing paternity bond. This is called the "paternity dispute action". If the judge agrees with you, you are automatically designated as the child's father. In the same decision, the judge annuls the paternity established so far and replaces it with your own.
topics: Family, Parent-child bond, Parent-child bond, Disputing a parent-child bond

question: Can I change the person I trust or end their mission?
answer: Attention, several laws use the term "trusted person" (e.g. the Patient Rights Act, the Euthanasia Act, the Mental Health Protection Act), but this term does not always have the same meaning. The mission of the trusted person is different depending on the law in which it intervenes. Here, we are talking about the trusted person in the context of the property administration measure and the oral protection of the person.
topics: Privacy, Measures for the protection of persons, Administration of property and or of the person (judicial protection), Trusted person

question: How to terminate the lease by mutual agreement in Wallonia?
answer: You can always terminate your lease by mutual agreement. Put the agreement in writing signed and dated by you and your landlord. Keep each of you a signed copy of this agreement. Thus, neither of you will be able to change your mind afterwards.
topics: Housing, Rental in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, End of the nine-year lease, By mutual agreement
Housing, Rental in Wallonia, Lease of main residence (Wallonia), End and break, End of short-term lease (less than or equal to 3 years), By mutual agreement

question: How many days of leave am I entitled to?
answer: The rules are not the same for public sector workers (employees) or for self-employed workers. There are also differences between employees and workers. In addition, this sheet explains the general rules laid down by law, but there are special rules for certain sectors of activity or for certain companies. Please consult your staff service, or the National Office of Annual Leave (ONVA). In principle, you are entitled to a maximum of 20 days of statutory leave per year, if you work 5 days a week. But the number of days of statutory leave you are entitled to during a year (called "conversion year") is proportional to the number of days you worked in the first month. For example, if you were born in 2022, you are entitled to 20 days of statutory leave.
topics: Work, salaried work, holidays and leave, annual leave

question: Will I get a lower pension if I work part-time?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. You can contact several services for more details: the Regional Directorate for the Control of Social Laws for the regulation of part-time work; the Social Inspectorate of the ONSS (National Office of Social Security) for the social security of part-time workers; your union or the CAPAC, and the ONEM for your right to unemployment as a part-time worker. Yes, the retirement pension is proportional to your career and therefore to your average salary over your entire career. If you do not work full-time, or if you work part-time, you contribute less and earn less.
topics: Work, salaried work, part-time work, My rights to social security as a part-time worker

question: Can the landlord increase my charges in Brussels?
answer: It depends on whether you pay your expenses in the form of a lump sum or a provision. To find out your type of expenses, look in your lease. If you pay a lump sum: The landlord can't claim an extra charge from you. Payment in the form of a lump sum is at the risk of everyone. You can't claim a refund if the lump sum exceeds your actual consumption. On the other hand, you (or your landlord) can ask the justice of the peace: Adjust the amount of the lump sum or convert it into real expenses.
topics: Housing, Rental in Brussels, Lease of main residence (Brussels), Rental, charges and insurance, Charges, Calculation and recovery of charges

question: Is the debt settlement plan final?
answer: This sheet concerns the collective settlement of debts (RCD). When the judge approves an amicable settlement plan accepted by all parties, it is final. It is not subject to appeal. On the other hand, when it is the judge who establishes a judicial settlement plan, his decision is subject to appeal within 30 days of the notification of the judgment. The plan is therefore not final. Moreover, if new facts occur during the plan (increased income, significant inheritance, major new charges, loss of employment, etc.), the plan can be modified or revised during the execution.
topics: Money, debts, solutions to combat debt, collective settlement of debts (RCD), pending proceedings

question: Am I entitled to disability benefits from the mutual insurance company if I have the means?
answer: Yes. You are entitled to disability benefits from the mutual society even if you have resources. Be careful, you must meet the conditions to be entitled to disability benefits. For more information, see our sheet <unk>What conditions do I have to meet to be entitled to disability benefits from the mutual society (employee or unemployed)?<unk> To be entitled to disability benefits you must: work for at least 12 months; 800 hours part-time.
topics: Social protection, health and illness, incapacity for work

question: How do I sublease my cat in Wallonia?
answer: This sheet has been updated more than a year ago. It must contain several pieces of information. For more information, see the question "Is an oral lease mandatory?" written in the ruble on the lease of the main residence. If the lease of the sublease is a lease of the main residence, the lease of the sublease has been written since June 15, 2007.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), Sub-location

question: How does the doctor assess my disability for the integration allowance (AI)?
answer: Your disability is assessed by a doctor from the General Directorate for Persons with Disabilities (DGPH). He evaluates your disability based on several criteria. The reduction in autonomy assessment form that you completed when you submitted your application for the IA allowance (point 17-18 points). The medical questionnaire completed by your doctor granting the medical examination can call you for a medical examination. The DGPH doctor can ask you questions during the medical examination. It is important to prepare well for the medical examination.
topics: Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: What should I do if there is no state of affairs in Wallonia?
answer: This presumption applies even if your contract contains the clause: "the building is in good condition". These clauses are not valid. This presumption is in your favor, as a tenant. If your landlord believes that there is damage, he can try to prove, by any means, that this condition was different at the entrance. He can, for example, present renovation work carried out before your entry into the premises, the exit of the premises with the previous tenant, invoices, photos, etc.
topics: Housing, Location in Wallonia, Kot (Wallonia), State of the place

question: Do I have to have the boiler maintained in my social housing in Wallonia?
answer: If you do not do the necessary maintenance, the SLSP can do it for you and then send you the invoice. Each tenant pays: maintenance, rental repairs, individual charges, and part of the collective charges (LSF). On the other hand, the SLSP itself organizes the individual maintenance of the boilers. The purpose of avoiding this is that some tenants of the boiler often forget that the invoice is paid by the tenant for their group.
topics: Housing, Housing in Wallonia, Social housing (Wallonia), Obligations and duties of the landlord and tenant, Charges, maintenance and expenses

question: Can I be asked to refund the income replacement allowance (ARR) I received?
answer: Yes, if you have received an income replacement allowance (ARR) to which you were not entitled. This is called the reimbursement of the income you did not declare, or you did not say that you lived with another person, etc. Then the 5 years are counted from the moment the Directorate General for Disabled Persons (DGPH) became aware of the fraud, the information is prescribed. This means that the DGPH can no longer recover.
topics: Social protection, disability, income replacement allowance (ARR)

question: Can I request a conciliation to settle a rental dispute in Brussels?
answer: Yes, you can ask for conciliation to settle your tenancy dispute. You can contact the justice of the peace by simple letter, specifying: your name, first name and address; the name, first name and address of the person you wish to invite to conciliation; the problem that opposes you and your request; your wish to attempt conciliation. The conciliation procedure is free of charge. Conciliation is a voluntary procedure.
topics: Housing, Renting in Brussels, Resolving a rental dispute (Brussels)

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. What if my complaint of domestic violence is dismissed?
answer: At the end of the information and in view of the elements of the file, the public prosecutor's office may decide to close the case without further action. This does not mean that the case is permanently closed; it can be reopened if new elements are communicated (e.g. new complaints, etc.). For example, the public prosecutor's office may decide to close the case without further action in the following cases: the search capacity is too low; the evidence is insufficient; the author is deceased.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: I am entitled to an integration allowance (AI). How much will I receive?
answer: The amount of your integration allowance (AI) depends on: your reduction in autonomy (EUR 12 points); the amount of your AI depends on your reduction in autonomy (EUR 12 points); the amount of your AI depends on your reduction in autonomy (EUR 18 points); the amount of your autonomy depends on your difficulties in: moving around; eating and preparing to eat; doing your toilet and dressing; maintaining your accommodation and doing household chores; assessing and avoiding dangers (living without supervision); talking and having contacts with other people.
topics: Social protection, disability, integration allowance (AI) and other benefits

question: I'm a parent. How old do I have to be to keep paying alimony?
answer: It is impossible to answer this question, because the law does not set a maximum age. The payment of the food contribution does not stop automatically at the age of 18. You must pay a food contribution until your child becomes independent. Autonomy is generally achieved when the young person has a diploma or has completed training that gives him access to the labour market. It is generally considered that: the young person with a qualifying secondary diploma can ask his parents to intervene for additional training; the payment of the food contribution is extended until the end of the vocational integration internship (the former "waiting stage").
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: Can I ask the CPAS not to call on my maintenance debtors for social assistance?
answer: The CPAS must check whether the use of your alimony debtors is: materially possible, and appropriate in view of your family situation. The CPAS can therefore waive the use of alimony debtors in two cases. If the use of your alimony debtors is materially impossible, for example because they do not have the necessary resources to pay alimony. During the social inquiry, the social worker examines the financial capabilities of your alimony debtors. He analyzes their income and their expenses. They must provide information on their income and usually also a copy of their last warning extracted from breaking family relations.
topics: Social protection, aid from the CPAS, social aid, exercising your right to a maintenance pension

Family, maintenance obligations, maintenance obligations (family solidarity), with a request for intervention from the CPAS, recourse to maintenance debtors at the time of granting the aid (a priori recourse)

question: What appeal can be made against a decision to refuse family reunification?
answer: You are entitled to free legal aid. If you are already residing in Belgium at the time of the refusal decision, the appeal is suspended. This means that you will not be deported until the CCE has made a decision, even if the group of foreigners (OE) has also made a decision to leave the territory. This is an appeal for annulment: the judge examines the legality of the decision of the CCE. If the CCE decides that the decision is illegal, it is annulled and must make a new decision.
topics: Foreigners, non-European stay, family reunification

question: I am a victim of domestic violence. As a victim of domestic violence, can I get a divorce if my husband refuses?
answer: Yes, you can file for divorce, even if your spouse disagrees with it or threatens to refuse to sign the papers. No one is obliged to remain married if they do not wish to. There are two divorce procedures: Divorce by mutual consent, when both spouses agree to divorce, and divorce by irrevocable separation. This procedure allows a third party, even if their spouse no longer agrees, to file for divorce.
topics: Family, situation of couples, domestic violence

question: What if I can't agree with the other parent on a decision about our minor child?
answer: If this is not possible or if it has failed, you can appeal to the family court (or to the judge of appeals in an emergency). For example, if the other party wants to enroll your child in a school that is not suitable for you, you can appeal to a parent or a mediator in the first place. If this does not work, you can ask the family judge to decide.
topics: Family, parent-child bond, parental authority

question: What is real estate seizure?
answer: The purpose of foreclosure is to sell a property (a house, an apartment, a piece of land, etc.) that belongs to the person who owes the money (the debtor) and to repay the person to whom the amount is owed (the creditor), thanks to the sale price.
topics: Money, debts, debt recovery procedures, judicial recovery of debts, the seizure of real estate

question: When and how to apply for the birth allowance in Brussels?
answer: You can apply for the birth allowance in advance, after 24 weeks of pregnancy. You then receive it 2 months before the expected date of birth. Be careful, if you received the birth allowance in November 2019 or December 2019, for a birth scheduled for 2020, but finally your child was born before 1 January 2020, you are entitled to the old amount of birth allowance. Your family allowance fund owes you the difference, because the old amount is higher. If you do not pay the birth allowance, you can apply for it at birth, or after birth, up to 3 years from the last day of the quarter in which the birth took place.
topics: Social protection, Family and birth allowances, Birth allowance, Birth allowance Child born before 1 January 2020, Child born in Brussels

question: I am lodging a complaint with a mediator. The company is located in Belgium. Under what conditions can I lodge a complaint with the consumer mediator?
answer: Before filing a complaint with the Consumer Ombudsman (SMC), you must contact the company concerned for: Obtain the information you wish; and try to resolve the conflict. You must contact the company in writing (mail, e-mail, fax). You must submit these written documents to the SMC. If you contact the company by telephone, make a note on paper: the date of the appeal; the approximate time of the appeal; and, if possible, the name of the employee of the SMC. You can submit this information to the SMC in the description of your complaint.
topics: Money, Consumer protection, Resolving a dispute with a company, Consumer Ombudsman (SMC)

question: For how long am I entitled to social integration income (RIS)?
answer: You are entitled to social integration income (RIS) as long as you meet the conditions for entitlement. From the moment you no longer meet the conditions, you are no longer entitled to RIS. The CPAS checks at least once a year whether you still meet the conditions of the RIS. It conducts a social survey. You must inform the CPAS of any changes in your family and financial situation. For example, you have a new job, you move in with your partner, etc.

question: I am a landlord. How are the fire insurance amounts calculated?

question: Do I have to house my child forever?
